    　　晋孝武帝母李太后本贱人，简文无子，曾遍令善相者相宫人，李太后给卑役不豫焉。相者指之：“此当生贵子，而有虎厄。”帝因幸之，生孝武帝、会稽王道子。既登尊位，服相者之见，而怪有虎厄，且生所未见，乃令人画作虎象。因以手抚，欲打虎戏，患手肿痛，遂以疾崩。
    　　晋太元初，苻坚遣将杨安侵襄阳，其一人于军中亡，有同乡人扶丧归。明日应到家，死者夜与妇梦云：“所送者非我尸，仓乐面下者是也。汝昔为吾作结发犹存，可解看便知。”迄明日，送丧者果至，妇语母如此，母不然之。妇自至南丰，细检他家尸，发如先，分明是其手迹。 [49]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [49]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 索元在历阳疾病 - 索元 likely a proper name, '在历阳疾病' means 'suffering from illness in Lixiang.'
    • 西界一年少女子姓某 - '西界' refers to the western region, '一年少女子姓某' means 'a young girl of a certain surname from the west.'
    • 自言为神所降 - '自言' means 'to claim,' '为神所降' means 'to say that she was sent by a deity.'
    • 许为治护 - '许' means 'to promise,' '为治护' means 'to treat and protect.'
    • 元性刚直 - '元' likely a proper name, '性刚直' means 'of a straightforward and upright nature.'
    • 以为妖惑 - '以为' means 'to consider,' '妖惑' means 'as an illusion or deception.'
    • 收以付狱 - '收' means 'to capture,' '付狱' means 'to deliver to prison.'
    • 戮之中于市中 - '戮之' means 'to execute,' '中于市中' means 'in the middle of the market.'
    • 女临死曰 - '临死' means 'on the verge of death,' '曰' means 'said.'
    • 却后十日，当令索元知其罪 - '却后十日' means 'ten days later,' '当令索元知其罪' means 'I will make Suxuan know his crime.'
    • 如期，元果亡 - '如期' means 'as expected,' '元果亡' means 'Suxuan indeed died.'
    • 晋孝武帝母李太后本贱人 - '晋孝武帝' refers to Emperor Xiaowu of Jin, '李太后' is his mother, '本贱人' means 'originally a lowly person.'
    • 简文无子 - '简文' refers to Emperor Jianwen of Jin, '无子' means 'no son.'
    • 曾遍令善相者相宫人 - '曾遍令' means 'once ordered,' '善相者' means 'skilled physiognomists,' '相宫人' means 'to examine the palace women.'
    • 李太后给卑役不豫焉 - '给卑役' means 'assigned to lowly service,' '不豫焉' means 'was not included.'
    • 相者指之 - '相者' means 'the physiognomist,' '指之' means 'pointed to her.'
    • 此当生贵子，而有虎厄 - '此当生贵子' means 'this one will give birth to a noble child,' '而有虎厄' means 'but will face a tiger calamity.'
    • 帝因幸之 - '帝' refers to the emperor, '因幸之' means 'therefore favored her.'
    • 生孝武帝、会稽王道子 - '孝武帝' and '会稽王道子' are the names of the emperor's son and his brother.
    • 既登尊位，服相者之见 - '既登尊位' means 'after ascending the throne,' '服相者之见' means 'believed the physiognomist's prediction.'
    • 而怪有虎厄 - '而怪' means 'found it strange,' '有虎厄' means 'about the tiger calamity.'
    • 且生所未见 - '且' means 'also,' '生所未见' means 'something he had never seen.'
    • 乃令人画作虎象 - '乃' means 'then,' '令人' means 'ordered someone,' '画作虎象' means 'to paint a tiger image.'
    • 因以手抚，欲打虎戏 - '因以手抚' means 'therefore touched it with his hand,' '欲打虎戏' means 'to play at hitting the tiger.'
    • 患手肿痛 - '患' means 'suffered,' '手肿痛' means 'swollen and painful hands.'
    • 遂以疾崩 - '遂' means 'therefore,' '以疾崩' means 'died of illness.'
    • 晋太元初 - '晋太元初' refers to the early years of the Taiyuan era of the Jin dynasty.
    • 苻坚遣将杨安侵襄阳 - '苻坚' is the name of the ruler of the Former Qin dynasty, '遣将杨安侵襄阳' means 'sent General Yang An to invade Xiangyang.'
    • 其一人于军中亡 - '其一人' means 'one of them,' '于军中亡' means 'died in the army.'
    • 有同乡人扶丧归 - '有同乡人' means 'a fellow townsman,' '扶丧归' means 'carried the coffin back home.'
    • 明日应到家 - '明日' means 'the next day,' '应到家' means 'should arrive home.'
    • 死者夜与妇梦云 - '死者' means 'the dead person,' '夜' means 'at night,' '与妇梦云' means 'told his wife in a dream.'
    • 所送者非我尸，仓乐面下者是也 - '所送者非我尸' means 'the one being carried is not my body,' '仓乐面下者是也' means 'the one with the face down in the coffin is.'
    • 汝昔为吾作结发犹存，可解看便知 - '汝昔为吾作结发' means 'you once tied my hair for me,' '犹存，可解看便知' means 'it still exists, you can untie it to see.'
    • 迄明日，送丧者果至 - '迄明日' means 'by the next day,' '送丧者果至' means 'the coffin-bearer indeed arrived.'
    • 妇语母如此 - '妇' means 'the wife,' '语母' means 'told her mother,' '如此' means 'like this.'
    • 母不然之 - '母' means 'the mother,' '不然之' means 'did not believe it.'
    • 妇自至南丰 - '妇' means 'the wife,' '自至' means 'went herself,' '南丰' is a place name.
    • 细检他家尸 - '细检' means 'carefully examined,' '他家尸' means 'the body of another family.'
    • 发如先，分明是其手迹 - '发如先' means 'as before,' '分明是其手迹' means 'clearly it is her own handiwork.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 索元 - Likely a proper name, possibly a historical figure.
    • 历阳 - A place name, likely a city or region in ancient China.
    • 晋孝武帝 - Emperor Xiaowu of Jin, a ruler of the Jin dynasty.
    • 李太后 - The mother of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin.
    • 简文 - Emperor Jianwen of Jin, the father of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin.
    • 苻坚 - The ruler of the Former Qin dynasty.
    • 杨安 - A general of the Former Qin dynasty.
    • 襄阳 - A city in ancient China.

    Suxuan, suffering from illness in Lixiang, was visited by a young woman of a certain surname from the western region. She claimed to have been sent by a deity to inform Suxuan and promised to treat and protect him. Suxuan, being of a straightforward and upright nature, considered her to be an illusion or deception. He captured her and delivered her to prison, where she was executed in the middle of the market. On her deathbed, the woman said, "Ten days later, I will make Suxuan know his crime." As expected, Suxuan indeed died ten days later.

    The mother of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin, Empress Li, was originally of lowly status. Emperor Jianwen of Jin had no son, so he once ordered skilled physiognomists to examine the palace women. Empress Li was assigned to lowly service and was not included in the examination. One of the physiognomists pointed to her and said, "This one will give birth to a noble child, but will face a tiger calamity." Emperor Jianwen therefore favored her, and she gave birth to Emperor Xiaowu of Jin and his brother, the Prince of Kuaiji, Daozi. After ascending the throne, Emperor Xiaowu believed the physiognomist's prediction but found it strange about the tiger calamity, as he had never seen a tiger. He then ordered someone to paint a tiger image and touched it with his hand, playing at hitting the tiger. His hands became swollen and painful, and he died of illness.

    In the early years of the Taiyuan era of the Jin dynasty, Fu Jian, ruler of the Former Qin, sent General Yang An to invade Xiangyang. One of his soldiers died in the army, and a fellow townsman carried the coffin back home. The next day, the coffin-bearer was expected to arrive home. The dead soldier told his wife in a dream, "The one being carried is not my body; the one with the face down in the coffin is. You once tied my hair for me, and it still exists; you can untie it to see." By the next day, the coffin-bearer indeed arrived. The wife told her mother about the dream, but the mother did not believe it. The wife went herself to Nanfeng and carefully examined the body of another family. The hair was as before, clearly her own handiwork. [49]

    北府索卢贞者，本中郎荀羡之吏也。以晋太元五年六月中病亡，经一宿而苏。云见羡之子粹，惊喜曰：“君算未尽，然官须得三将，故不得便尔相放。君若知有干捷如君者，当以相代。”卢贞即举龚颖，粹曰：“颖堪事否？”卢贞曰：“颖不复下已。”粹初令卢贞疏其名，缘书非鬼用，粹乃索笔自书之。卢贞遂得出。忽见一曾邻居者，死亡七八年矣，为太山门主，谓卢贞曰：“索都督独得归邪？”因嘱卢贞曰：“卿归，为谢我妇。我未死时，埋万五千钱于宅中大床下。我乃本欲与女市钏，不意奄，终不得言于女妻也。”卢贞许之。及苏，遂使人报其妻，已卖宅移居武进矣。固往语之，仍告买宅主，令掘之，果得钱如其数焉。即遣其妻与女市钏。寻而龚颖亦亡，时果共奇其事。 [50]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [50]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 為之一空 - Set phrase meaning completely emptied; fields were left barren.
    • 經一宿而蘇 - After one night, he revived.
    • 君算未盡 - Your fate is not yet complete.
    • 官須得三將 - The official needs three generals.
    • 干捷如君者 - As capable and agile as you.
    • 不復下已 - He is no longer inferior to me.
    • 緣書非鬼用 - Because the writing is not used by ghosts.
    • 索都督獨得歸邪 - Why does the General alone get to return?
    • 卿歸，為謝我婦 - When you return, please convey my greetings to my wife.
    • 埋萬五千錢於宅中大床下 - Buried 15,000 coins under the large bed in the house.
    • 本欲與女市鐲 - Originally wanted to buy bracelets for my daughter.
    • 不意奄 - Unaware of my sudden death.
    • 終不得言於女妻也 - Could not speak to my daughter or wife.
    • 遂使人報其妻 - He then sent someone to inform his wife.
    • 已賣宅移居武進矣 - She has already sold the house and moved to Wujin.
    • 固往語之 - He insisted on telling her.
    • 告買宅主 - Told the new owner of the house.
    • 令掘之 - To dig it up.
    • 果得錢如其數焉 - Indeed, the money was found as stated.
    • 即遣其妻與女市鐲 - He immediately sent his wife to buy bracelets for his daughter.
    • 尋而龔穎亦亡 - Soon after, Gong Ying also died.
    • 時果共奇其事 - At that time, people indeed found the event strange.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 北府 - Refers to the Northern Army, a significant military force during the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
    • 中郎荀羡 - Zhonglang Xun Xian, a high-ranking official during the Jin Dynasty.
    • 晉太元五年 - The fifth year of the Taiyuan era of the Jin Dynasty, which corresponds to 380 AD.
    • 三將 - Refers to three generals, likely a reference to a specific military context.
    • 龔穎 - Gong Ying, another person mentioned in the text, who also experienced a similar event.
    • 太山門主 - "Master of the Gate of Tai Mountain," a term often used in Chinese mythology to refer to a guardian spirit or a position in the afterlife.
    • 武進 - A place name, likely referring to a city or region in China.

    Proper Nouns

    • 北府索盧貞 - A proper name referring to a person from the Northern Army.
    • 中郎荀羡 - Zhonglang Xun Xian, a high-ranking official.
    • 晉太元五年 - The fifth year of the Taiyuan era of the Jin Dynasty.
    • 羡之子粹 - Cui, the son of Xun Xian.
    • 龔穎 - Gong Ying, another person mentioned in the text.
    • 太山門主 - "Master of the Gate of Tai Mountain."
    • 武進 - A place name.

    A man from the Northern Army named Suolu Zhen, who was originally a subordinate of Zhonglang Xun Xian, died in June of the fifth year of the Taiyuan era of the Jin Dynasty. After one night, he revived and said he saw Xian's son Cui. Cui was surprised and said, "Your fate is not yet complete, but the official needs three generals, so I cannot let you go just yet. If you know of someone as capable and agile as you, I will have them take your place." Suolu Zhen then recommended Gong Ying. Cui asked, "Is Gong Ying capable?" Suolu Zhen replied, "He is no longer inferior to me." Cui initially asked Suolu Zhen to write down the name, but since the writing was not used by ghosts, Cui took a pen and wrote it himself. Suolu Zhen then left. Suddenly, he saw a former neighbor who had been dead for seven or eight years, who was now the Master of the Gate of Tai Mountain. He asked Suolu Zhen, "Why does the General alone get to return?" He then instructed Suolu Zhen to say upon his return, "Please convey my greetings to my wife. Before I died, I buried 15,000 coins under the large bed in the house. I originally wanted to buy bracelets for my daughter, but unaware of my sudden death, I could not speak to my daughter or wife." Suolu Zhen agreed. Upon reviving, he sent someone to inform his wife, who had already sold the house and moved to Wujin. He insisted on telling her and also informed the new owner of the house to dig it up. Indeed, the money was found as stated. He immediately sent his wife to buy bracelets for his daughter. Soon after, Gong Ying also died, and at that time, people indeed found the event strange. [50]

    琅邪人，姓王，忘名，居钱塘。妻朱氏，以太元九年病亡，有二孤儿。王复以其年四月暴死，三日，而心下犹暖，经七日方苏。说：初死时，有二十余人，皆乌衣，见录。剥去到朱门白壁，状如宫殿。吏朱衣紫带，玄冠介帻。或所被着，悉珠玉相连结，非世中仪服。复前，见一人长大，所着衣状如云气。王向叩头，自说：“妇已亡，余孤儿，尚小，无奈何。”便流涕。此人为之动容，云：“汝命自应来，以汝孤儿，特与三年之期。”王又曰：“三年不足活儿。”左右有一人语云：“俗尸何痴？此间三年，世中是三十年。”因便送出。又三十年，王果卒。 [51]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [51]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 琅邪人 - A person from Langya, an ancient region in Shandong Province.
    • 姓王 - Surname Wang.
    • 忘名 - Forgotten name, implying the name is unknown or not recorded.
    • 居钱塘 - Resided in Qiantang (modern-day Hangzhou).
    • 妻朱氏 - Wife surnamed Zhu.
    • 以太元九年病亡 - Died of illness in the ninth year of the Taiyuan era (384 AD).
    • 有二孤儿 - Left behind two orphaned children.
    • 王复以其年四月暴死 - Wang died suddenly in the fourth month of that same year.
    • 三日，而心下犹暖 - Three days later, his heart was still warm, indicating recent death.
    • 经七日方苏 - After seven days, he revived.
    • 初死时，有二十余人，皆乌衣 - When he first died, there were more than twenty people, all wearing black clothing.
    • 见录 - Were taken or led somewhere.
    • 剥去到朱门白壁 - Taken to a red gate with white walls, describing a grand, palatial setting.
    • 状如宫殿 - Resembling a palace.
    • 吏朱衣紫带，玄冠介帻 - Officials wearing red robes, purple sashes, black hats, and pointed headgear.
    • 或所被着，悉珠玉相连结 - Some were adorned with jewelry and precious stones connected together.
    • 非世中仪服 - Not the attire found in the world, suggesting a supernatural or otherworldly setting.
    • 复前，见一人长大，所着衣状如云气 - He approached and saw a tall figure wearing clothes resembling clouds.
    • 王向叩头 - Wang bowed his head in respect.
    • 自说 - Spoke for himself.
    • 妇已亡，余孤儿，尚小，无奈何 - My wife is dead, I have two young orphans, I have no choice.
    • 便流涕 - He wept.
    • 此人为之动容 - The person showed compassion.
    • 云 - Said.
    • 汝命自应来 - Your life will naturally come back.
    • 以汝孤儿，特与三年之期 - For your orphans, I grant you a special period of three years.
    • 王又曰 - Wang replied.
    • 三年不足活儿 - Three years is not enough to raise the children.
    • 左右有一人语云 - Someone nearby said.
    • 俗尸何痴 - How foolish the common folk are.
    • 此间三年，世中是三十年 - Three years here is thirty years in the world.
    • 因便送出 - They then sent him back.
    • 又三十年，王果卒 - After another thirty years, Wang indeed died.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 琅邪 - An ancient region in Shandong Province, known for its historical significance.
    • 钱塘 - Modern-day Hangzhou, a city with a rich history and cultural heritage.
    • 太元九年 - The ninth year of the Taiyuan era (384 AD), a period during the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
    • 乌衣 - Black clothing, often associated with mourning or the underworld.
    • 朱门白壁 - Red gate with white walls, symbolizing a grand and noble place.
    • 吏朱衣紫带，玄冠介帻 - Official attire, indicating a formal and authoritative setting.
    • 珠玉相连结 - Jewelry and precious stones, symbolizing wealth and status.
    • 非世中仪服 - Attire not found in the world, suggesting a supernatural or otherworldly setting.
    • 云气 - Cloud-like appearance, often associated with deities or spirits.

    Proper Nouns

    • 琅邪 - Ancient region in Shandong.
    • 钱塘 - Modern-day Hangzhou.
    • 太元九年 - 384 AD.
    • 乌衣 - Black clothing.
    • 朱门白壁 - Red gate with white walls.
    • 吏朱衣紫带，玄冠介帻 - Official attire.
    • 珠玉相连结 - Jewelry and precious stones.
    • 非世中仪服 - Supernatural attire.
    • 云气 - Cloud-like appearance.

    A Man from Langya

    A man from Langya, surnamed Wang, whose name is forgotten, lived in Qiantang. His wife, surnamed Zhu, died of illness in the ninth year of the Taiyuan era (384 AD), leaving behind two orphaned children. Wang himself died suddenly in the fourth month of that same year. Three days later, his heart was still warm, and he revived after seven days. He recounted: When he first died, there were more than twenty people, all wearing black clothing, who took him to a place with a red gate and white walls, resembling a palace. The officials there wore red robes, purple sashes, black hats, and pointed headgear. Some were adorned with jewelry and precious stones connected together, not the attire found in the world. He approached and saw a tall figure wearing clothes resembling clouds. Wang bowed his head in respect and spoke for himself: "My wife is dead, I have two young orphans, I have no choice." He wept. The person showed compassion and said, "Your life will naturally come back. For your orphans, I grant you a special period of three years." Wang replied, "Three years is not enough to raise the children." Someone nearby said, "How foolish the common folk are. Three years here is thirty years in the world." They then sent him back. After another thirty years, Wang indeed died. [51]

    晋太元十年，阮瑜之居在始兴佛图前，少孤贫不立，哭泣无时。忽见一鬼书砖着前云：“父死归玄冥，何为久哭泣？即后三年中，君家可得立。仆当寄君家，不使有损失。勿畏我为凶，要为君作吉。”后鬼恒在家，家须用者，鬼与之。二三年，用小差，为鬼作食，共谈笑语议。阮问姓，答云：姓李名留之，是君姊夫耳。”阮问：“君那得来？”鬼云：“仆受罪已毕，今暂生鬼道，权寄君家，后四五年当去。”曰：“复何处去？”答云：“当生世间。”至期，果别而去。 [52]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [52]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 少孤贫不立 - Literally 'young, orphaned, poor, and without establishment,' meaning he was young, orphaned, poor, and struggling to make a living.
    • 哭泣无时 - Means 'crying without ceasing,' indicating constant mourning.
    • 忽见一鬼书砖着前 - Literally 'suddenly saw a ghost writing on a brick in front,' describing a supernatural event.
    • 父死归玄冥 - Literally 'father died and returned to the underworld,' referring to the afterlife.
    • 何为久哭泣 - Means 'why are you crying for so long,' a question from the ghost.
    • 即后三年中，君家可得立 - Means 'in about three years, your family will be established,' a prophecy from the ghost.
    • 仆当寄君家，不使有损失 - Means 'I will stay with your family and will not cause any loss,' a promise from the ghost.
    • 勿畏我为凶，要为君作吉 - Means 'do not fear me as being harmful, I will bring good fortune to you,' a reassurance from the ghost.
    • 后鬼恒在家 - Means 'afterwards, the ghost always stayed at home.'
    • 家须用者，鬼与之 - Means 'whenever the family needed something, the ghost provided it.'
    • 二三年，用小差 - Means 'after two or three years, the family's situation improved slightly.'
    • 为鬼作食，共谈笑语议 - Means 'he prepared food for the ghost, and they talked and laughed together.'
    • 阮问姓，答云：姓李名留之，是君姊夫耳 - Means 'Ruan asked about the ghost's name, and it replied: 'My name is Li Liu Zhi, I am your sister's husband.''
    • 阮问：‘君那得来？’ - Means 'Ruan asked: 'How did you come here?'
    • 鬼云：‘仆受罪已毕，今暂生鬼道，权寄君家，后四五年当去。’ - Means 'The ghost replied: 'I have finished my punishment, now I temporarily live as a ghost, staying with your family for four or five years, then I will leave.'
    • 复何处去？ - Means 'Where will you go after that?'
    • 答云：‘当生世间。’ - Means 'It replied: 'I will be reborn in the world.'
    • 至期，果别而去 - Means 'When the time came, the ghost indeed parted and left.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋太元十年 - The reign title of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin, indicating the historical context of the story.
    • 始兴佛图前 - Refers to a place near a Buddhist temple in Shixing, suggesting a setting with religious significance.
    • 玄冥 - Refers to the underworld or the afterlife, a common concept in Chinese mythology.
    • 姊夫 - Refers to the husband of one's sister, a familial relationship.
    • 鬼道 - Refers to the realm of ghosts, a concept in Chinese folklore.
    • 世间 - Refers to the human world, contrasting with the ghostly realm.

    Proper Nouns

    • 阮瑜 - The main character of the story.
    • 李留之 - The name of the ghost, who is revealed to be Ruan's sister's husband.
    • 姊夫 - The husband of Ruan's sister.
    • 晋太元 - The reign title of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin.
    • 始兴 - A place name, likely referring to a region in present-day Guangdong Province.
    • 佛图 - A Buddhist temple or pagoda.

    In the tenth year of the Taiyuan era of the Jin dynasty, Ruan Yu lived near the Buddhist temple in Shixing. He was young, orphaned, poor, and struggling to make a living, crying without ceasing. Suddenly, he saw a ghost writing on a brick in front of him, saying, "Your father has died and returned to the underworld. Why are you crying for so long? In about three years, your family will be established. I will stay with your family and will not cause any loss. Do not fear me as being harmful; I will bring good fortune to you."

    Afterwards, the ghost always stayed at home. Whenever the family needed something, the ghost provided it. After two or three years, the family's situation improved slightly. Ruan prepared food for the ghost, and they talked and laughed together.

    Ruan asked about the ghost's name, and it replied, "My name is Li Liu Zhi, I am your sister's husband." Ruan asked, "How did you come here?" The ghost replied, "I have finished my punishment, now I temporarily live as a ghost, staying with your family for four or five years, then I will leave." Ruan asked, "Where will you go after that?" It replied, "I will be reborn in the world." When the time came, the ghost indeed parted and left. [52]

    晋太元中，瓦官寺佛图前淳于矜，年少洁白。送客至石头城南，逢一女子，美姿容。矜悦之，因访问。二情既和，将入城北角，共尽欢好，便各分别。期更克集，便欲结为伉俪。女曰：“得婿如君，死何恨？我兄弟多，父母并在，当问我父母。”矜便令女婢问其父母，父母亦悬许之。女因敕婢取银百斤，绢百匹，助矜成婚。经久，养两儿。当作秘书监，明日，驺卒来召，车马导从，前后部鼓吹。经少日，有猎者过，觅矜，将数十狗，径突入，咋妇及儿，并成狸。绢帛金银，并是草及死人骨蛇魅等。 [53]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [53]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 晋太元中 - Refers to the reign period of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin, specifically the Taiyuan era, which lasted from 376 to 396 AD.
    • 瓦官寺佛图前 - Wangguan Temple Buddha Pagoda Front, indicating a specific location.
    • 年少洁白 - Describes the youth as young and white (possibly fair-skinned).
    • 送客至石头城南 - Sending guests to the south of Shitou City, indicating a specific action and location.
    • 逢一女子，美姿容 - Encountered a woman with beautiful appearance, describing the encounter.
    • 矜悦之 - Jin was pleased with her, indicating Jin's reaction.
    • 因访问 - Asked about her, indicating a conversation.
    • 二情既和 - Their feelings were harmonious, indicating mutual attraction.
    • 将入城北角 - Plan to enter the north corner of the city, indicating a planned action.
    • 共尽欢好 - Enjoyed each other's company, indicating a romantic relationship.
    • 便各分别 - Then separated, indicating the end of their time together.
    • 期更克集 - Agreed to meet again, indicating a future plan.
    • 便欲结为伉俪 - Wanted to marry each other, indicating a desire for marriage.
    • 女曰：“得婿如君，死何恨？我兄弟多，父母并在，当问我父母。” - The woman said, 'To have a husband like you, what regret would I have? I have many brothers, and my parents are still alive. I should ask my parents.' This is a direct quote, indicating the woman's response.
    • 矜便令女婢问其父母 - Jin then ordered a female servant to ask her parents, indicating a specific action.
    • 父母亦悬许之 - Her parents also tentatively agreed, indicating the parents' response.
    • 女因敕婢取银百斤，绢百匹 - The woman then ordered the servant to fetch a hundred pounds of silver and a hundred bolts of silk, indicating a specific action.
    • 助矜成婚 - To help Jin complete the marriage, indicating the purpose of the silver and silk.
    • 经久，养两儿 - After a long time, they had two sons, indicating the outcome of the marriage.
    • 当作秘书监 - He was supposed to become the Secretary General, indicating a future position.
    • 明日，驺卒来召 - The next day, a mounted official came to summon him, indicating a specific event.
    • 车马导从，前后部鼓吹 - With a retinue of carriages and horses, and music before and after, indicating the grandeur of the summons.
    • 经少日 - After a few days, indicating a short period of time.
    • 有猎者过 - There was a hunter passing by, indicating a specific event.
    • 觅矜，将数十狗，径突入 - Looking for Jin, with dozens of dogs, rushed in directly, indicating the hunter's actions.
    • 咋妇及儿，并成狸 - Bitten the wife and children, turning them all into weasels, indicating the tragic outcome.
    • 绢帛金银，并是草及死人骨蛇魅等 - The silk, cloth, silver, and gold turned into grass, dead bones, and snake ghosts, indicating the magical transformation.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋太元中 - The reign period of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin, a significant era in Chinese history.
    • 瓦官寺佛图前 - Wangguan Temple, a historical temple in Nanjing, known for its Buddha pagoda.
    • 淳于矜 - A personal name, likely a noble or official during the Jin dynasty.
    • 石头城 - Shitou City, an ancient city in Nanjing, known for its strategic importance.
    • 秘书监 - A high-ranking official position in the imperial court, responsible for important documents and records.
    • 驺卒 - A mounted official, typically a messenger or envoy.
    • 鼓吹 - A musical ensemble, often used in ceremonial and official functions.

    Proper Nouns

    • 晋太元中 - The reign period of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin.
    • 瓦官寺佛图前 - Wangguan Temple Buddha Pagoda Front.
    • 淳于矜 - A personal name, likely a noble or official.
    • 石头城 - Shitou City.
    • 秘书监 - A high-ranking official position.
    • 驺卒 - A mounted official.
    • 鼓吹 - A musical ensemble.

    During the Taiyuan era of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin, at the front of the Buddha pagoda of Wangguan Temple, there was a young man named Chunyu Jin, who was fair-skinned and handsome. While sending guests to the south of Shitou City, he encountered a woman with a beautiful appearance. Jin was pleased with her and asked about her. Their feelings were harmonious, and they planned to meet in the north corner of the city to enjoy each other's company, but then they separated. They agreed to meet again and wanted to marry each other. The woman said, "To have a husband like you, what regret would I have? I have many brothers, and my parents are still alive. I should ask my parents." Jin then ordered a female servant to ask her parents, and her parents also tentatively agreed. The woman then ordered the servant to fetch a hundred pounds of silver and a hundred bolts of silk to help Jin complete the marriage. After a long time, they had two sons. Jin was supposed to become the Secretary General. The next day, a mounted official came to summon him, with a retinue of carriages and horses and music before and after. After a few days, a hunter passed by, looking for Jin, with dozens of dogs, rushed in directly, and bit the wife and children, turning them all into weasels. The silk, cloth, silver, and gold turned into grass, dead bones, and snake ghosts. [53]

    晋太元中，高衡为魏郡太守，戍石头。其孙雅之在厩中，云有神来降，自称白头公，拄杖，光耀照屋。与雅之轻举霄行，暮至京口，晨已来还。后雅之父子为桓玄所灭。
    　　大元中，临海有李巫，不知所由来。能卜相作，水符治病多愈，亦礼佛读经。语人云：“明年天下当大疫，此境尤剧。又，二纪之后，此邦之西北大郡，僵尸横路。”时汝南周叔道罢临海令，权停家。巫云：“周令今去宜南行，必当暴死。”便指北山曰：“后二十日，此应有异事彰也。后十日余，大石夜颓落百丈，砰磕若雷。庾楷为临海太守，过诣周，设馔作伎。至夜，庾还航中，天晓。庾自披屏风，呼：“叔道，何痴不起？”左右怃看，气绝久矣。到明年，县内病死者数千人。 [54]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [54]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 晋太元中 - The Taiyuan period of the Jin dynasty, a specific historical time frame.
    • 高衡为魏郡太守 - Gao Heng served as the governor of Wei Prefecture.
    • 戍石头 - Garrisoned at Shitou.
    • 其孙雅之在厩中 - His grandson Ya Zhi was in the stable.
    • 云有神来降 - Reported that a god descended.
    • 自称白头公 - Called himself White-Haired Old Man.
    • 拄杖 - Carrying a cane.
    • 光耀照屋 - His light illuminated the house.
    • 与雅之轻举霄行 - Flew with Ya Zhi through the sky.
    • 暮至京口，晨已来还 - Arrived at Jingkou in the evening and returned in the morning.
    • 后雅之父子为桓玄所灭 - Later, Ya Zhi and his father were killed by Huan Xuan.
    • 大元中 - During the Danyuan period, another specific historical time frame.
    • 临海有李巫 - In Linhai, there was a man named Li Wu.
    • 不知所由来 - Unknown origin.
    • 能卜相作，水符治病多愈 - Could perform divination, make talismans, and treat illnesses with water charms, curing many.
    • 亦礼佛读经 - Also worshiped Buddha and read scriptures.
    • 语人云 - Told people.
    • 明年天下当大疫，此境尤剧 - Next year, there will be a great epidemic in the world, especially severe in this region.
    • 二纪之后，此邦之西北大郡，僵尸横路 - After two decades, the large prefecture in the northwest of this state will have corpses lying in the streets.
    • 时汝南周叔道罢临海令，权停家 - At that time, Zhou Shudao of Runan resigned from his position as magistrate of Linhai and temporarily stayed at home.
    • 巫云 - The sorcerer said.
    • 周令今去宜南行，必当暴死 - The magistrate should go south now, he will certainly die suddenly.
    • 便指北山曰 - Pointed to the north mountain and said.
    • 后二十日，此应有异事彰也 - In twenty days, there will be an unusual event here.
    • 后十日余，大石夜颓落百丈，砰磕若雷 - After ten more days, a large stone fell at night from a hundred feet, crashing like thunder.
    • 庾楷为临海太守，过诣周，设馔作伎 - Yu Kai, the governor of Linhai, visited Zhou, prepared a feast, and performed entertainments.
    • 至夜，庾还航中，天晓 - At night, Yu returned to his boat, and it was dawn.
    • 庾自披屏风，呼：“叔道，何痴不起？” - Yu himself pulled aside the screen and called out, 'Shudao, why are you so foolish and not getting up?'.
    • 左右怃看，气绝久矣 - Those around him looked in shock, and he had been dead for a long time.
    • 到明年，县内病死者数千人 - By the next year, thousands of people in the county had died from illness.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋太元中 - The Taiyuan period of the Jin dynasty, a significant historical period.
    • 高衡 - A historical figure who served as the governor of Wei Prefecture.
    • 魏郡 - A prefecture during the Jin dynasty.
    • 石头 - A location where Gao Heng was garrisoned.
    • 白头公 - A title given to a deity, possibly a reference to an ancient Chinese deity.
    • 京口 - A place name, likely referring to a city or town.
    • 桓玄 - A historical figure, likely a powerful official or warlord.
    • 大元中 - The Danyuan period, another significant historical period.
    • 临海 - A place name, likely referring to a city or region.
    • 李巫 - A historical figure, likely a sorcerer or shaman.
    • 汝南 - A place name, likely referring to a city or region.
    • 周叔道 - A historical figure, likely a magistrate or local official.
    • 庾楷 - A historical figure, likely a governor or high-ranking official.
    • 临海太守 - The governor of Linhai, a high-ranking official.
    • 设馔作伎 - Preparing a feast and performing entertainments, common practices in ancient Chinese social gatherings.
    • 航中 - Refers to a boat or ship, likely a place where Yu Kai stayed.
    • 屏风 - A screen or partition, commonly used in ancient Chinese homes.
    • 气绝 - Death, a common term in classical Chinese texts.
    • 病死者 - Those who died from illness, a common term in historical records.

    During the Taiyuan period of the Jin dynasty, Gao Heng served as the governor of Wei Prefecture and was stationed at Shitou. His grandson Ya Zhi was in the stable when he reported that a god had descended, calling himself the White-Haired Old Man, carrying a cane, and his light illuminated the house. He flew with Ya Zhi through the sky, arriving at Jingkou in the evening and returning in the morning. Later, Ya Zhi and his father were killed by Huan Xuan.

    During the Danyuan period, in Linhai, there was a man named Li Wu, whose origin was unknown. He could perform divination, make talismans, and treat illnesses with water charms, curing many. He also worshiped Buddha and read scriptures. He told people, "Next year, there will be a great epidemic in the world, especially severe in this region. Moreover, after two decades, the large prefecture in the northwest of this state will have corpses lying in the streets." At that time, Zhou Shudao of Runan resigned from his position as magistrate of Linhai and temporarily stayed at home. The sorcerer said, "The magistrate should go south now, he will certainly die suddenly." He pointed to the north mountain and said, "In twenty days, there will be an unusual event here." After ten more days, a large stone fell at night from a hundred feet, crashing like thunder. Yu Kai, the governor of Linhai, visited Zhou, prepared a feast, and performed entertainments. At night, Yu returned to his boat, and it was dawn. Yu himself pulled aside the screen and called out, "Shudao, why are you so foolish and not getting up?" Those around him looked in shock, and he had been dead for a long time. By the next year, thousands of people in the county had died from illness. [54]

    泰元中，有一师从远来，莫知所出，云：“人命应终，有生乐代死者，则死者可生。若逼人求代，亦复不过少时。”人闻此，咸怪其虚诞。王子猷。子敬兄弟特相和睦。子敬疾，属纩，子猷谓之曰：“吾才不如弟，位亦通塞，请以余年代弟。”师曰：“夫生代死者，以己年限有余，得以足亡者耳。今贤弟命既应终，君侯算亦当尽，复何所代？”子猷先有背疾，子敬疾笃，恒禁来往。闻亡，便抚心悲惋，都不得一声，背即溃裂。推师之言，信而有实。 [55]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [55]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 有生乐代死者 - Those who are willing to live in place of the deceased.
    • 若逼人求代 - If forced to replace someone.
    • 亦复不过少时 - It will also not last for long.
    • 属纩 - Placing a silk cloth over the mouth and nose of a dying person.
    • 吾才不如弟 - My talent is not as good as my younger brother's.
    • 位亦通塞 - My position is also limited.
    • 请以余年代弟 - I request that I be allowed to take my younger brother's place.
    • 夫生代死者 - Those who live in place of the deceased.
    • 以己年限有余 - Using their own remaining years.
    • 得以足亡者耳 - To make up for the deceased.
    • 命既应终 - Fate has already determined the end.
    • 君侯算亦当尽 - Your fate is also nearly at an end.
    • 复何所代 - What can be replaced?
    • 子敬疾笃 - My younger brother's illness is severe.
    • 恒禁来往 - He always forbade visits.
    • 闻亡 - Upon hearing of his death.
    • 抚心悲惋 - To hold one's heart in sorrow and grief.
    • 都不得一声 - Not uttering a single sound.
    • 背即溃裂 - His back immediately ruptured.
    • 推师之言 - Considering the master's words.
    • 信而有实 - True and substantiated.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 泰元中 - A time period during the Later Jin Dynasty (376-417 AD).
    • 王子猷、子敬兄弟 - Refers to Wang Xizhi and his younger brother Wang Xianzhi, both famous calligraphers and artists of the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
    • 子敬疾笃 - Refers to the severe illness of Wang Xianzhi.
    • 属纩 - A traditional practice in ancient China where a silk cloth is placed over the mouth and nose of a dying person to check for breathing.

    Proper Nouns

    • 泰元中 - A time period during the Later Jin Dynasty.
    • 王子猷 - Wang Xizhi, a famous calligrapher and artist.
    • 子敬 - Wang Xianzhi, the younger brother of Wang Xizhi.
    • 属纩 - A traditional practice in ancient China.

    During the Taiyuan era, there came a master from afar, whose origins were unknown. He said, "When a person's fate is to die, if there is someone willing to live in their place, then the deceased can be revived. But if forced to replace someone, it will also not last for long." People who heard this found it strange and considered it absurd. Wang Xizhi and his younger brother Wang Xianzhi were particularly close and harmonious. When Wang Xianzhi fell ill and was near death, Wang Xizhi said to him, "My talent is not as good as yours, and my position is also limited. I request that I be allowed to take your place." The master replied, "Those who live in place of the deceased use their own remaining years to make up for the deceased. Now, your younger brother's fate has already determined the end, and your fate is also nearly at an end. What can be replaced?" Wang Xizhi had a pre-existing back condition, and Wang Xianzhi's illness was severe, so he always forbade visits. Upon hearing of his death, Wang Xizhi held his heart in sorrow and grief, not uttering a single sound, and his back immediately ruptured. Considering the master's words, they were true and substantiated. [55]

    王允、祖安国、张显等，以太元中乘船。见仙人赐糖饴三饼，大如比输钱，厚二分。
    　　大元中，北地人陈良，与沛国刘舒友善。又与同邻李焉，共为商贾，曾获厚利，共致酒相庆，焉遂害良。以韦裹之，弃之荒草。经十许日，良复生归家。说：死时，见一人着赤帻引良去，造一城门，门下有一床，见一老人执朱笔点校。赤帻人言曰：“向下土有一人，姓陈名良，游魂而已，未有统摄，是以将来。”校籍者曰：“可令便去。”良既出，忽见友人刘舒，谓曰：“不图于此相见。卿今幸蒙尊神所遣，然我家厕屋后桑树中有一狸，常作妖怪，我家数数横受苦恼。卿归，岂能为我说邪？”良然之。既苏，乃诣官疏李焉而伏罪。仍特报舒家，家人涕泣，云悉如言。因伐树得狸，杀之，其怪遂绝。 [56]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [56]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 太元中 - Refers to the reign period of Emperor Xiaowu of Eastern Jin, specifically the year 383 AD, known as the Battle of Fei River.
    • 乘船 - Literally 'riding a boat,' used here to mean traveling by boat.
    • 仙人 - Refers to a deity or immortal in Chinese mythology.
    • 糖饴 - Refers to a type of candy or sweet.
    • 饼 - Refers to a flat, round cake or biscuit.
    • 比输钱 - A unit of measurement, likely referring to a specific size or weight.
    • 厚二分 - Means 'two fen thick,' where fen is a unit of length or thickness.
    • 北地人 - Refers to someone from the northern region.
    • 沛国 - Refers to a historical region in present-day Anhui Province.
    • 共为商贾 - Means 'together engage in commerce.'
    • 获厚利 - Means 'to gain substantial profit.'
    • 致酒相庆 - Means 'to offer wine to celebrate.'
    • 以韦裹之 - Means 'to wrap it in leather.'
    • 荒草 - Refers to overgrown grass or wilderness.
    • 造一城门 - Means 'to create a city gate.'
    • 执朱笔点校 - Means 'to hold a red pen for checking or correcting.'
    • 可令便去 - Means 'can let him go immediately.'
    • 死时 - Refers to the time of death.
    • 见一人着赤帻 - Means 'to see a person wearing a red headband.'
    • 引良去 - Means 'to lead Teng Liang away.'
    • 门下有一床 - Means 'there is a bed under the gate.'
    • 赤帻人言曰 - Means 'the person with the red headband said.'
    • 向下土有一人 - Means 'there is one person in the lower world.'
    • 姓陈名良 - Means 'named Chen Liang.'
    • 游魂而已 - Means 'just a wandering spirit.'
    • 未有统摄 - Means 'not yet governed.'
    • 校籍者曰 - Means 'the one checking the records said.'
    • 忽见友人刘舒 - Means 'suddenly saw his friend Liu Shu.'
    • 不图于此相见 - Means 'did not expect to meet here.'
    • 卿今幸蒙尊神所遣 - Means 'you are now fortunate to be sent by the revered deity.'
    • 我家厕屋后桑树中有一狸 - Means 'there is a fox in the mulberry tree behind my toilet house.'
    • 常作妖怪 - Means 'often causes mischief.'
    • 我家数数横受苦恼 - Means 'our family often suffers greatly.'
    • 卿归，岂能为我说邪 - Means 'when you return, can you tell me about it?'
    • 既苏，乃诣官疏李焉而伏罪 - Means 'after regaining consciousness, he went to the authorities to report Ruan Yan and confessed his crime.'
    • 仍特报舒家 - Means 'then specially informed Liu Shu's family.'
    • 家人涕泣 - Means 'the family members wept.'
    • 云悉如言 - Means 'said exactly as he had described.'
    • 因伐树得狸 - Means 'they cut down the tree and found the fox.'
    • 杀之，其怪遂绝 - Means 'killed it, and the strange occurrences ceased.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 太元中 - Refers to the reign period of Emperor Xiaowu of Eastern Jin, specifically the year 383 AD, known for the Battle of Fei River.
    • 北地人 - Refers to someone from the northern region, which was often associated with different cultural practices compared to the south.
    • 沛国 - Refers to a historical region in present-day Anhui Province, known for its significance in Chinese history.
    • 仙人 - Refers to deities or immortals in Chinese mythology, often associated with supernatural abilities and longevity.
    • 糖饴 - Refers to a type of candy or sweet, which was a common gift or offering in ancient China.
    • 饼 - Refers to a flat, round cake or biscuit, often used in rituals or as food offerings.
    • 比输钱 - A unit of measurement, likely referring to a specific size or weight, used to describe the dimensions of the candy.
    • 厚二分 - Means 'two fen thick,' where fen is a unit of length or thickness, used to describe the thickness of the candy.
    • 韦 - Refers to leather, often used for wrapping or binding items.
    • 荒草 - Refers to overgrown grass or wilderness, often associated with desolate or abandoned areas.
    • 城门 - Refers to a city gate, a significant structure in ancient Chinese cities.
    • 朱笔 - Refers to a red pen, often used for writing or marking important documents.
    • 赤帻 - Refers to a red headband, often worn by officials or deities in ancient China.
    • 游魂 - Refers to a wandering spirit, often associated with the afterlife or the realm of the dead.
    • 统摄 - Refers to governance or control, often used in the context of spiritual or administrative authority.
    • 厕屋 - Refers to a toilet house, often a humble or unsanitary area.
    • 狸 - Refers to a fox, often associated with trickery or mischief in Chinese folklore.
    • 妖怪 - Refers to strange or supernatural occurrences, often associated with evil spirits or malevolent forces.

    Proper Nouns

    • 王允 - A person's name.
    • 祖安国 - A person's name.
    • 张显 - A person's name.
    • 陈良 - A person's name.
    • 刘舒 - A person's name.
    • 李焉 - A person's name.
    • 赤帻人 - A person with a red headband.
    • 校籍者 - The one checking the records.
    • 狸 - A fox.

    During the reign of Emperor Xiaowu, Wang Yun, Zu Anguo, and Zhang Xian were traveling by boat. They encountered an immortal who gave them three candies, each as large as a certain coin and two fen thick.

    During the reign of Emperor Xiaowu, a man from the northern region named Chen Liang was friends with Liu Shu from Pei State. He also had a neighbor named Li Yan with whom he engaged in commerce and once gained substantial profits. They celebrated together with wine, and Li Yan subsequently harmed Chen Liang. He wrapped Chen Liang in leather and left him in overgrown grass. After about ten days, Chen Liang revived and returned home. He recounted: "At the time of my death, I saw a person wearing a red headband leading me away to a city gate. Under the gate, there was a bed, and I saw an old man holding a red pen for checking or correcting. The person with the red headband said, 'There is a person in the lower world, named Chen Liang, just a wandering spirit, not yet governed, so he has come here.' The one checking the records said, 'Let him go immediately.' I then came out and suddenly saw my friend Liu Shu, who said, 'I did not expect to meet here. You are now fortunate to be sent by the revered deity. However, there is a fox in the mulberry tree behind my toilet house that often causes mischief, and our family often suffers greatly. When you return, can you tell me about it?' I agreed. After regaining consciousness, I went to the authorities to report Li Yan and confessed my crime. I then specially informed Liu Shu's family, who wept, saying exactly as I had described. They cut down the tree and found the fox, killed it, and the strange occurrences ceased." [56]

    晋太元末，长星见，孝武甚恶之。是日，华林园中饮，帝因举杯属星曰：“长星，劝尔一杯酒！自古亦何时有万岁天子？”取杯酬之。帝亦寻崩也。
    　　南康宫亭庙，殊有神验。晋孝武世，有一沙门至庙，神像见之，泪出交流，因标姓字，则是昔友也。自说：“我罪深，能见济脱不？”沙门即为斋戒诵经，语曰：“我欲见卿真形。”神云：“禀形甚丑，不可出也。”沙门苦请，遂化为蛇，身长数丈，垂头梁上，一心听经，目中血出。至七日七夜，蛇死，庙亦歇绝。
    　　晋孝武帝于殿中北窗下清暑，忽见一人，着白夹黄练单衣，举身沾濡，自称华林园中池水神，名曰淋涔君也。若善见待，当相福佑。时帝饮已醉，取常所佩刀掷之。刀空过无碍，神忿曰：“不以佳士垂接，当令知所以居。”少时，而帝暴崩。皆呼此灵为祸也。 [57]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [57]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 長星見 - Celestial phenomenon, likely referring to a comet or a meteor, often interpreted as an omen in ancient China.
    • 孝武甚惡之 - 孝武 refers to Emperor Xiaowu of Jin, and 甚惡之 means 'he deeply disliked it,' indicating his negative reaction to the omen.
    • 舉杯屬星 - 舉杯 means 'to raise a cup,' and 屬星 means 'to address the star,' implying a gesture of addressing the omen directly.
    • 勸爾一杯酒 - Direct address to the omen, literally meaning 'encourage you with a cup of wine,' which is a metaphorical way of trying to appease or challenge the omen.
    • 自古亦何時有萬歲天子 - Rhetorical question meaning 'since ancient times, when has there been a ruler who lives forever?' This reflects the emperor's skepticism about the concept of eternal rule.
    • 取杯酬之 - 取杯 means 'to take the cup,' and 酬之 means 'to reciprocate' or 'to return the gesture,' indicating that the emperor took the cup back and returned it to the omen.
    • 帝亦尋崩也 - 尋崩 means 'soon after his death,' indicating that the emperor died shortly after the event.
    • 南康宫亭庙 - Refers to a temple in Nankang, known for its divine power.
    • 神像見之，淚出交流 - 神像 means 'divine image,' and 泪出交流 means 'tears flowed continuously,' indicating the emotional reaction of the divine image.
    • 標姓字 - 標 means 'to mark,' and 姓字 means 'name and surname,' indicating the process of identifying oneself.
    • 昔友也 - Means 'an old friend,' suggesting a past relationship.
    • 我罪深，能見濟脫不 - 我罪深 means 'my sins are deep,' and 能見濟脫不 means 'can you help me escape?' indicating the divine image's plea for assistance.
    • 齋戒诵经 - 齋戒 means 'fasting and abstaining,' and 诵经 means 'reciting scriptures,' indicating a religious ritual.
    • 真形 - Means 'true form,' suggesting the transformation of the divine image into a more visible or tangible form.
    • 垂頭梁上 - 垂頭 means 'hanging its head,' and 梁上 means 'on the beam,' indicating the position of the snake.
    • 一心听经，目中血出 - 一心听经 means 'listening to the scriptures with all one's heart,' and 目中血出 means 'blood coming out of the eyes,' indicating intense concentration and suffering.
    • 蛇死，庙亦歇绝 - 蛇死 means 'the snake died,' and 庙亦歇绝 means 'the temple also ceased to function,' indicating the end of the temple's divine power.
    • 晋孝武帝于殿中北窗下清暑 - 清暑 means 'cooling off,' indicating the emperor's activity during a hot day.
    • 着白夹黄练单衣 - 着 means 'wearing,' 白夹黄练 means 'white and yellow linen,' and 单衣 means 'single garment,' describing the appearance of the deity.
    • 举身沾濡 - 举身 means 'entire body,' and 沾濡 means 'dripping wet,' indicating the deity's wet appearance.
    • 若善见待，当相福佑 - 若善见待 means 'if treated well,' and 当相福佑 means 'will bestow blessings,' indicating the deity's promise of good fortune.
    • 取常所佩刀掷之 - 取常所佩刀 means 'take the sword he usually wore,' and 掷之 means 'throw it,' indicating the emperor's action.
    • 刀空过无碍 - 刀空过 means 'the sword passed through,' and 无碍 means 'without obstruction,' indicating the supernatural nature of the event.
    • 不以佳士垂接，当令知所以居 - 不以佳士垂接 means 'not treating a good person with respect,' and 当令知所以居 means 'will make them know their place,' indicating the deity's threat.
    • 少时，而帝暴崩 - 少时 means 'shortly after,' and 暴崩 means 'sudden death,' indicating the emperor's quick demise.
    • 皆呼此灵为祸也 - 皆呼此灵 means 'all called this spirit,' and 为祸也 means 'a disaster,' indicating the general perception of the event as a bad omen.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋太元末 - Refers to the end of the Taiyuan period of the Eastern Jin Dynasty, a time of significant political and social change.
    • 孝武帝 - Emperor Xiaowu of Jin, a ruler known for his interest in alchemy and his eventual downfall.
    • 长星 - Often interpreted as a comet or a meteor, seen as an omen of significant events, often negative.
    • 华林园 - A famous imperial garden in the capital city of Jiankang (modern-day Nanjing), where the emperor often held banquets.
    • 南康宫亭庙 - A temple in Nankang known for its divine power and the story of a divine image that wept.
    • 晋孝武帝于殿中北窗下清暑 - The emperor cooling off in the northern window of the palace, a common practice in hot weather.

    Proper Nouns

    • 晋太元末 - The end of the Taiyuan period of the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
    • 孝武帝 - Emperor Xiaowu of Jin.
    • 华林园 - A famous imperial garden in Jiankang (modern-day Nanjing).
    • 南康宫亭庙 - A temple in Nankang known for its divine power.
    • 淋涔君 - The name of the water deity encountered by the emperor.

    At the end of the Taiyuan era of the Eastern Jin Dynasty, a long star appeared in the sky, which Emperor Xiaowu deeply disliked. On that day, he held a banquet in the Huayin Garden and, raising his cup, addressed the star, saying, "Long star, I offer you a cup of wine! Since ancient times, when has there been a ruler who lives forever?" He then took the cup and returned it to the star. Soon after, the emperor died.

    The temple in Nankang was renowned for its divine power. During the reign of Emperor Xiaowu, a monk came to the temple, and the divine image saw him, shedding tears continuously. The monk marked his name and surname, revealing that they were old friends. The divine image said, "My sins are deep, can you help me escape?" The monk then fasted and recited scriptures, telling the divine image, "I want to see your true form." The divine image replied, "My true form is very ugly, I cannot show it." The monk persistently requested, and the divine image transformed into a snake, several zhang long, hanging its head from the beam, listening intently to the scriptures, with blood coming out of its eyes. After seven days and nights, the snake died, and the temple's divine power ceased.

    Emperor Xiaowu of Jin was cooling off in the northern window of the palace when he suddenly saw a person wearing a white and yellow linen single garment, dripping wet. The person introduced himself as the water deity of the Huayin Garden, named Lin Cen Jun. If treated well, he would bestow blessings. At that time, the emperor was already drunk and took the sword he usually wore and threw it. The sword passed through without any obstruction. The deity, angered, said, "Not treating a good person with respect, I will make you know your place." Shortly after, the emperor suddenly died. All called this spirit a disaster. [57]

    义熙三年，山阴徐琦每出门，见一女子，貌极艳丽，琦便解臂上银钤赠之。女曰：“感君来贶。”以青铜镜与琦，便尔结为伉俪。
    　　晋义熙五年，彭城刘澄常见鬼。及为左卫司马，与将军巢营廨宇相接。澄夜相就坐语，见一小儿，赭衣，手把赤帜，团团似芙蓉花。数日，巢大遭火。
    　　义熙七年，东阳费道思新娶得妇，相爱。妇梳头，道思戏拔银钗着户阁头。
    　　晋义熙中，范寅为南康郡时，赣县吏说：先入山采薪，得二龟，皆如二尺盘大。薪未足，遇有两树骈生，吏以龟侧置树间，复行采伐。去龟处稍远，天雨，懒复取。后经十二年，复入山，见先龟，一者甲已枯；一者尚生，极长，树木所夹处，可厚四寸许，两头厚尺余，如马鞍状。 [58]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [58]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 為之一空 - Set phrase meaning completely emptied; fields were left barren.

    Proper Nouns

    • 紹興 - Era name of the Southern Song; rendered as Shaoxing.

    During the third year of the reign of Emperor Gong of Liu Song (407 AD), Xu Qi from Shanyin would often go out and see a woman with an extremely beautiful appearance. He then unbuckled the silver bracelet from his arm and gave it to her. The woman said, "Thank you for your gift." She gave him a bronze mirror, and thus they became husband and wife.

    During the fifth year of the reign of Emperor Gong of Liu Song (409 AD), Liu Cheng from Pengcheng often saw ghosts. When he became the Left Guard司马, his office was adjacent to the general's camp. One night, Liu Cheng met him to sit and talk, and saw a small child wearing a reddish-brown robe, holding a red flag that was round like a lotus flower. Several days later, the general's camp suffered a great fire.

    During the seventh year of the reign of Emperor Gong of Liu Song (411 AD), Fei Daosi from Dongyang recently married a wife, and they loved each other. The wife was combing her hair, and Fei Daosi playfully pulled out a silver hairpin and placed it on the door frame.

    During the middle period of the reign of Emperor Gong of Liu Song (407-420 AD), when Fan Yin was the magistrate of Nankang County, a county official from Ganzhou said: "First went into the mountains to gather firewood and found two turtles, both as large as two-foot plates. The firewood was not enough, and he encountered two trees growing close together. The official placed the turtles sideways between the trees and continued to cut more wood. A bit farther away from where the turtles were, it rained, and he was too lazy to retrieve them. After twelve years, he returned to the mountain and saw the previous turtles. One turtle's shell had already dried up, and the other was still alive, extremely long, with the trees clamping it about four inches thick, about a foot thick at both ends, like a horse saddle." [58]

    义熙中，江乘聂湖忽有一板，广数尺，长二丈余，恒停在此川溪，采菱及捕鱼者资以自济。后有数人共乘板入湖，试以刀斫，即有血出，板仍没，数人溺死。
    　　河东贾弼之，小名翳儿，具谙究世谱。义熙中，为琅邪府参军。夜梦有一人，面皻疱，甚多须，大鼻■〈目闲〉目，请之曰；“爱君之貌，欲易头，可乎？”弼曰：“人各有头面，岂容此理？”明昼又梦，意甚恶之。乃于梦中许易。明朝起，自不觉，而人悉惊走藏。云：“那汉何处来？”琅邪王大惊，遣传教呼视，弼到琅邪，遥见起还内。弼取镜自看，方知怪异。因还家，家人悉惊入内，妇女走藏，云：“那得异男子？”弼坐自陈说良久，并遣人至府检问，方信。后能半面啼，半面笑，两足、手、口、各捉一笔，俱书，辞意皆美。此为异也，余并如先。俄而安帝崩，恭帝立。 [59]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [59]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 義熙中 - A time period during the Eastern Jin dynasty, specifically the reign of Emperor Xiaowu (r. 403–418).
    • 江乘聂湖 - A specific location, likely a lake in Jiangcheng (江乘), a county in present-day Nanjing, Jiangsu province.
    • 廣數尺，長二丈余 - Describes the size of the board, which is several feet wide and over twenty feet long.
    • 恒停在此川溪 - Means 'always stays in this river or stream,' indicating a consistent presence.
    • 采菱及捕鱼者资以自济 - Means 'fishers and lotus gatherers use it to save themselves,' suggesting the board serves as a life-saving device.
    • 后有数人共乘板入湖 - Means 'later, several people got onto the board and entered the lake,' indicating an incident where the board was used.
    • 试以刀斫 - Means 'they tried cutting it with a knife,' describing an experiment conducted on the board.
    • 即有血出 - Means 'blood immediately flowed out,' indicating a surprising and mysterious reaction.
    • 板仍没 - Means 'the board then sank,' describing the aftermath of the experiment.
    • 数人溺死 - Means 'several people drowned,' indicating the tragic outcome.
    • 河东贾弼之 - A person named Jia Bi Zhi from Hedong (河东), a region in present-day Shanxi province.
    • 小名翳儿 - His nickname, 'Yier.'
    • 具谙究世谱 - Means 'thoroughly familiar with genealogies,' indicating his expertise in family histories.
    • 夜梦有一人 - Means 'he dreamed of a man at night,' introducing a supernatural element.
    • 面皻疱 - Means 'with many warts on his face,' describing the man's appearance.
    • 甚多须 - Means 'with a lot of beard,' further detailing the man's appearance.
    • 大鼻■〈目闲〉目 - Means 'with a large nose and eyes like a bird's,' describing the man's facial features.
    • 请之曰 - Means 'he said to him,' indicating a direct address.
    • 爱君之貌，欲易头，可乎 - Means 'I love your appearance, I want to exchange heads, can I?' indicating a strange request.
    • 弼曰 - Means 'Jia replied,' indicating his response.
    • 人各有头面，岂容此理 - Means 'Everyone has their own head and face, how can this be possible?' indicating his refusal.
    • 明昼又梦 - Means 'the next day he dreamed again,' indicating a recurring dream.
    • 意甚恶之 - Means 'he felt very disgusted by it,' indicating his negative reaction.
    • 乃于梦中许易 - Means 'so he agreed to exchange in his dream,' indicating a change of heart.
    • 明朝起，自不觉 - Means 'the next morning he woke up without realizing,' indicating a gradual realization.
    • 而人悉惊走藏 - Means 'everyone was surprised and ran away to hide,' indicating a reaction from others.
    • 那汉何处来 - Means 'where did that man come from?' indicating their confusion.
    • 琅邪王大惊 - Means 'the Prince of Langya was greatly alarmed,' indicating a high-ranking official's reaction.
    • 遣传教呼视 - Means 'he sent a messenger to call him over,' indicating an official summons.
    • 弼到琅邪，遥见起还内 - Means 'Jia arrived at Langya and saw him from afar returning inside,' indicating the man's disappearance.
    • 弼取镜自看 - Means 'Jia took a mirror and looked at himself,' indicating a self-inspection.
    • 方知怪异 - Means 'only then did he realize the strangeness,' indicating his realization.
    • 因还家 - Means 'he returned home,' indicating his departure.
    • 家人悉惊入内 - Means 'his family were all surprised and went inside,' indicating their reaction.
    • 妇女走藏 - Means 'women and girls ran and hid,' indicating their fear.
    • 那得异男子 - Means 'where did that strange man come from?' indicating their confusion.
    • 弼坐自陈说良久 - Means 'Jia sat and explained for a long time,' indicating his attempt to clarify.
    • 并遣人至府检问 - Means 'and sent people to the government office to check and ask,' indicating an official investigation.
    • 方信 - Means 'only then did they believe,' indicating the resolution of the mystery.
    • 后能半面啼，半面笑 - Means 'later he could cry on one side of his face and smile on the other,' indicating a bizarre physical change.
    • 两足、手、口、各捉一笔，俱书，辞意皆美 - Means 'both feet, hands, and mouth each held a pen and wrote simultaneously, with all words and meanings being beautiful,' indicating a miraculous ability.
    • 此为异也，余并如先 - Means 'this is strange, everything else remains as before,' indicating the continuation of his condition.
    • 俄而安帝崩，恭帝立 - Means 'soon after Emperor An died, Emperor Gong ascended the throne,' indicating a historical transition.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 义熙 - The era name of Emperor Xiaowu of the Eastern Jin dynasty (403–418).
    • 江乘聂湖 - A specific location in Jiangcheng, a county in present-day Nanjing, Jiangsu province.
    • 河东 - A region in present-day Shanxi province.
    • 琅邪王 - The Prince of Langya, a high-ranking official during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 安帝 - Emperor An of the Eastern Jin dynasty (r. 396–418).
    • 恭帝 - Emperor Gong of the Eastern Jin dynasty (r. 419–420).

    Proper Nouns

    • 江乘聂湖 - A specific location in Jiangcheng, a county in present-day Nanjing, Jiangsu province.
    • 河东 - A region in present-day Shanxi province.
    • 琅邪王 - The Prince of Langya, a high-ranking official during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 安帝 - Emperor An of the Eastern Jin dynasty (r. 396–418).
    • 恭帝 - Emperor Gong of the Eastern Jin dynasty (r. 419–420).

    During the reign of Emperor Xiaowu (Yixi period), a large board suddenly appeared in the Jiangcheng Lake. It was several feet wide and over twenty feet long, always staying in the river or stream. Fishers and lotus gatherers used it to save themselves. Later, several people got onto the board and entered the lake. They tried cutting it with a knife, and blood immediately flowed out. The board then sank, and several people drowned.

    Jia Bi Zhi, from Hedong, was known by his nickname Yier and was thoroughly familiar with genealogies. During the Yixi period, he served as a military officer in the Langya government. One night, he dreamed of a man with many warts on his face, a lot of beard, and a large nose with eyes like a bird's. The man said to him, "I love your appearance. I want to exchange heads, can I?" Jia replied, "Everyone has their own head and face. How can this be possible?" The next day, he dreamed again and felt very disgusted by it. So, he agreed to exchange in his dream. The next morning, he woke up without realizing, and everyone was surprised and ran away to hide, asking, "Where did that man come from?" The Prince of Langya was greatly alarmed and sent a messenger to call him over. Jia arrived at Langya and saw the man from afar returning inside. He took a mirror and looked at himself, only then realizing the strangeness. He returned home, and his family were all surprised and went inside. Women and girls ran and hid, asking, "Where did that strange man come from?" Jia sat and explained for a long time, and sent people to the government office to check and ask. Only then did they believe. Later, he could cry on one side of his face and smile on the other, and both feet, hands, and mouth each held a pen and wrote simultaneously, with all words and meanings being beautiful. This was strange, but everything else remained as before. Soon after Emperor An died, Emperor Gong ascended the throne. [59]

    晋义熙中，羌主姚略坏洛阳阴沟取砖，得一双雄鹅，并金色，交颈长鸣，声闻九皋，养之此沟。
    　　隆安初，陈郡殷氏为临湘令。县中一鬼，长三丈余，跂上屋，犹垂脚至地。殷入便来，命之。每摇屏风，动窗户，病转甚。其弟覌亦见，恒拔刀在侧，与言争。鬼语云：“勿为骂我，当打汝口破！”鬼忽隐形，打口流血。后遂喎偏，成残废人。
    　　安帝隆安初，雍州刺史高平郄恢家内，忽有一物如蜥蜴。每来辄先扣户，则便有数枚，便灭灯火，儿女大小，莫不惊惧。以白郄，不信，须臾即来。至龙安二年，郄恢与殷仲堪谋议不同，下奔京师，道路遇害，并及诸子。 [60]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [60]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 晋义熙中 - Refers to the time during the reign of Emperor Xiaowu of the Eastern Jin Dynasty, specifically the year 407 AD.
    • 羌主姚略 - Refers to Yao Lüe, the leader of the Qiang people.
    • 坏洛阳阴沟取砖 - Literally means 'demolishing the underground channels of Luoyang to take bricks,' but it is likely a metaphor for a destructive action.
    • 一双雄鹅 - Refers to a pair of male geese.
    • 并金色 - Means 'both golden in color.'
    • 交颈长鸣 - Describes the geese with their necks entwined, making a long, resonant sound.
    • 声闻九皋 - A poetic expression meaning 'the sound could be heard from a great distance,' where '九皋' (jiu gao) refers to a distant place.
    • 隆安初 - Refers to the early years of the reign of Emperor Huan of the Eastern Jin Dynasty, specifically 397 AD.
    • 陈郡殷氏 - Refers to a family from Chen Prefecture named Yin.
    • 临湘令 - Refers to the position of magistrate in Linxiang County.
    • 跂上屋 - Means 'standing on tiptoe on the roof.'
    • 摇屏风，动窗户 - Describes the ghost shaking the screens and moving the windows.
    • 拔刀在侧 - Means 'holding a knife beside himself.'
    • 勿为骂我，当打汝口破 - A statement by the ghost, meaning 'Do not curse me, or I will break your mouth.'
    • 忽隐形 - Means 'suddenly became invisible.'
    • 打口流血 - Means 'struck his mouth, causing blood to flow.'
    • 遂喎偏 - Means 'became crooked or lopsided.'
    • 成残废人 - Means 'became a disabled person.'
    • 安帝隆安初 - Refers to the early years of the reign of Emperor An of the Eastern Jin Dynasty, specifically 397 AD.
    • 雍州刺史 - Refers to the position of governor of Yongzhou.
    • 高平郄恢 - Refers to Xi Hui, a man from Gaoping.
    • 忽有一物如蜥蜴 - Means 'suddenly there was an object like a lizard.'
    • 扣户 - Means 'knocking on the door.'
    • 则便有数枚 - Means 'then several appeared.'
    • 灭灯火 - Means 'putting out the lights.'
    • 龙安二年 - Refers to the second year of the Long'an era, which is 402 AD.
    • 与殷仲堪谋议不同 - Means 'having different opinions with Yin Zhongkan.'
    • 下奔京师 - Means 'fled to the capital.'
    • 道路遇害 - Means 'encountered danger on the road.'
    • 并及诸子 - Means 'including his sons.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋义熙中 - The reign of Emperor Xiaowu of the Eastern Jin Dynasty (405-419 AD).
    • 羌主姚略 - Yao Lüe, a historical figure who was a leader of the Qiang people.
    • 洛阳 - Luoyang, an important city in Chinese history, often the capital of various dynasties.
    • 隆安初 - The early years of the reign of Emperor Huan of the Eastern Jin Dynasty (397-402 AD).
    • 陈郡殷氏 - A noble family from Chen Prefecture.
    • 临湘令 - The position of magistrate in Linxiang County.
    • 安帝隆安初 - The early years of the reign of Emperor An of the Eastern Jin Dynasty (397-402 AD).
    • 雍州刺史 - The position of governor of Yongzhou, a region in northwest China.
    • 高平郄恢 - Xi Hui, a historical figure from Gaoping.
    • 殷仲堪 - Yin Zhongkan, a historical figure who was a prominent official during the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
    • 龙安二年 - The second year of the Long'an era, a period in the Eastern Jin Dynasty.

    Proper Nouns

    • 晋义熙中 - The reign of Emperor Xiaowu of the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
    • 羌主姚略 - Yao Lüe, a historical figure.
    • 洛阳 - Luoyang, a historical city.
    • 隆安初 - The early years of the reign of Emperor Huan of the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
    • 陈郡殷氏 - A noble family from Chen Prefecture.
    • 临湘令 - The position of magistrate in Linxiang County.
    • 安帝隆安初 - The early years of the reign of Emperor An of the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
    • 雍州刺史 - The position of governor of Yongzhou.
    • 高平郄恢 - Xi Hui, a historical figure.
    • 殷仲堪 - Yin Zhongkan, a historical figure.
    • 龙安二年 - The second year of the Long'an era.

    During the reign of Emperor Xiaowu of the Eastern Jin Dynasty (407 AD), Yao Lüe, the leader of the Qiang people, demolished the underground channels of Luoyang to take bricks and found a pair of golden male geese. These geese had their necks entwined and made a long, resonant sound that could be heard from a great distance. They were kept in the same underground channel.

    In the early years of the reign of Emperor Huan of the Eastern Jin Dynasty (397 AD), a man from Chen Prefecture named Yin served as the magistrate of Linxiang County. There was a ghost in the county, more than three zhang tall, who stood on tiptoe on the roof, with his feet still touching the ground. Whenever Yin entered, the ghost would appear and command him. The ghost would shake the screens and move the windows, causing Yin's illness to worsen. His younger brother, Yin Guan, also saw the ghost and always held a knife beside him, arguing with the ghost. The ghost said, "Do not curse me, or I will break your mouth." Suddenly, the ghost became invisible and struck Yin's mouth, causing blood to flow. Later, Yin became crooked and lopsided, becoming a disabled person.

    In the early years of the reign of Emperor An of the Eastern Jin Dynasty (397 AD), Xi Hui, the governor of Yongzhou from Gaoping, suddenly saw an object like a lizard in his home. Whenever it came, it would first knock on the door, and then several would appear, putting out the lights. All the children and adults in the house were terrified. When he reported this to Xi Hui, he did not believe him, but the object appeared shortly after. In the second year of the Long'an era (402 AD), Xi Hui and Yin Zhongkan had different opinions and fled to the capital. On the road, they encountered danger and were killed, along with their sons. [60]

    晋安帝隆安初，曲阿民谢盛乘船，入湖采菱。见一蛟来向船，船回避，蛟又从其后。盛便以叉杀之，惧而还家，经年无患。至元兴中，普天亢旱，盛与同旅数人，步至湖中，见先叉在地，拾取之，云：“是我叉。”人问其故，具以实对。行数步，乃得心痛，还家一宿便死。
    　　殷仲宗以隆安初入蜀，为毛璩参军。至涪陵郡，暮宿在亭屋中。忽有一鬼，体上皆毛，于窗棂中执仲宗臂牵仲宗。大呼，左右来救之，鬼乃去。
    　　晋隆安年中，颜从尝起新屋，夜梦人语云：“君何坏我冢？”明日，床前掘除之，遂见一棺材。从便为设祭，云：“今当移好处，别作小冢。”明朝，一人诣门求通，姓朱名护。列坐，乃言云：“我居四十年，昨厚贶，相感何已！今是吉日，便可出棺矣。仆巾箱中有金镜以相助。”遂以棺头举巾箱，出金镜三双赠从。 [61]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [61]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 晋安帝隆安初 - Refers to the early years of the Long'an era during the reign of Emperor An of Jin (397-401 AD).
    • 曲阿民谢盛 - Refers to a man named Xie Sheng from Qu'a (modern-day Jurong, Jiangsu).
    • 入湖采菱 - Means 'entering the lake to pick lotus roots.'
    • 见一蛟来向船 - Means 'saw a dragon-like creature approaching the boat.'
    • 船回避 - Means 'the boat tried to avoid it.'
    • 蛟又从其后 - Means 'the dragon-like creature followed behind.'
    • 盛便以叉杀之 - Means 'Xie Sheng then killed it with a spear.'
    • 惧而还家 - Means 'fearing for his safety, he returned home.'
    • 经年无患 - Means 'for many years without any trouble.'
    • 至元兴中 - Refers to the middle years of the Yuanxing era (403-404 AD).
    • 普天亢旱 - Means 'a severe drought across the land.'
    • 盛与同旅数人 - Means 'Xie Sheng and several fellow travelers.'
    • 步至湖中 - Means 'walked to the middle of the lake.'
    • 见先叉在地 - Means 'saw the spear lying on the ground.'
    • 拾取之 - Means 'picked it up.'
    • 具以实对 - Means 'explained the truth in detail.'
    • 乃得心痛 - Means 'then suffered from a heartache.'
    • 还家一宿便死 - Means 'returned home and died the next day.'
    • 殷仲宗以隆安初入蜀 - Refers to Yin Zhongzong entering Sichuan during the early years of the Long'an era.
    • 为毛璩参军 - Means 'serving as a military officer under Mao Qu.'
    • 至涪陵郡 - Refers to reaching Fuling Prefecture.
    • 暮宿在亭屋中 - Means 'stayed overnight in a pavilion house.'
    • 忽有一鬼 - Means 'suddenly saw a ghost.'
    • 体上皆毛 - Means 'with hair all over its body.'
    • 于窗棂中执仲宗臂 - Means 'grabbed Yin Zhongzong's arm through the window lattice.'
    • 大呼，左右来救之 - Means 'shouted loudly, and those around him came to rescue him.'
    • 鬼乃去 - Means 'the ghost then left.'
    • 晋隆安年中 - Refers to the middle years of the Long'an era (397-401 AD).
    • 颜从尝起新屋 - Refers to Yan Cong building a new house.
    • 夜梦人语云 - Means 'dreamed that someone said at night.'
    • 君何坏我冢 - Means 'Why did you destroy my tomb?'
    • 明日，床前掘除之 - Means 'The next day, he dug it up in front of his bed.'
    • 遂见一棺材 - Means 'and found a coffin.'
    • 从便为设祭 - Means 'Yan Cong then set up an offering.'
    • 今当移好处，别作小冢 - Means 'Now I will move it to a better place and build a small tomb.'
    • 明朝，一人诣门求通 - Means 'The next morning, a person came to the door to request an audience.'
    • 姓朱名护 - Refers to a man named Zhu Hu.
    • 列坐，乃言云 - Means 'sat down and said.'
    • 我居四十年 - Means 'I lived here for forty years.'
    • 昨厚贶，相感何已 - Means 'Yesterday, you gave me a generous gift, how deeply I am grateful.'
    • 今是吉日，便可出棺矣 - Means 'Today is a good day, so you can take out the coffin.'
    • 仆巾箱中有金镜以相助 - Means 'In my hat box, there are three gold mirrors to help you.'
    • 遂以棺头举巾箱 - Means 'Then, he lifted the hat box with the coffin.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋安帝隆安初 - The Long'an era (397-401 AD) during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 曲阿 - A county in modern-day Jurong, Jiangsu.
    • 蛟 - A mythical dragon-like creature often associated with water.
    • 元兴中 - The Yuanxing era (403-404 AD) during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 殷仲宗 - A historical figure who served as a military officer during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 毛璩 - A prominent military leader during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 涪陵郡 - A prefecture in modern-day Fuling, Chongqing.
    • 晋隆安年中 - The middle years of the Long'an era (397-401 AD).
    • 颜从 - A historical figure who built a new house and had a supernatural experience.

    Proper Nouns

    • 晋安帝 - Emperor An of Jin.
    • 隆安 - The Long'an era (397-401 AD).
    • 曲阿 - A county in modern-day Jurong, Jiangsu.
    • 谢盛 - Xie Sheng, a man from Qu'a.
    • 元兴 - The Yuanxing era (403-404 AD).
    • 殷仲宗 - Yin Zhongzong, a historical figure.
    • 毛璩 - Mao Qu, a prominent military leader.
    • 涪陵郡 - A prefecture in modern-day Fuling, Chongqing.
    • 颜从 - Yan Cong, a historical figure.
    • 朱护 - Zhu Hu, a person who visited Yan Cong.

    During the early years of the Long'an era under Emperor An of Jin, a man named Xie Sheng from Qu'a (modern-day Jurong, Jiangsu) was rowing a boat into a lake to pick lotus roots. Suddenly, he saw a dragon-like creature approaching his boat. The boat tried to avoid it, but the creature followed behind. Xie Sheng then killed it with a spear, fearing for his safety, he returned home. For many years afterward, he had no further troubles. However, during the middle years of the Yuanxing era, a severe drought struck the land. Xie Sheng, along with several fellow travelers, walked to the middle of the lake and saw his spear lying on the ground. He picked it up and said, "This is my spear." When asked why he had it, he explained the truth in detail. After walking a few steps, he suddenly suffered from a heartache and returned home, where he died the next day.

    Yin Zhongzong entered Sichuan during the early years of the Long'an era and served as a military officer under Mao Qu. Upon reaching Fuling Prefecture, he stayed overnight in a pavilion house. Suddenly, a ghost with hair all over its body appeared and grabbed Yin Zhongzong's arm through the window lattice, pulling him. Yin shouted loudly, and those around him came to rescue him. The ghost then left.

    During the middle years of the Long'an era, Yan Cong built a new house. One night, he dreamed that someone said to him, "Why did you destroy my tomb?" The next day, he dug up the area in front of his bed and found a coffin. Yan Cong then set up an offering and said, "Now I will move it to a better place and build a small tomb." The next morning, a person named Zhu Hu came to the door to request an audience. They sat down, and Zhu Hu said, "I lived here for forty years. Yesterday, you gave me a generous gift, how deeply I am grateful! Today is a good day, so you can take out the coffin. In my hat box, there are three gold mirrors to help you." He then lifted the hat box with the coffin and gave Yan Cong three gold mirrors. [61]

    晋安帝元兴中，一人年出二十，未婚对，然目不干色，曾无秽行。尝行田，见一女甚丽，谓少年曰：“闻君自以柳李之俦，亦复有桑中之欢邪？”女便歌，少年微有动色。后复重见之，少年问姓，云：“姓苏，名琼，家在涂中。”遂要还，尽欢。从弟便突入以杖打女，即化成雌白鹄。
    　　晋元熙中，桂阳郡有一老翁，常以钓为业。后清晨出钓，遇大鱼食饵，掣纶甚急，船人奄然俱没。家人寻丧于钓所，见老翁及鱼并死，为钓纶所缠。鱼腹下有丹字，文曰：“我闻曾潭乐，故从檐潭来。磔死弊老翁，持钓数见欺。好食赤鲤鲙，今日得汝为。”
    　　孙恩作逆时，吴兴纷乱，一男子避急，突入蒋侯庙。始入门，木像弯弓射之，即死。行人及守庙者无不皆见也。 [62]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [62]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 年出二十 - Means 'over twenty years old,' indicating the age of the young man.
    • 未婚对 - Means 'unmarried' or 'without a spouse.'
    • 目不干色 - Means 'never having seen a woman' or 'remaining chaste.'
    • 曾无秽行 - Means 'never engaged in immoral actions.'
    • 桑中之欢 - A reference to the idiom '桑中之约,' meaning a secret romantic rendezvous or a private tryst.
    • 从弟 - Means 'younger brother' or 'cousin,' depending on the context.
    • 奄然俱没 - Means 'suddenly disappeared' or 'were suddenly submerged.'
    • 弊老翁 - Means 'old man' or 'elderly person,' with '弊' possibly indicating a derogatory tone.
    • 数见欺 - Means 'often deceived' or 'often tricked.'
    • 好食赤鲤鲙 - Means 'enjoys eating red carp slices.'
    • 磔死 - Means 'torn apart' or 'crucified.'
    • 纷乱 - Means 'chaos' or 'disorder.'
    • 突入 - Means 'suddenly entered' or 'rushed in.'
    • 木像弯弓射之 - Means 'the wooden statue bent its bow and shot at him.'
    • 无不皆见 - Means 'everyone saw it.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋安帝元兴中 - Refers to the reign of Emperor An of Jin during the Yuanxing period (399-401 AD).
    • 晋元熙中 - Refers to the reign of Emperor Yuan of Jin during the Yuanxi period (419-420 AD).
    • 桂阳郡 - A historical county in present-day Hunan Province, China.
    • 蒋侯庙 - A temple dedicated to Jiang Hou, a local deity or hero.
    • 孙恩作逆时 - Refers to the rebellion led by Sun En against the Eastern Jin Dynasty (399-402 AD).
    • 吴兴 - A historical county in present-day Zhejiang Province, China.

    Proper Nouns

    • 晋安帝 - Emperor An of Jin.
    • 元兴 - The reign title of Emperor An of Jin.
    • 晋元熙 - The reign title of Emperor Yuan of Jin.
    • 桂阳郡 - A historical county in present-day Hunan Province, China.
    • 蒋侯庙 - A temple dedicated to Jiang Hou.
    • 孙恩 - A rebel leader during the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
    • 吴兴 - A historical county in present-day Zhejiang Province, China.

    During the reign of Emperor An of Jin in the Yuanxing period, there was a young man over twenty years old who was unmarried and had never seen a woman, nor had he ever engaged in immoral actions. While working in the fields, he saw a beautiful woman who said to him, "Have you had a secret romantic encounter like those of Li and Liu?" The woman then sang, and the young man showed a slight reaction. Later, he met her again and asked her name. She replied, "My name is Su Qiong, and I live in the area of Tu." He invited her home, and they enjoyed themselves together. His younger brother suddenly entered and struck the woman with a stick, causing her to transform into a white goose.

    During the reign of Emperor Yuan of Jin in the Yuanxi period, an old man in Guiyang County often fished for a living. One morning, he went fishing and encountered a large fish that quickly pulled the line, causing both the old man and the fish to suddenly disappear. His family found them dead at the fishing spot, entangled by the fishing line. The fish's belly had red writing that said, "I heard of the joy in Zengtan, so I came from Yantian. I was torn apart by the old man, who often deceived me with his fishing. I enjoy eating red carp slices, and today I got you."

    During the rebellion led by Sun En, chaos erupted in Wuxing. A man sought refuge in Jiang Hou Temple. As soon as he entered, the wooden statue bent its bow and shot at him, killing him. Everyone who passed by or guarded the temple saw it happen. [62]

    诸葛长民富贵后，尝一月或数十日辄于夜眠中惊起，跳踉如与人相打状。毛修之尝与同宿，骇愕不达此意，视之良久。长民告毛：“此物奇健，非我无以制之。”毛曰：“是何物？”长民曰：“我正见一物甚黑，而手脚不分明。少日中多夕来，辄共斗，深自惊惧焉。”屋中柱及椽角间，悉见有蛇头。令人以刀悬斫，应刀隐灭，去辄复出。悉以纸裹柱桷，纸内蔌蔌如有行声。
    　　司马休之遣文武千余人迎家，达南都，值风泊船。上岸伐薪，见聚肉有数百斤，乃割取之。还以镬煮之，汤始欲热，皆変成数千虾蟆也。
    　　姚泓叔父大将军绍总司戎政，召胡僧问以休咎。僧乃以面为大胡饼形，径一丈，僧坐在上。先食正西，次食正北，次食正南，所余卷而吞之。讫便起去，了无所言。是岁五月，杨盛大破姚军于清水。九月，晋师北讨，扫定颍洛，遂席卷丰镐，生禽泓焉。 [63]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [63]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 嘗一月或數十日 - Means 'sometimes once a month or every few days,' indicating irregular intervals.
    • 夜眠中驚起 - Means 'suddenly waking up from sleep at night,' describing a startled awakening.
    • 跳踉如與人相打狀 - Describes a jumping and struggling motion as if fighting someone.
    • 深自驚惧焉 - Means 'feeling deeply frightened,' expressing intense fear.
    • 悉見有蛇頭 - Means 'all could see snake heads,' describing a visual phenomenon.
    • 令人以刀懸斫 - Means 'having someone cut with a sword,' describing an action taken to address the issue.
    • 應刀隱滅 - Means 'disappearing when cut,' describing the immediate effect of the action.
    • 去輒復出 - Means 'appearing again after disappearing,' indicating a recurring issue.
    • 悉以紙裹柱桷 - Means 'wrapping all the columns and rafters with paper,' describing a protective measure.
    • 紙內蔌蔌如有行聲 - Describes a rustling sound inside the paper, suggesting movement.
    • 遣文武千余人迎家 - Means 'sending over a thousand soldiers and officials to escort his family,' indicating a significant escort.
    • 達南都 - Means 'reaching the southern capital,' specifying the destination.
    • 值風泊船 - Means 'encountering a storm and anchoring the boat,' describing a specific event.
    • 上岸伐薪 - Means 'going ashore to cut firewood,' describing an activity.
    • 見聚肉有數百斤 - Means 'seeing a pile of meat weighing hundreds of pounds,' describing a discovery.
    • 還以镬煮之 - Means 'returning to cook it in a large pot,' describing an action.
    • 湯始欲熱 - Means 'the water just beginning to heat up,' describing the initial stage of cooking.
    • 皆變成数千虾蟆也 - Means 'all turning into thousands of frogs,' describing a transformation.
    • 總司戎政 - Means 'overseeing military affairs,' describing a high-ranking position.
    • 召胡僧問以休咎 - Means 'calling a foreign monk to ask about good and bad omens,' describing a consultation.
    • 僧乃以面為大胡饼形 - Means 'the monk then made a large flatbread shape,' describing an action.
    • 徑一丈 - Means 'one zhang in diameter,' specifying the size.
    • 先食正西，次食正北，次食正南 - Means 'eating first the west, then the north, then the south,' describing a sequence of actions.
    • 所余卷而吞之 - Means 'wrapping the remaining part and swallowing it,' describing a final action.
    • 是岁五月 - Means 'in the fifth month of that year,' specifying a time.
    • 清水 - Refers to a specific location.
    • 九月 - Specifies a time.
    • 晋师北讨 - Means 'the Jin army marching north to attack,' describing a military action.
    • 扫定颍洛 - Means 'cleansing and securing Ying and Luo,' describing a military success.
    • 遂席卷丰镐 - Means 'thus sweeping through Feng and Hao,' describing a rapid conquest.
    • 生禽泓焉 - Means 'capturing Yao Hong alive,' describing a military achievement.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 诸葛长民 - A historical figure known for his role in the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 毛修之 - A historical figure who was a close associate of Zhuge Changmin.
    • 司马休之 - A historical figure who was a military general and politician.
    • 姚泓 - A historical figure, the last emperor of the Later Qin dynasty.
    • 大将军绍 - A high-ranking military official.
    • 胡僧 - Refers to a foreign monk, likely from Central Asia.
    • 休咎 - Refers to good and bad omens, often consulted in divination.
    • 清水 - A specific location in Chinese history, possibly a river or a region.
    • 颍洛 - Refers to the regions of Ying and Luo, important areas in Chinese history.
    • 丰镐 - Refers to the ancient capitals of the Western Zhou dynasty.
    • 晋师 - Refers to the military forces of the Jin dynasty.
    • 北讨 - Refers to a northern campaign, often associated with military expeditions.
    • 扫定 - Refers to the act of cleansing and securing a region.
    • 生禽 - Refers to capturing someone alive.

    Proper Nouns

    • 诸葛长民 - A historical figure.
    • 毛修之 - A historical figure.
    • 司马休之 - A historical figure.
    • 姚泓 - A historical figure.
    • 大将军绍 - A high-ranking military official.
    • 清水 - A specific location.
    • 颍洛 - Specific regions.
    • 丰镐 - Ancient capitals.
    • 晋师 - Military forces of the Jin dynasty.
    • 杨盛 - A historical figure.
    • 姚军 - Military forces of the Later Qin dynasty.

    After Zhuge Changmin became wealthy and powerful, he would sometimes wake up suddenly during the night, jumping and struggling as if fighting with someone. Mao Xiu, who had once shared a bed with him, was startled and did not understand what was happening. He watched for a long time. Zhuge told Mao, "This thing is unusually strong, and without me, I cannot control it." Mao asked, "What is this thing?" Zhuge replied, "I see a very dark object, and its limbs are not clearly defined. It appears more often in the evening, and we often fight, which deeply frightens me." Inside the house, on the columns and rafters, everyone could see snake heads. Someone was instructed to cut at them with a sword, and they would disappear when cut but reappear shortly after. They wrapped all the columns and rafters with paper, and inside the paper, there was a rustling sound as if something was moving.

    During the reign of Sima Xiuzhi, he sent over a thousand soldiers and officials to escort his family to the southern capital. They encountered a storm and anchored their boat. Going ashore to cut firewood, they saw a pile of meat weighing hundreds of pounds and cut it up. Returning to cook it in a large pot, the water just began to heat up, and it all turned into thousands of frogs.

    Yao Hong's uncle, the Grand General Shao, who oversaw military affairs, called a foreign monk to ask about good and bad omens.
