The monk then made a large flatbread shape, one zhang in diameter, and sat on it. He first ate the west, then the north, then the south, and finally wrapped the remaining part and swallowed it. After finishing, he got up and left, saying nothing. In the fifth month of that year, Yang Sheng decisively defeated Yao Hong's army at Qing Shui. In September, the Jin army marched north to attack, cleansing and securing Ying and Luo, and thus sweeping through Feng and Hao, capturing Yao Hong alive. [63]

    安定人姓韦，北伐姚泓之时，归国至都，住亲知家。时□□扰乱，齐有客来问之，韦云：“今虽免虑，而体气惙然，未有气力。思作一羹，尤莫能得，至凄苦。”夜中眠熟，忽有扣床而来告者云：“官与君钱。”便惊，出户，见一千钱在外。又见一乌纱冠帻子执板背户而立，呼主人共视，比来已不复见，而取钱用之。
    　　晋末黄祖，奉亲至孝。母病笃，庭中稽颡。俄顷，天汉开明，有一老公，将小儿，持箱自通。即以两丸药赐母服之，众患顿消。因停宿。夜中厅事上有五色气际天，琴歌清好。祖往视之，坐斗帐里，四角及顶上各有一大珠，形如鹅子，明彩炫耀。翁曰：“汝入三月，可泛河而来。”依期行，见门题曰“善福门”，内有水曰“湎源池”，有芙蕖如车轮。 [64]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [64]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 安定人姓韋 - Classical way of introducing a person by their place of origin and surname.
    • 北伐姚泓 - Refers to a military campaign against Yao Hong, a historical event.
    • 歸國至都 - Means 'returned to the capital after the campaign.'
    • 住親知家 - Means 'stayed at the home of a close acquaintance.'
    • 時□□擾亂 - The text is incomplete here, but it likely refers to a period of chaos or unrest.
    • 思作一羹 - Means 'thought of making a soup,' indicating a desire for a simple meal.
    • 尤莫能得 - Means 'especially could not obtain,' emphasizing the difficulty.
    • 至凄苦 - Means 'extremely miserable,' describing the emotional state.
    • 夜中眠熟 - Means 'slept soundly at night.'
    • 忽有扣床而来告者 - Means 'suddenly someone knocked on the bed and reported.'
    • 官与君钱 - Means 'the official gave you money.'
    • 便惊 - Means 'became startled.'
    • 出户 - Means 'went outside.'
    • 见一千钱在外 - Means 'saw one thousand coins outside.'
    • 乌纱冠帻子执板背户而立 - Describes a figure wearing a black cap and a headband, holding a tablet, standing behind the door.
    • 呼主人共视 - Means 'called the host to see together.'
    • 比来已不复见 - Means 'by the time they came, it had already disappeared.'
    • 取钱用之 - Means 'took the money and used it.'
    • 晋末黄祖 - Refers to Huang Zu at the end of the Jin Dynasty.
    • 奉亲至孝 - Means 'showed filial piety to his parents.'
    • 母病笃 - Means 'mother was gravely ill.'
    • 庭中稽颡 - Means 'kneeled and bowed in the courtyard.'
    • 俄顷 - Means 'in a short while.'
    • 天汉开明 - Means 'the Milky Way became clear.'
    • 老公 - Means 'an old man.'
    • 小儿 - Means 'a child.'
    • 持箱自通 - Means 'carried a box and came to introduce himself.'
    • 即以两丸药赐母服之 - Means 'gave two pills to the mother to take.'
    • 众患顿消 - Means 'all ailments disappeared instantly.'
    • 因停宿 - Means 'stayed overnight.'
    • 夜中厅事上有五色气际天 - Means 'at night, there was a colorful mist reaching up to the sky in the hall.'
    • 琴歌清好 - Means 'the music and songs were clear and pleasant.'
    • 祖往视之 - Means 'Huang Zu went to look at it.'
    • 坐斗帐里 - Means 'sat inside a small tent.'
    • 四角及顶上各有一大珠 - Means 'there was a large pearl at each corner and on the top.'
    • 形如鹅子 - Means 'shaped like a gosling.'
    • 明彩炫耀 - Means 'shining brightly and dazzling.'
    • 翁曰 - Means 'the old man said.'
    • 汝入三月，可泛河而来 - Means 'you can come by boat in three months.'
    • 依期行 - Means 'followed the appointed time.'
    • 见门题曰“善福门” - Means 'saw a gate with the inscription 'Good Fortune Gate.'
    • 内有水曰“湎源池” - Means 'inside, there was a pool called 'Mourning Source Pool.'
    • 有芙蕖如车轮 - Means 'there were lotus flowers as large as carriage wheels.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 安定 - A historical region in China, now part of Gansu Province.
    • 姚泓 - Yao Hong, a ruler of the Later Qin dynasty.
    • 北伐 - Refers to a northern campaign, typically military.
    • 晋末 - Refers to the late Jin Dynasty, a period of political instability.
    • 黄祖 - Huang Zu, a figure from the late Jin Dynasty, known for his filial piety.
    • 奉亲至孝 - A Confucian virtue emphasizing respect and care for one's parents.
    • 天汉开明 - A poetic description of the Milky Way becoming visible, often used in classical literature to symbolize divine intervention.
    • 琴歌清好 - Refers to the harmonious and pleasant sounds of music and singing, often associated with spiritual or supernatural experiences.

    Proper Nouns

    • 安定 - A historical region.
    • 姚泓 - A historical figure.
    • 晋末 - A historical period.
    • 黄祖 - A historical figure.
    • 善福门 - A gate with the inscription 'Good Fortune Gate.'
    • 湎源池 - A pool with the name 'Mourning Source Pool.'

    A man from Anding, surnamed Wei, returned to the capital after participating in the northern campaign against Yao Hong. He stayed at the home of a close acquaintance. During a period of unrest, a guest from Qi came to visit him. Wei said, "Although I have escaped the troubles, I feel weak and lack the strength. I thought of making a soup, but could not even manage that, to the point of great misery." One night, while he was sleeping soundly, he suddenly heard someone knocking on his bed and announcing, "The official has given you money." Wei was startled and went outside, where he saw one thousand coins lying there. He also saw a figure wearing a black cap and a headband, holding a tablet and standing behind the door. He called the host over to see, but by the time they arrived, the figure had already disappeared, and Wei took the money and used it.

    At the end of the Jin Dynasty, there was Huang Zu, who showed great filial piety to his parents. His mother was gravely ill, and he knelt and bowed in the courtyard. Shortly after, the Milky Way became clear, and an old man appeared with a child, carrying a box and introducing himself. He gave two pills to Huang Zu's mother to take, and all her ailments disappeared instantly. They stayed overnight. That night, in the hall, there was a colorful mist reaching up to the sky, accompanied by clear and pleasant music and singing. Huang Zu went to look at it and saw the old man sitting inside a small tent, with a large pearl at each corner and on the top, shaped like a gosling, shining brightly and dazzling. The old man said, "You can come by boat in three months." Huang Zu followed the appointed time and arrived at a gate with the inscription "Good Fortune Gate." Inside, there was a pool called "Mourning Source Pool," with lotus flowers as large as carriage wheels. [64]

    晋临川太守谢摛，夜中闻鼓吹声。兄藻曰：“夜者阴间，不及存，将在身后。”及死，赠长水校尉，加鼓吹。
    　　晋兖州刺史沛国宋处宗，尝买一长鸣鸡，爱养甚至，恒笼着窗间。鸡遂作人语，与处宗谈论，极有言致，终日不辍。处宗因此言功大进。
    　　晋王文度镇广陵，忽见二驺，持鹄头板来召之。王大惊问驺：“我作何官？”驺云：“召作平北将军、徐兖二州刺史。”王曰：““我已作此官，何故复召邪？”鬼云：“此人间耳，今所作是天上官也。”王大惧之。寻见迎官玄衣人及鹄衣小吏甚多。王寻病薨。 [65]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [65]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 夜者阴间，不及存，将在身后 - Cultural belief that the night is the realm of the dead, not reaching the living world, it will be after death.
    • 鸡遂作人语，与处宗谈论，极有言致，终日不辍 - Supernatural event where the rooster speaks human language and has meaningful conversations with Chuzong all day long.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋临川太守 - Office of governor of Linchuan in the Jin dynasty.
    • 晋兖州刺史 - Office of governor of Yan Prefecture in the Jin dynasty.
    • 晋王文度 - Historical figure Wang Wende, a governor in the Jin dynasty.
    • 广陵 - City of Guangling in the Jin dynasty.
    • 长水校尉 - Military rank in the Jin dynasty.
    • 鼓吹 - Musical honor in ancient China.
    • 长鸣鸡 - Rooster that crows loudly.
    • 平北将军 - Military rank in the Jin dynasty.
    • 徐兖二州刺史 - Offices of governor of Xuzhou and Yan Prefectures in the Jin dynasty.
    • 玄衣人 - Officials in black robes, often associated with funerals or supernatural beings.
    • 鹄衣小吏 - White-robed attendants, often associated with supernatural beings.

    Proper Nouns

    • 谢摛 - Historical figure Xie Chi.
    • 谢藻 - Elder brother of Xie Chi, Zhao.
    • 宋处宗 - Historical figure Song Chuzong.
    • 王文度 - Historical figure Wang Wende.

    Xie Chi, the governor of Linchuan in the Jin dynasty, heard the sound of drums and bugles at night. His elder brother Zhao said, "The night is the realm of the dead, not reaching the living world, it will be after death." Upon his death, he was posthumously granted the position of Changshui Xiaowei, with the addition of drums and bugles.

    Song Chuzong, the governor of Yan Prefecture in Pei State during the Jin dynasty, once bought a rooster that crowed loudly. He loved and cared for it very much, always keeping it in a cage by the window. The rooster then spoke human language and conversed with Chuzong, having very meaningful conversations all day long without stopping. Chuzong's eloquence greatly improved because of this.

    Wang Wende, the governor of Guangling in the Jin dynasty, suddenly saw two attendants holding white-headed boards and came to summon him. Wang was greatly surprised and asked the attendant, "What office am I holding?" The attendant said, "Summoned to be the General of the North and Governor of Xuzhou and Yan Prefectures." Wang said, "I have already held this office, why am I being summoned again?" The ghost said, "This is just in the human world, what I am now being appointed to is a heavenly office." Wang was greatly afraid. Soon, he saw many officials in black robes and white-robed attendants coming to greet him. Wang soon fell ill and died. [65]

    晋庐陵太守庐企，字子及。上祖坐事系狱，而非其罪。见蝼蛄行其左右，相谓曰：“使尔有神，能活我死，不当善乎？”因投饭与蝼蛄，食尽去。有顷复来，形体稍大，意异之。复与食，数日间其大如豚。及当行刑，蝼蛄掘壁根，为大孔，破，得从此孔出亡。后遇赦得活。
    　　晋秘书监太原温敬林亡一年，妇柏氏，忽见林还，共寝处，不肯见子弟。兄子来见林，林小开窗出面见之。后酒醉形露，是邻家老黄狗，乃打杀之。
    　　王仲文为河南主簿，居缑氏县。夜归，道经大泽中。顾车后有一白狗，甚可爱，便欲呼取。忽変为人形，长五六尺，状似方相，或前或却，如欲上车。仲文大怖，走至舍，捉火来视，便失所在。月余日，仲文将奴共在路，忽复见，与奴并顿伏，俱死。 [66]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [66]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 坐事 - Means 'convicted of a crime' or 'imprisoned for a reason,' often implying wrongful imprisonment.
    • 見蝼蛄行其左右 - Literally 'seeing a roach moving on his left and right,' where '蝼蛄' (roach) is used metaphorically to refer to a small creature.
    • 使尔有神，能活我死，不当善乎 - A rhetorical question expressing a wish for the creature to have the power to save him if it had divine abilities.
    • 因投饭与蝼蛄 - Means 'therefore he threw food to the roach,' indicating an act of kindness or desperation.
    • 形体稍大 - Means 'the body gradually grew larger,' suggesting the roach's transformation.
    • 意异之 - Means 'he was surprised by it,' indicating a change in perception or attitude.
    • 复与食 - Means 'he fed it again,' showing continued interaction.
    • 大如豚 - Means 'as big as a pig,' describing the roach's growth.
    • 当行刑 - Means 'when he was about to be executed,' setting the context for the miraculous escape.
    • 掘壁根，为大孔，破，得从此孔出亡 - Means 'dug a large hole in the wall, broke through, and escaped through this hole,' describing the roach's escape.
    • 后遇赦得活 - Means 'later he was pardoned and survived,' indicating the outcome of the events.
    • 亡一年 - Means 'one year after his death,' setting the timeline for the second story.
    • 忽见林还 - Means 'suddenly she saw Lin return,' indicating a supernatural event.
    • 共寝处，不肯见子弟 - Means 'they slept together, but she refused to see her children,' suggesting a mysterious or ghostly presence.
    • 兄子来见林，林小开窗出面见之 - Means 'her nephew came to see Lin, and Lin opened a small window to show his face,' indicating a cautious appearance.
    • 后酒醉形露，是邻家老黄狗，乃打杀之 - Means 'later, when he was drunk, his true form was revealed as a neighbor's old yellow dog, and he was beaten to death,' describing the revelation and its consequence.
    • 夜归，道经大泽中 - Means 'on his way home at night, he passed through a large marsh,' setting the scene for the encounter.
    • 顾车后有一白狗，甚可爱 - Means 'he looked back and saw a white dog behind his carriage, which was very cute,' describing the initial encounter.
    • 忽变为人形，长五六尺，状似方相，或前或却，如欲上车 - Means 'suddenly it changed into a human form, about five or six feet tall, resembling a spirit, moving forward and backward, as if wanting to get on the carriage,' describing the transformation.
    • 仲文大怖，走至舍，捉火来视，便失所在 - Means 'Zhong Wen was greatly frightened, ran to his house, took a torch to look, and then it disappeared,' describing the reaction and the disappearance.
    • 月余日，仲文将奴共在路，忽复见，与奴并顿伏，俱死 - Means 'after more than a month, Zhong Wen and his servant were on the road, suddenly saw it again, and both fell down and died,' describing the final encounter and its tragic outcome.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋庐陵太守庐企，字子及 - Refers to a historical figure, Lu Qi, who served as the Governor of Lu陵 during the Jin Dynasty.
    • 晋秘书监太原温敬林 - Refers to Wen Jinglin, a high-ranking official in the Jin Dynasty, who held the position of Secretary of the Imperial Library.
    • 王仲文为河南主簿 - Refers to Wang Zhongwen, a minor official serving as a clerk in the government of Henan during the Jin Dynasty.

    Proper Nouns

    • 晋庐陵太守庐企 - The title and name of a historical figure.
    • 晋秘书监太原温敬林 - The title and name of a historical figure.
    • 王仲文 - The name of a historical figure.

    During the Jin Dynasty, Governor Lu Qi of Lu陵, whose courtesy name was Ziji, had an ancestor who was imprisoned for a crime, though it was not his fault. Seeing a roach moving on his left and right, he said to himself, "If you have divine power, and can save me from death, would it not be good?" He then threw food to the roach, which ate it all and left. After a while, it returned, and its body gradually grew larger. He was surprised by this and fed it again. Within a few days, it grew as big as a pig. When he was about to be executed, the roach dug a large hole in the wall, broke through, and he escaped through this hole. Later, he was pardoned and survived.

    One year after the death of Wen Jinglin, the Secretary of the Imperial Library from Taiyuan, his wife Bai suddenly saw Lin return. They slept together, but she refused to see her children. Her nephew came to see Lin, and Lin opened a small window to show his face. Later, when he was drunk, his true form was revealed as a neighbor's old yellow dog, and he was beaten to death.

    Wang Zhongwen, a clerk in the government of Henan, lived in Gou county. On his way home at night, he passed through a large marsh. Looking back, he saw a white dog behind his carriage, which was very cute, and he wanted to call it. Suddenly, it changed into a human form, about five or six feet tall, resembling a spirit, moving forward and backward, as if wanting to get on the carriage. Zhong Wen was greatly frightened, ran to his house, took a torch to look, and then it disappeared. After more than a month, Zhong Wen and his servant were on the road, suddenly saw it again, and both fell down and died. [66]

    颍川陈庆孙家后有神树，多就求福，遂起庙，名天神庙庆孙有乌牛，神于空中言：“我是天神，乐卿此牛。若不与我，来月二十日当杀尔儿。”庆孙曰：“人生有命，命不由汝。”至日，儿果死。复言：“汝不与我，至五月杀汝妇。”又不与。至时妇果死。又来言：“汝不与我，秋当杀汝。”又不与。至秋遂不死。鬼乃来谢曰：“君为人心正，方受大福。愿莫道此事，天地闻之，我罪不细。实见小鬼，得作司命度事干，见君妇儿终期，为此欺君索食耳，愿深恕亮。君禄籍年八十三，家方如意，鬼神佑助，吾亦当奴仆相事。”遂闻稽颡声。
    　　毕修之外祖母郭氏，尝夜独寝，唤婢，应而不至，郭屡唤犹尔。后闻塌床声甚重，郭厉声呵婢，又应诺诺不至。俄见屏风上有一面，如方相。两目如升，光明一屋，手掌如簸箕，指长数寸，又挺动其耳目。郭氏道精进，一心至念，此物乃去。久之，婢辈悉来，云：“向欲应，如有物镇压之者。体轻便来。” [67]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [67]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 夫文章者，原出五經 - Formal thesis statement meaning that literary writing has its roots in the Five Classics.
    • 生於書者也 - Means 'arise from the *Shu* (Book of Documents).' Here, *書* refers to the *Shujing* (Book of Documents), one of the Five Classics.
    • 陶冶性靈 - Metaphor of smelting and shaping one's inner nature.
    • 從容諷諫 - Means to offer remonstrance in an indirect, measured, and graceful way.
    • 行有餘力，則可習之 - Means that one may study literary writing after fulfilling more basic duties.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 五經 - Refers to the Five Classics: the *Shijing* (Book of Poetry), *Shujing* (Book of Documents), *Yijing* (Book of Changes), *Liji* (Book of Rites), and *Chunqiu* (Spring and Autumn Annals).
    • 書，易，詩，禮，春秋 - Titles of the classics that the passage treats as sources for distinct literary forms.

    A Divine Tree in Yenchuan

    In the courtyard of Chen Qingsun's family in Yenchuan, there was a divine tree, and many people sought blessings from it. As a result, a temple was built, named the "Temple of the Heavenly Deity." Chen Qingsun had a black ox, and a deity spoke from the sky, saying, "I am the Heavenly Deity, and I am fond of your ox. If you do not give it to me, on the twentieth day of next month, I will kill your son." Chen Qingsun replied, "Human life is determined by fate, and fate is not in your hands." On that day, his son indeed died. The deity then said, "If you do not give me the ox, I will kill your wife in May." Chen Qingsun did not give it. When May arrived, his wife indeed died. The deity came again and said, "If you do not give me the ox, I will kill you in autumn." Chen Qingsun still did not give it. By autumn, he was not killed. The ghost then came to apologize, saying, "Your heart is upright, and you are about to receive great blessings. I ask you not to speak of this matter. If the heavens and earth hear it, my sin will be great. I have indeed seen a small ghost, and I have been appointed as the Keeper of Fate. I saw the end of your wife and son, and I lied to you to seek food. I hope you will deeply forgive me. Your lifespan is recorded as eighty-three years, and your family will soon be in harmony. The ghosts and spirits will assist you, and I will serve you as a servant." Then he heard the sound of prostration.

    Guo Shi, the maternal grandmother of Bi Xiu, once slept alone at night and called her maid, but the maid did not respond. Guo Shi called her repeatedly, but the maid still did not respond. Later, she heard a heavy thudding sound on the bed. Guo Shi scolded the maid loudly, but the maid still did not respond. Soon, she saw a face on the screen that looked like a square-faced figure. Its eyes were as large as a winnowing basket, illuminating the entire room, and its hands were as large as a winnowing basket, with fingers several inches long. Its ears and eyes also moved. Guo Shi's deep concentration and sincere devotion caused the face to disappear. After a long time, the maids all came, saying, "We wanted to respond, but there was an invisible force pressing us down. We could only come when we felt lighter." [67]

    桓邈为汝南郡人，赍四乌鸭作礼。大儿梦四乌衣人请命，觉，忽见鸭将杀，遂救之，买肉以代，还梦，四人来谢而去。
    　　桓恭为桓安民参军，在丹徒所住廨。床前一小陷穴，详视是古墓，棺已朽坏。桓食，常先以鲑饭投穴中，如此经年。后眠始觉，见一人在床前，云：“我终没以来，七百余年，后绝嗣灭，烝尝莫継。君恒食见播及，感德无已。依君籍，当应为宁州刺史。”后果如言。
    　　庾宏为竟陵王府佐，家在江陵。宏令奴无患者载米饷家，未达三里，遭劫被杀，尸流泊查口村。时岸旁有文欣者，母病，医云：“须得髑髅屑，服之即差。”欣重赏募索。有邻妇杨氏，见无患尸，因断头与欣。欣烧之，欲去皮肉，经三日夜不焦，眼角张转。欣虽异之，犹惜不弃。因刮耳颊骨与母服之，即觉骨停喉中，经七日而卒。寻而杨氏得疾，通身洪肿，形如牛马，见无患头来骂云：“善恶之报，其能免乎？”杨氏以语儿，言终而卒。 [68]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [68]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 赍四乌鸭作礼 - Carrying four black ducks as a gift; a specific act of offering.
    • 大儿梦四乌衣人请命 - The eldest son dreamed of four black-cloaked people asking for his help; a dream involving supernatural beings.
    • 觉，忽见鸭将杀，遂救之，买肉以代 - Upon waking, he suddenly saw the ducks about to be killed, so he rescued them and bought meat to substitute; an act of compassion and substitution.
    • 还梦，四人来谢而去 - He had another dream, and the four people came to thank him and then left; a follow-up dream indicating gratitude.
    • 桓恭为桓安民参军 - Huan Gong served as a military officer under Huan Anmin; a specific official position.
    • 床前一小陷穴 - A small hole in front of the bed; a physical description of a location.
    • 详视是古墓，棺已朽坏 - Upon careful inspection, it was an ancient tomb, and the coffin was already decayed; a discovery of an ancient burial site.
    • 桓食，常先以鲑饭投穴中 - Huan Gong often threw cooked rice into the hole before eating; a ritual act of offering.
    • 依君籍，当应为宁州刺史 - According to your family records, you should become the governor of Ningzhou; a prediction based on lineage.
    • 庾宏为竟陵王府佐 - Yu Hong served as an assistant to the Prince of Jingling; a specific official position.
    • 家在江陵 - His home is in Jiangling; a place of residence.
    • 令奴无患者载米饷家 - He ordered his servant, who was healthy, to carry rice to his family; a specific task given to a servant.
    • 遭劫被杀，尸流泊查口村 - He was robbed and killed, and his body floated to the village of Zha Kou; a tragic event.
    • 时岸旁有文欣者，母病，医云：“须得髑髅屑，服之即差。” - At the time, there was a man named Wen Xin by the riverbank whose mother was ill, and the doctor said, 'You need some bone powder from a skull to cure her.'; a request for a specific medical ingredient.
    • 欣重赏募索 - Wen Xin offered a reward to find the ingredient; a method of seeking help.
    • 有邻妇杨氏，见无患尸，因断头与欣 - A neighbor's wife named Yang saw the body of the healthy servant and cut off his head to give to Wen Xin; an act of providing the needed ingredient.
    • 欣烧之，欲去皮肉，经三日夜不焦，眼角张转 - Wen Xin burned it, trying to remove the flesh, but after three days and nights, it did not burn, and the eyes opened and turned; a supernatural reaction to the burning.
    • 杨氏以语儿，言终而卒 - Yang told her children, and she died after finishing speaking; a final act of communication before death.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 桓邈 - A historical figure.
    • 桓安民 - A historical figure.
    • 桓恭 - A historical figure.
    • 庾宏 - A historical figure.
    • 竟陵王 - A title of a prince during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 宁州 - A historical administrative region in China.
    • 江陵 - A historical city in China.
    • 查口村 - A specific village.

    Proper Nouns

    • 桓邈 - A historical figure.
    • 桓安民 - A historical figure.
    • 桓恭 - A historical figure.
    • 庾宏 - A historical figure.
    • 竟陵王 - A title of a prince during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 宁州 - A historical administrative region in China.
    • 江陵 - A historical city in China.
    • 查口村 - A specific village.

    Huan Miao was a native of Ruyan County, and he carried four black ducks as a gift. His eldest son dreamed of four black-cloaked people asking for his help. Upon waking, he suddenly saw the ducks about to be killed and rescued them, buying meat to substitute. He had another dream, and the four people came to thank him and then left.

    Huan Gong served as a military officer under Huan Anmin in the residence in Dantu. In front of his bed, there was a small hole. Upon careful inspection, it was an ancient tomb, and the coffin was already decayed. Huan Gong often threw cooked rice into the hole before eating, doing so for many years. One day, after sleeping and waking up, he saw a person standing in front of his bed, who said, "Since my death, over seven hundred years have passed, and my descendants have died out, with no one to continue the ancestral rites. You have always shared your food with me, and I am deeply grateful. According to your family records, you should become the governor of Ningzhou." Later, he indeed became the governor of Ningzhou as predicted.

    Yu Hong served as an assistant to the Prince of Jingling, and his home was in Jiangling. He ordered his healthy servant to carry rice to his family. The servant had not yet reached three li when he was robbed and killed, and his body floated to the village of Zha Kou. At the time, there was a man named Wen Xin by the riverbank whose mother was ill, and the doctor said, "You need some bone powder from a skull to cure her." Wen Xin offered a reward to find the ingredient. A neighbor's wife named Yang saw the body of the healthy servant and cut off his head to give to Wen Xin. Wen Xin burned it, trying to remove the flesh, but after three days and nights, it did not burn, and the eyes opened and turned. Wen Xin was surprised but still cherished the head and did not discard it. He scraped the bones from the ears and cheeks and gave them to his mother to eat. She immediately felt the bones stuck in her throat and died after seven days. Soon after, Yang fell ill, her body swelling like that of a cow or horse, and she saw the head of the healthy servant come to scold her, saying, "The retribution for good and evil, how can it be avoided?" Yang told her children, and she died after finishing speaking. [68]

    阳羡县小吏吴龛，有主人在溪南。尝以一日乘掘头舟过水，溪内忽见一五色浮石。取内床头，至夜化成一女子，自称是河伯女。
    　　河南人赵良，与其乡人诸生至长安。及新安界，遭霖雨，粮乏，相谓曰：“尔当正饥，那得美食邪？”在后堂应时羹饭备具，两人惊愕，不敢食。有人声曰：“但食无嫌也。”明日早，两人复曰：“那复得美食？”即复在前。遂至长安，无他祸福。
    　　成彪兄丧，哀悼结气，昼夜哭泣。兄提二升酒一盘梨就之，引酌相欢。彪问略答，彪悲咽问：“兄今在天上，福多苦多？”久弗应，肃然无言。泻余酒着瓯中，挈罂而去。后钓于湖，经所共饮处，释纶悲感。有大鱼跳入船中，俯视诸小鱼。彪仰天号恸，俯而见之，悉放诸小鱼，大者便自出船去。 [69]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [69]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 掘头舟 - A type of boat, possibly a small or simple vessel, used in this context to describe the mode of transportation.
    • 化成一女子 - Literally means 'transformed into a woman,' referring to the magical transformation of the stone.
    • 自称是河伯女 - Literally means 'she herself called herself the daughter of the River God,' indicating the identity of the transformed being.
    • 霖雨 - Refers to continuous rain, often used to describe heavy or prolonged rainfall.
    • 正饥 - Literally means 'exactly hungry,' emphasizing the precise state of hunger.
    • 那得美食邪 - Literally means 'how can we get good food?' expressing a rhetorical question about the availability of food.
    • 尔当正饥 - Literally means 'you will be exactly hungry,' reinforcing the idea of precise hunger.
    • 但食无嫌也 - Literally means 'just eat without any hesitation,' encouraging immediate consumption.
    • 提二升酒一盘梨 - Literally means 'carried two liters of wine and a tray of pears,' describing the items brought by the brother.
    • 引酌相欢 - Literally means 'poured and drank together in joy,' describing a shared drinking experience.
    • 略答 - Literally means 'briefly answered,' indicating a minimal response.
    • 兄今在天上，福多苦多 - Literally means 'brother, are you now in heaven, more blessed or more miserable?' expressing a question about the brother's current state.
    • 久弗应 - Literally means 'did not respond for a long time,' indicating a prolonged silence.
    • 泻余酒着瓯中 - Literally means 'poured the remaining wine into a bowl,' describing the action of pouring leftover wine.
    • 挈罂而去 - Literally means 'carried the jar and left,' describing the departure with the jar.
    • 钓于湖 - Literally means 'fishing in the lake,' describing the activity of fishing.
    • 释纶悲感 - Literally means 'released the line with sorrow and emotion,' describing the act of letting go of the fishing line while feeling sad.
    • 俯视诸小鱼 - Literally means 'looked down at the many small fish,' describing the act of looking at the fish.
    • 悉放诸小鱼 - Literally means 'released all the small fish,' describing the act of releasing the fish.
    • 大者便自出船去 - Literally means 'the large ones then left the boat on their own,' describing the departure of the larger fish.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 河伯女 - Refers to the daughter of the River God, a figure from Chinese mythology associated with rivers and water.
    • 新安界 - Refers to the boundary of Xin'an, a region in ancient China.
    • 长安 - Refers to Chang'an, the capital city of ancient China during various dynasties.
    • 天上 - Refers to heaven, a common term in Chinese literature and mythology.

    Yangxia County clerk Wu Kan had a host living by the southern bank of a stream. One day, he was traveling by a small boat when he suddenly saw a colorful floating stone in the stream. He picked it up and placed it beside his bed. By night, the stone transformed into a woman who introduced herself as the daughter of the River God.

    A man from Henan named Zhao Liang, along with his fellow townsman and scholars, traveled to Chang'an. When they reached the Xin'an boundary, they encountered continuous rain and were short on food. They said to each other, "You will be exactly hungry, how can we get good food?" Suddenly, in the back room, a feast of soup and rice was prepared. The two men were astonished and hesitant to eat. A voice said, "Just eat without any hesitation." The next morning, the two men again asked, "Will we get more good food?" And indeed, more food was present. They continued on to Chang'an, and no other misfortunes befell them.

    Cheng Biao's brother had passed away, and Biao was mourning so deeply that he cried day and night. His brother brought two liters of wine and a tray of pears to him, and they drank together in joy. Biao asked his brother a few questions and then inquired, "Brother, are you now in heaven, more blessed or more miserable?" His brother did not respond for a long time, remaining solemn and silent. He poured the remaining wine into a bowl and left with the jar. Later, while fishing in a lake, passing by the place where they had drunk together, Biao released his fishing line with sorrow and emotion. A large fish jumped into the boat, and he looked down at the many small fish. Biao cried out to the heavens, then looked down and released all the small fish. The larger fish then left the boat on their own. [69]

    东平吕球，丰财美貌。乘船至曲阿湖，值风不得行，泊菰际。见一少女，乘船采菱，举体皆衣荷叶。因问：“姑非鬼邪？衣服何至如此？”女则有惧色，答云：“子不闻‘荷衣兮蕙带，倏而来兮忽而逝’乎？”然有惧容，回舟理棹，逡巡而去。球遥射之，即获一獭，向者之船，皆是苹蘩薀藻之叶。见老母立岸侧，如有所候，望见船过，因问云：“君向来不见湖中采菱女子邪？”球云：“近在右。”寻射，复获老獭。居湖次者咸云：“湖中常有采菱女，容色过人，有时至人家，结好者甚众。”
    　　河东常丑奴寓居章安县，以采蒲为业。将一小儿，湖边拔蒲，暮，恒宿空田舍中。时日向暝，见一女子，容姿殊美，乘一小船，载莼径前，投丑奴舍寄住。丑奴嘲之，灭火共卧，觉有腥气，又指甚短，惕然疑是魅。女已知人意，便求出户，変而为獭。 [70]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [70]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 东平吕球，丰财美貌 - Describes Lu Qiu, a wealthy and handsome man from Dongping.
    • 乘船至曲阿湖，值风不得行，泊菰际 - Indicates that Lu Qiu was sailing to Qu'a Lake, encountered a storm, and had to anchor near the reeds.
    • 见一少女，乘船采菱，举体皆衣荷叶 - Describes a young woman seen on a boat picking water chestnuts, dressed entirely in lotus leaves.
    • 子不闻‘荷衣兮蕙带，倏而来兮忽而逝’乎 - A poetic allusion to a line from the *Chuci* (Songs of Chu), suggesting the transient and ethereal nature of the woman.
    • 姑非鬼邪？衣服何至如此 - A rhetorical question asking if the woman is a ghost, given her unusual attire.
    • 倏而来兮忽而逝 - Another poetic allusion, emphasizing the fleeting and mysterious nature of the woman.
    • 回舟理棹，逡巡而去 - Describes the woman turning her boat and departing hesitantly.
    • 球遥射之，即获一獭 - Indicates that Lu Qiu shot at the woman and hit a badger.
    • 向者之船，皆是苹蘩薀藻之叶 - Reveals that the boat was made of water plant leaves.
    • 老母立岸侧，如有所候 - Describes an old woman standing on the shore, as if waiting for someone.
    • 君向来不见湖中采菱女子邪 - A rhetorical question asking if Lu Qiu saw the water chestnut-picking woman.
    • 近在右 - Indicates that the woman was to the right.
    • 寻射，复获老獭 - Describes Lu Qiu shooting again and hitting an old badger.
    • 河东常丑奴寓居章安县，以采蒲为业 - Introduces Chang Chounu, a resident of Zhang'an County, who makes a living by harvesting reeds.
    • 将一小儿，湖边拔蒲，暮，恒宿空田舍中 - Describes Chang Chounu taking a child to the lake to harvest reeds, staying overnight in an empty farmhouse.
    • 时日向暝，见一女子，容姿殊美，乘一小船，载莼径前，投丑奴舍寄住 - Describes a beautiful woman arriving in a small boat, bringing water chestnuts, and asking to stay at Chang Chounu's house.
    • 丑奴嘲之，灭火共卧，觉有腥气，又指甚短，惕然疑是魅 - Describes Chang Chounu teasing the woman, extinguishing the fire, and sleeping with her, noticing a fishy smell and short fingers, suspecting she might be a demon.
    • 女已知人意，便求出户，変而为獭 - Describes the woman realizing Chang Chounu's suspicion and transforming into a badger.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 东平 - A region in Shandong Province.
    • 曲阿湖 - A lake in the area, possibly referring to a specific body of water.
    • 荷衣兮蕙带，倏而来兮忽而逝 - A poetic line from the *Chuci* (Songs of Chu), emphasizing the transient and ethereal nature of the woman.
    • 河东 - A region in Shanxi Province.
    • 章安县 - A county in Zhejiang Province.
    • 采蒲 - Harvesting reeds, a common activity in the region.
    • 莼 - Water chestnuts, a type of aquatic plant.

    Proper Nouns

    • 东平吕球 - Lu Qiu, a wealthy and handsome man from Dongping.
    • 曲阿湖 - Qu'a Lake, a specific body of water.
    • 河东常丑奴 - Chang Chounu, a resident of Zhang'an County.
    • 章安县 - Zhang'an County, a location in Zhejiang Province.

    East of Ping, there lived a wealthy and handsome man named Lu Qiu. One day, he was sailing to Qu'a Lake when he encountered a storm and had to anchor near the reeds. As he looked around, he saw a young woman on a boat picking water chestnuts, dressed entirely in lotus leaves. Curious, he asked her, "Are you not a ghost? Why are you dressed like this?" The woman looked frightened and replied, "Have you not heard the words, 'Wearing lotus robes and tied with sweet flag, I come suddenly and vanish swiftly'?" Despite her fear, she turned her boat and hesitantly departed. Lu Qiu shot at her and hit a badger. Upon closer inspection, he realized that the boat was made of water plant leaves. He then saw an old woman standing on the shore, as if waiting for someone. She asked him, "Did you not see the water chestnut-picking woman in the lake earlier?" Lu Qiu replied, "She was to the right." He shot again and hit another badger. People living near the lake said, "There is often a beautiful water chestnut-picking woman in the lake, whose appearance surpasses others, and she sometimes visits homes, making many friends."

    In another part of the story, Chang Chounu, a resident of Zhang'an County, made a living by harvesting reeds. One evening, he took a child to the lake to harvest reeds and stayed overnight in an empty farmhouse. As the day grew dark, he saw a beautiful woman arrive in a small boat, bringing water chestnuts, and asked to stay at his house. Chang Chounu teased her, extinguished the fire, and slept with her. He noticed a fishy smell and short fingers, and became suspicious that she might be a demon. The woman realized his suspicion and transformed into a badger before leaving. [70]

    人有山行坠涧者，无出路，饥饿欲死。见龟蛇甚多，朝暮引颈向四方。人因学之，遂不饥。体殊轻便，能登岩岸。经数年后，竦身举臂，遂超出涧上，即得还家。颜色悦泽，颇更聪慧。泪食谷，啖滋味，百日复其本质。
    　　建德民虞敬上厕，輙有一人授手内草与之，不睹其形，如此非一过。后至厕，久无送者，但闻户外斗声。窥之，正见死奴与死婢争先进草。奴适在前，婢便因后挝，由此辄两相击。食顷，敬欲出，婢奴阵势方未已，乃厉声叱之，奄如火灭。自是遂绝。
    　　广陵韩咎字兴彦，陈敏反时，与敏弟恢战于寻阳。还营下马，觉鞭重，见有绿锦囊，中有短卷书着鞭鞘，皆不知所从来。开视之，故谷纸佛神咒经，乃世之常闻也。 [71]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [71]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 人有山行坠涧者 - Narrative opening describing a person who falls into a ravine while traveling through the mountains.
    • 引颈向四方 - Literally means 'stretching the neck towards all directions,' likely indicating a behavior or ritual practiced by the animals.
    • 体殊轻便 - Means 'the body becomes unusually light and agile,' suggesting a physical transformation.
    • 竦身举臂 - Describes a specific posture or movement that allows the person to leap out of the ravine.
    • 颜色悦泽 - Means 'appearance becomes lustrous and healthy,' indicating a positive physical change.
    • 颇更聪慧 - Means 'becomes much wiser,' suggesting an intellectual improvement.
    • 泪食谷，啖滋味 - Literally means 'weeping while eating grains, savoring flavors,' which might be interpreted as a metaphor for enjoying simple pleasures.
    • 奄如火灭 - Means 'suddenly disappears like a fire extinguished,' describing the abrupt end of the supernatural phenomenon.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 山行坠涧 - Refers to a dangerous situation in mountainous terrain, common in ancient Chinese literature.
    • 龟蛇 - Common symbols in Chinese mythology, often associated with longevity and protection.
    • 建德 - A place name, likely referring to a region in present-day Zhejiang Province.
    • 陈敏 - A historical figure, a rebel leader during the Three Kingdoms period.
    • 广陵 - Another place name, likely referring to a region in present-day Jiangsu Province.
    • 佛神咒经 - Refers to a Buddhist text, indicating the influence of Buddhism in the region.

    Proper Nouns

    • 建德 - A place name, likely referring to a region in present-day Zhejiang Province.
    • 虞敬 - The name of the man from Jiande.
    • 陈敏 - A historical figure, a rebel leader during the Three Kingdoms period.
    • 广陵 - A place name, likely referring to a region in present-day Jiangsu Province.
    • 韩咎 - The name of the man from Guangling.
    • 兴彦 - The courtesy name of Han Gou.
    • 寻阳 - A place name, likely referring to a city in present-day Hubei Province.

    A Person Who Fell into a Ravine

    There was once a person traveling through the mountains who fell into a ravine. He had no way out and was starving to death. He saw many turtles and snakes, which would stretch their necks toward all directions every morning and evening. The person imitated their behavior and thus stopped feeling hungry. His body became unusually light and agile, allowing him to climb rocky cliffs. After several years, he leaped out of the ravine by raising his body and arms, and returned home. His appearance became lustrous and healthy, and he became much wiser. He wept while eating grains and savored flavors, and after a hundred days, he regained his original form.

    A Man from Jiande

    A man from Jiande named Yu Jing went to the toilet, and a person would always give him grass, though he never saw the giver's form. This happened many times. One day, when he went to the toilet, there was no one giving him grass for a long time, but he heard fighting outside the door. Peering out, he saw a dead servant and a dead maid fighting over who should give him the grass first. The servant was just ahead, and the maid struck him from behind, causing them to fight each other. After a meal's time, Yu Jing wanted to leave, but the fight between the servant and the maid had not yet stopped. He shouted at them sternly, and they suddenly disappeared like a fire extinguished. From then on, the phenomenon ceased.

    A Man from Guangling

    Han Gou, courtesy name Xing Yan, from Guangling, fought against Tien Mi's younger brother, Hui, in Xunyang during the rebellion. When he returned to his camp and dismounted, he felt his whip was unusually heavy. He found a green silk pouch with a short scroll inside, attached to his whip. He did not know where it came from. When he opened it, it was an ancient Buddhist scripture written on old paper, which was a well-known text in the world. [71]

    武宣程羁，偏生，未被举。家常使种葱，后连理树生于园圃。
    　　谯郡胡馥之娶妇李氏，十余年无子，而妇卒。哭恸，云：“竟无遗体遂伤，此酷何深！”妇忽起坐曰：“感君痛悼，我不即朽。君可瞑后见就，依平生时阴阳，当为君生一男。”语毕，还卧。馥之如言，不取灯烛，暗而就之交接。后叹曰：“亡人亦无生理。可别作屋见置，瞻视满十月，然后殡。”尔来觉妇身微暖，如未亡。既及十月，果生一男，男名灵产。
    　　王伯阳亡，其子营墓，得三漆棺，移置南冈。夜梦鲁肃瞋云：“当杀汝父！”寻复梦见伯阳云：“鲁肃与弟争墓。”后于坐褥上见数升血，疑鲁肃杀之故也。墓今在长广桥东一里。
    　　海陵民黄寻，先居家单贫。尝因大风雨，散钱飞至其家，来触篱援，误落在余处，皆拾而得之。寻后巨富，钱至数千万，遂擅名于江表。 [72]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [72]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 偏生 - To be born out of turn or unexpectedly.
    • 未被举 - Not being selected or chosen.
    • 连理树 - A tree with intertwined branches, symbolizing a harmonious union or special bond.
    • 竟无遗体遂伤 - To lose everything and be deeply hurt.
    • 此酷何深 - How deep is this cruelty.
    • 亡人亦无生理 - The deceased has no chance of survival.
    • 可别作屋见置 - You can build another house for her and place her there.
    • 瞻视满十月 - To observe her for ten months.
    • 后于坐褥上见数升血 - Later, on the cushion, there was a handful of blood.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 程羁 - A person's name, possibly a historical figure or a fictional character.
    • 胡馥之 - A person's name, possibly a historical figure or a fictional character.
    • 李氏 - A person's name, possibly a historical figure or a fictional character.
    • 鲁肃 - A historical figure, a prominent general and statesman of the Three Kingdoms period.
    • 长广桥 - A place name, possibly a bridge or a location in ancient China.
    • 海陵 - A place name, possibly a region in ancient China.
    • 江表 - Refers to the region south of the Yangtze River, often used to describe the area known as Jiangnan.

    Proper Nouns

    • 程羁 - A person's name.
    • 胡馥之 - A person's name.
    • 李氏 - A person's name.
    • 鲁肃 - A historical figure.
    • 长广桥 - A place name.
    • 海陵 - A place name.
    • 江表 - A region name.

    Cheng Ji of Wu Xuan was born out of turn and not selected. His family often made him plant scallions, and later a conjoined tree grew in the garden.

    Hu Fu Zhi of Qi County married a woman named Li, and they had no children for over a decade before she died. He cried bitterly, saying, "To lose everything and be deeply hurt, how deep is this cruelty!" The woman suddenly sat up and said, "I am moved by your grief. I will not decay immediately. You can come to me after you sleep, following our usual intimate times, and I will give you a son." After speaking, she lay down again. Hu Fu Zhi followed her instructions, without taking a lamp or candle, and they had intercourse in the dark. Later, he sighed, "The deceased has no chance of survival. You can build another house for her and place her there, observe her for ten months, and then bury her." Since then, he felt his wife's body was slightly warm, as if she were still alive. After ten months, a son was indeed born, and he was named Ling Chan.

    Wang Boyang died, and his son moved his tomb to a new location, finding three lacquered coffins and placing them on the southern hill. At night, he dreamed that Lu Su angrily said, "I will kill your father!" Later, he dreamed that Wang Boyang said, "Lu Su and his brother are fighting over the tomb." Later, on the cushion, there was a handful of blood. He suspected that Lu Su killed him. The tomb is now located one li east of Changguang Bridge.

    Huang Xun of Haiting was once a poor man. During a storm, coins flew into his home, touching the fence, and all of them were picked up. Later, he became very wealthy, with millions of coins, and eventually became famous in the Jiangnan region. [72]

    余杭人沉纵，家素贫，与父同入山。还，未至家，见一人左右导从四百许，前车辎重，马鞭夹道，卤簿如二千石。遥见纵父子，便唤住，就纵手中然火。纵因问：“是何贵人？”答曰：“是斗山王，在余杭南。”纵知是神，叩头云：“愿见佑助！”后入山得一玉枕。从此所向如意，田蚕并收，家遂富。
    　　项县民姚牛，年十余岁。父为乡人所杀，牛常卖衣物市刀戟，图欲报仇。后在县署前相遇，手刃之于众中。吏捕得，官长深矜孝节，为推迁其事，会赦得免。又为州郡论救，遂得无他。令后出猎，逐鹿入草中，有古深阱数处，马将趣之。忽见一公，举杖击马，马惊避，不得及鹿。令怒，引弓将射之。公曰：“此中有阱，恐君堕耳！”令曰：“汝为何人？”翁跪曰：“民姚牛父也，感君活牛，故来谢恩。”因灭不见。令身感冥事，在官数年，多惠于民。 [73]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [73]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 家素贫 - The family has always been poor. The word '素' emphasizes the long-standing nature of the poverty.
    • 与父同入山 - Went into the mountains with his father. This is a straightforward description of an action.
    • 还，未至家 - On their return, they had not yet reached home. The phrase '还' indicates the act of returning.
    • 左右导从四百许 - With about four hundred attendants on either side. The word '许' indicates an approximate number.
    • 前车辎重 - The front vehicles carrying heavy loads. '辎重' refers to supplies and equipment.
    • 马鞭夹道 - Horses and whips lined the road. This describes a grand procession.
    • 卤簿如二千石 - The entourage was like that of a high-ranking official. '卤簿' refers to the ceremonial procession, and '二千石' is a rank equivalent to a high-ranking official.
    • 遥见纵父子，便唤住 - Seeing them from afar, the person called them to stop. '唤住' means to call and stop.
    • 就纵手中然火 - Took fire from the hand of Zong. '然火' means to take fire.
    • 愿见佑助 - I wish to be protected and assisted. '佑助' means protection and assistance.
    • 从此所向如意 - From then on, everything went smoothly. '如意' means as one wishes.
    • 田蚕并收 - Both farming and sericulture were successful. '并收' means both succeeded.
    • 家遂富 - The family became wealthy. '遂' means eventually or ultimately.
    • 年十余岁 - Aged more than ten years old. '十余岁' means over ten years old.
    • 父为乡人所杀 - His father was killed by a villager. '为...所' is a passive construction.
    • 常卖衣物市刀戟 - He often sold clothes to buy weapons. '市' means to buy.
    • 图欲报仇 - Planning to avenge his father. '图' means to plan.
    • 后在县署前相遇 - Later, he met the villager in front of the county office. '县署' means county office.
    • 手刃之于众中 - He killed him in front of everyone. '手刃' means to kill with one's own hands.
    • 吏捕得 - The officials captured him. '捕得' means to capture.
    • 官长深矜孝节 - The officials highly respected his filial piety and righteousness. '深矜' means to highly respect.
    • 为推迁其事 - They delayed the case. '推迁' means to delay.
    • 会赦得免 - He was released due to a general amnesty. '会赦' means due to a general amnesty.
    • 又为州郡论救 - And was further defended by the state and county officials. '论救' means to defend or plead on behalf of.
    • 遂得无他 - And thus he was spared any further punishment. '无他' means no further trouble.
    • 令后出猎 - The magistrate went hunting later. '令' means magistrate.
    • 逐鹿入草中 - Chasing deer into the grass. '逐' means to chase.
    • 有古深阱数处 - There were several ancient deep pits. '古深阱' means ancient deep pits.
    • 马将趣之 - The horse was about to approach them. '趣' means to approach.
    • 忽见一公 - Suddenly saw an old man. '公' means old man.
    • 举杖击马 - Raised a stick and struck the horse. '举杖' means to raise a stick.
    • 马惊避 - The horse was startled and avoided the pit. '惊避' means to be startled and avoid.
    • 不得及鹿 - And could not catch up with the deer. '及' means to catch up.
    • 令怒，引弓将射之 - The magistrate was angry and drew his bow to shoot at the old man. '引弓' means to draw a bow.
    • 此中有阱，恐君堕耳 - There are pits here, I fear you might fall. '恐' means to fear.
    • 汝为何人 - Who are you? '何人' means who.
    • 翁跪曰 - The old man knelt and said. '翁' means old man.
    • 感君活牛，故来谢恩 - I am grateful to you for saving my son, so I come to thank you. '感' means to be grateful, '活' means to save, '谢恩' means to thank.
    • 因灭不见 - Then disappeared. '因' means then, '灭' means to disappear.
    • 令身感冥事 - The magistrate personally experienced supernatural events. '冥事' means supernatural events.
    • 在官数年，多惠于民 - He served in office for several years and was very benevolent to the people. '惠' means to be benevolent.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 余杭 - A place name, a county in Zhejiang Province.
    • 斗山王 - A reference to a local deity or spirit, likely a folk belief or local legend.
    • 项县 - A place name, a county in Henan Province.
    • 姚牛 - A personal name, 'Yao Niu'.
    • 灵帝 - A reference to Emperor Ling of Han, though not explicitly mentioned in the text, it is a historical context.
    • 县署 - The county office, a governmental institution.
    • 州郡 - Refers to state and county officials, indicating a hierarchical administrative system.
    • 令 - Refers to the magistrate, a local government official.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • 与父同入山 - This phrase can be read as 'went into the mountains with his father' or 'went into the mountains, and his father went with him.'
    • 左右导从四百许 - This phrase can be read as 'with about four hundred attendants on either side' or 'with attendants on both sides, numbering about four hundred.'
    • 前车辎重，马鞭夹道，卤簿如二千石 - This long sequence must be read as a single description of a grand procession, with each element contributing to the overall image.
    • 遥见纵父子，便唤住，就纵手中然火 - This sequence must be read as a series of actions performed by the mysterious figure, emphasizing the sudden and unexpected nature of the encounter.
    • 从此所向如意，田蚕并收，家遂富 - This sequence must be read as a series of outcomes that followed the encounter, emphasizing the positive changes in Zong's life.
    • 项县民姚牛，年十余岁 - This phrase introduces Yao Niu, emphasizing his youth and the tragic circumstances of his father's death.
    • 常卖衣物市刀戟，图欲报仇 - This sequence must be read as a series of actions and intentions, emphasizing Yao Niu's determination to avenge his father.
    • 后在县署前相遇，手刃之于众中 - This sequence must be read as a series of events leading to Yao Niu's act of vengeance, emphasizing the dramatic and public nature of the event.

    A man from Yuhang named Shen Zong, whose family had always been poor, went into the mountains with his father. On their return, they had not yet reached home when they saw a person with about four hundred attendants on either side, the front vehicles carrying heavy loads, horses and whips lining the road, and an entourage like that of a high-ranking official. Seeing them from afar, the person called them to stop and took fire from Zong's hand. Zong asked, "Who is this noble person?" The person replied, "I am the Lord of Dou Mountain, located to the south of Yuhang." Zong realized it was a deity and knelt, saying, "I wish to be protected and assisted!" Later, he found a jade pillow in the mountains. From then on, everything went smoothly, both farming and sericulture were successful, and the family became wealthy.

    A man named Yao Niu from Xiang County, aged more than ten years old, had his father killed by a villager. Yao Niu often sold clothes to buy weapons, planning to avenge his father. Later, he met the villager in front of the county office and killed him in front of everyone. The officials captured him, but the officials highly respected his filial piety and righteousness, delayed the case, and he was released due to a general amnesty. He was further defended by the state and county officials and thus was spared any further punishment. The magistrate went hunting later, chasing deer into the grass, where there were several ancient deep pits. The horse was about to approach them when suddenly saw an old man who raised a stick and struck the horse. The horse was startled and avoided the pit, and could not catch up with the deer. The magistrate was angry and drew his bow to shoot at the old man. The old man said, "There are pits here, I fear you might fall!" The magistrate asked, "Who are you?" The old man knelt and said, "I am Yao Niu's father, grateful to you for saving my son, so I come to thank you." Then he disappeared. The magistrate personally experienced supernatural events and served in office for several years, being very benevolent to the people. [73]

    吴县费升为九里亭吏，向暮，见一女从郭中来，素衣，哭，入埭，向一新冢哭。日暮，不得入门，便寄亭宿。升作酒食，至夜，升弹琵琶令歌，女云：“有丧仪，勿笑人也。”歌音甚媚，云：“精气感冥昧，所降若有缘。嗟我遘良契，寄忻霄梦间。”中曲云：“成公从仪起，兰香降张硕。荀云冥分结，缠绵在今夕。”下曲云：“伫我风云会，正矣今夕游。神交虽未久，中心已绸缪。”寝处向明，升去，顾谓曰：“且至御亭。”女便惊怖。猎人至，郡狗入屋，于床咬死，成大狸。 [74]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [74]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 有丧仪，勿笑人也 - A polite request to respect the deceased and not mock.
    • 精气感冥昧，所降若有缘 - A poetic expression suggesting that spirits descend due to some kind of karmic connection.
    • 嗟我遘良契，寄忻霄梦间 - Expresses joy at meeting a good match, even if it's only in a dream.
    • 成公从仪起，兰香降张硕 - Refers to historical or mythological figures, possibly indicating a pattern of spiritual encounters.
    • 荀云冥分结，缠绵在今夕 - Suggests a deep, fated connection that is being realized in the present.
    • 伫我风云会，正矣今夕游 - Expresses anticipation of a meeting under auspicious conditions.
    • 神交虽未久，中心已绸缪 - Indicates that although the spiritual connection is new, the hearts are already closely intertwined.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 九里亭 - A specific location, likely a historical or literary reference.
    • 素衣 - Wearing white is often associated with mourning in Chinese culture.
    • 新冢 - A newly constructed tomb, indicating recent burial.
    • 寄亭宿 - Staying at a pavilion, which was common in ancient times for travelers.
    • 弹琵琶令歌 - Playing the pipa and singing, a common form of entertainment.
    • 御亭 - An imperial pavilion, suggesting a place of importance.
    • 成大狸 - Transformation into a large cat, possibly a supernatural event.

    Proper Nouns

    • 吴县 - A county in ancient China.
    • 费升 - The name of the official.

    Wu County official Fei Sheng served at Jiu Li Pavilion. As dusk fell, he saw a woman coming from the outskirts, dressed in white, weeping. She entered a dike and approached a newly built tomb, weeping there. As night fell, she could not enter the tomb, so she stayed overnight at the pavilion. Sheng prepared wine and food, and at night, he played the pipa and sang. The woman said, "There is a funeral, do not mock me." Her singing voice was very charming, and she sang, "Spiritual energy connects the unseen, descending as if by fate. Ah, I have met a good match, finding joy in dreams." In the middle part of her song, she sang, "Cheng Gong rises with ceremony, Lan Xiang descends upon Zhang Shuo. Xun Yun says that fate is bound, entwined in this night." In the concluding part, she sang, "Await my meeting in the clouds and winds, it is auspicious tonight. Though our spiritual connection is brief, our hearts are already closely intertwined." As dawn approached, Sheng left, and the woman said, "Let us go to the imperial pavilion." She became frightened. Hunters arrived, and the county dogs entered the house, biting her to death on the bed, transforming her into a large cat. [74]

    代郡界，有一亭，常有怪，不可诣止。有诸生壮勇，行歌止宿，亭吏止之。诸生曰：“我自能消此。”乃住宿食。至夜，鬼吹五孔笛，有一手，都不能得摄笛。诸生不耐，忽便笑谓：“汝止有一手，那得遍笛？我为汝吹来。”鬼云：“卿为我少指邪？”乃引手，即有数十指出。诸生知其可击，拔剑斫之，得一老雄鸡，从者并鸡雏耳。
    　　一士人姓王，坐斋中。有一人通刺诣之，题刺云舒甄仲。既去，疑非人，寻刺，曰：是予舍西土瓦中人。令掘之，果于瓦器中得一铜人，长尺余。 [75]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [75]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 代郡界 - Refers to the border area of Dai Commandery, indicating a specific geographical location.
    • 亭 - Refers to a small administrative or resting place along the road, often serving as a checkpoint or lodging.
    • 不可诣止 - Literally means 'cannot be approached or stopped,' suggesting a place that is off-limits or dangerous.
    • 诸生壮勇 - Refers to young scholars or students who are brave and strong.
    • 行歌止宿 - Means 'to sing while traveling and rest overnight,' describing the action of the scholars.
    • 亭吏止之 - Means 'the official at the pavilion stopped them,' indicating the authority figure's intervention.
    • 我自能消此 - Means 'I can deal with this myself,' expressing the scholar's confidence.
    • 乃住宿食 - Means 'then they stayed and ate,' indicating the scholars' decision to remain.
    • 至夜 - Means 'by night,' setting the time frame for the subsequent events.
    • 鬼吹五孔笛 - Means 'the ghost blew a five-holed flute,' describing the supernatural activity.
    • 有一手 - Literally means 'has one hand,' but in this context, it likely refers to a single limb or appendage.
    • 都不能得摄笛 - Means 'could not hold the flute properly,' indicating the ghost's difficulty.
    • 诸生不耐 - Means 'the scholars could not bear it,' expressing their frustration.
    • 忽便笑谓 - Means 'suddenly laughed and said,' indicating the scholar's reaction.
    • 汝止有一手，那得遍笛？ - Means 'You only have one hand, how can you play the flute?' expressing the scholar's taunt.
    • 我为汝吹来 - Means 'I will play for you,' indicating the scholar's offer to help.
    • 卿为我少指邪？ - Means 'Are you giving me more fingers?' expressing the ghost's response.
    • 乃引手 - Means 'then extended his hand,' indicating the ghost's action.
    • 即有数十指出 - Means 'then had dozens of fingers appear,' describing the ghost's transformation.
    • 诸生知其可击 - Means 'the scholars knew they could attack,' indicating their realization.
    • 拔剑斫之 - Means 'drew their swords and struck,' describing the scholars' action.
    • 得一老雄鸡 - Means 'they found an old rooster,' indicating the outcome of the attack.
    • 从者并鸡雏耳 - Means 'the followers were just chicks,' indicating the nature of the ghost's followers.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 代郡 - Refers to Dai Commandery, an administrative region during the Han Dynasty, located in present-day northern China.
    • 亭 - Refers to a small administrative or resting place along the road, often serving as a checkpoint or lodging.
    • 鬼 - Refers to a ghost or spirit, a common figure in Chinese folklore and literature.
    • 五孔笛 - Refers to a five-holed flute, a traditional musical instrument in China.
    • 诸生 - Refers to young scholars or students, often associated with Confucian education.
    • 亭吏 - Refers to the official in charge of the pavilion, a position of authority in local administration.

    Proper Nouns

    • 代郡 - The name of the commandery.
    • 亭 - The name of the pavilion.
    • 诸生 - The name of the scholars.
    • 亭吏 - The name of the official in charge of the pavilion.
    • 鬼 - The name of the supernatural entity.
    • 五孔笛 - The name of the musical instrument.
    • 老雄鸡 - The name of the old rooster.
    • 鸡雏 - The name of the chicks.

    In the border area of Dai Commandery, there was a pavilion that was often haunted and could not be approached. A group of brave and strong young scholars, singing as they traveled, stopped there for the night. The official at the pavilion tried to stop them, but the scholars said, "We can handle this ourselves," and decided to stay and eat. By night, a ghost began to play a five-holed flute with one hand, unable to hold it properly. The scholars could not bear it and suddenly laughed, saying, "You only have one hand, how can you play the flute? I will play for you." The ghost replied, "Are you giving me more fingers?" and then extended his hand, revealing dozens of fingers. The scholars realized they could attack and drew their swords, striking the ghost. They found an old rooster, and its followers were just chicks.

    In another instance, a scholar named Wang was sitting in his study. A man introduced himself by leaving a card, which read "Shu Zhenzhong." After the man left, Wang suspected that the man was not human. He searched for the card and found it, which said, "This is the person from the earthenware in the west of my house." He ordered the area to be dug up, and indeed, they found a copper statue about a foot long in a clay vessel. [75]

    襄阳城南有秦民，为性至孝，亲没，泣血三年。人有为其咏《蓼莪》诗者，民闻其义，涕泗不自胜。
    　　寻阳参军梦一妇人，前跪自称：“先葬近水淹没，诚能见救，虽不能富贵，可令君薄免祸。”参军答曰：“何以为志？”妇人曰：“君见渚边上有鱼钗，即我也。”参军明旦觅，果见一毁坟，其上有钗，移置高燥处。却十余日，参军行至东桥，牛奔直趋水，垂堕，忽转，正得无恙也。
    　　清河崔茂伯女，结婚裴氏，克期未至，女暴亡。提一金罂，受二升许，径到裴床前立，以罂赠裴。
    　　宏农徐俭家，有一远来客寄宿。有马一匹，中夜惊跳。客不安，骑马而去。一物长丈余，来逐马后，客射之，闻如中木声。明日寻昨路，见箭着一碓栅。
    　　刘松在家，忽见一鬼，拔剑斫之。鬼走，松起逐。见鬼在高山岩石上卧，乃往逼突。群鬼争走，遗置药杵臼及所余药，因将还家。松为人合药时，临熟取一撮经此臼者，无不效验。 [76]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [76]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 為性至孝 - Literally 'of nature extremely filial,' describing someone who is exceptionally devoted to filial piety.
    • 泣血三年 - A hyperbolic expression meaning 'shedding tears for three years,' indicating extreme grief and devotion.
    • 《蓼莪》詩 - Refers to the *Liaoluo* poem, a classical Chinese poem from the *Book of Songs* that laments the loss of parents.
    • 涕泗不自勝 - Means 'tears and snot could not be controlled,' describing overwhelming emotion.
    • 何以為志 - Literally 'what is the sign,' asking for a marker or sign to identify the deceased.
    • 毀坟 - Means 'destroyed tomb,' referring to a tomb that has been damaged by water.
    • 薄免祸 - Means 'slightly avoid disaster,' suggesting a minor relief from misfortune.
    • 金罂 - Literally 'golden jar,' a container, likely symbolic or valuable.
    • 直趋水 - Means 'ran straight into the water,' describing the horse's sudden movement.
    • 中木声 - Means 'sound like hitting wood,' describing the sensation of the arrow hitting something solid.
    • 碓栅 - Refers to a threshing rack or a wooden structure used for threshing grain.
    • 合药 - Means 'to prepare medicine,' referring to the process of making medicinal preparations.
    • 无不效验 - Means 'all are effective,' indicating that the medicine works without fail.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 襄阳 - Refers to Xiangyang, an ancient city in Hubei Province, China.
    • 寻阳 - Refers to Xunyang, an ancient city in Jiangxi Province, China.
    • 清河 - Refers to Qinghe, an ancient region in Hebei Province, China.
    • 宏农 - Refers to Hongnong, an ancient county in Shaanxi Province, China.
    • 《蓼莪》诗 - A classical Chinese poem from the *Book of Songs* that expresses deep sorrow and longing for deceased parents.
    • 崔茂伯 - A historical figure, likely a noble or official, though specific details are not provided in the text.
    • 裴氏 - Refers to the family name 'Pai,' a common surname in Chinese history.
    • 刘松 - A historical figure, likely a commoner or minor official, though specific details are not provided in the text.

    Proper Nouns

    • 襄阳 - The city of Xiangyang.
    • 寻阳 - The city of Xunyang.
    • 清河 - The region of Qinghe.
    • 宏农 - The county of Hongnong.
    • 崔茂伯 - A historical figure.
    • 裴氏 - The family name 'Pai.'
    • 刘松 - A historical figure.
    • 《蓼莪》诗 - The *Liaoluo* poem from the *Book of Songs*.

    In the south of Xiangyang, there was a man from Qin who was exceptionally filial. After his parents' death, he shed tears for three years. Someone recited the Liaoluo poem to him, and upon hearing its meaning, he could not control his tears and snot.

    In Xunyang, a military officer dreamed of a woman who knelt before him and said, "I was buried near water and drowned. If you can rescue me, even if you cannot become rich or powerful, you can slightly avoid disaster." The officer replied, "What sign do you have?" The woman said, "You will see a fish hairpin on the shore. That is me." The next morning, the officer searched and found a destroyed tomb with a hairpin on it. He moved it to a high and dry place. About ten days later, while traveling to the East Bridge, the officer's horse suddenly ran straight into the water, nearly falling. It suddenly turned and was unharmed.

    In Qinghe, the daughter of Cui Maobo, who was to marry into the Pai family, died suddenly before the wedding date. She carried a golden jar, about two liters in size, and went directly to the Pai family's bed, leaving the jar as a gift.

    In Hongnong, Xu Jian had a distant guest staying at his home. The guest brought a horse, which suddenly became frightened and jumped at midnight. The guest felt uneasy and rode the horse away. A creature about ten feet long followed the horse, and the guest shot it, hearing a sound like hitting wood. The next day, he retraced his steps and found the arrow stuck in a threshing rack.

    At home, Liu Song suddenly saw a ghost. He drew his sword and attacked it. The ghost ran away, and Liu chased it. He found the ghost lying on a high rock and approached it. The ghost fled, leaving behind a mortar and pestle along with some remaining medicine. Liu took these items home. When he prepared medicine, using a pinch of the medicine that had passed through the mortar, it was always effective. [76]

    曲阿有一人，忘姓名，从京还，逼暮不得至家。遇雨，宿广屋中。雨止月朗，遥见一女子，来至屋檐下。便有悲叹之音，乃解腰中绻绳，悬屋角自绞。又觉屋檐上如有人牵绳绞。此人密以刀斫绻绳，又斫屋上，见一鬼西走。向曙，女气方苏，能语：“家在前。”持此人将归，向女父母说其事。或是天运使然，因以女嫁与为妻。
    　　爰琮为新安太守，郡南界有刻石，爰至其下宴。忽有人得剪刀于石下者，众咸异之。综问主簿，主簿对曰：“昔吴长沙桓王尝饮饯孙洲，父老云：‘此洲狭而长，君尝为长沙乎？’果应。夫三刀为州，得交刀，君亦当交州。”后果交州。
    　　有一伧小儿，放牛野中，伴辈数人。见一鬼，依诸丛草间，处处设网，欲以捕人。设网后未竟，伧小儿窃取前网，仍以罨之，即缚得鬼。 [77]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [77]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 逼暮 - Approaching dusk or late in the day.
    • 廣屋 - A large, open-roofed building or structure.
    • 悲歎之音 - Sounds of sorrow and lamentation.
    • 懸屋角自絞 - Hanging from the corner of the roof and strangling oneself.
    • 密以刀斫 - Secretly cutting with a knife.
    • 西走 - To go westward.
    • 向曙 - At dawn or just before daybreak.
    • 氣方蘇 - The breath is just returning or coming back to life.
    • 持此人將歸 - Taking this person back home.
    • 或是天運使然 - Perhaps it was fate or destiny.
    • 因以女嫁與為妻 - Therefore, he married her as his wife.
    • 宴 - Feast or banquet.
    • 忽有人得剪刀於石下者 - Suddenly, someone found a pair of scissors under the stone.
    • 主簿 - An official who handles administrative duties.
    • 昔吳长沙桓王 - Historical figure Sun Quan, the founder of the state of Wu during the Three Kingdoms period.
    • 此洲狭而长 - This island is narrow and long.
    • 三刀為州 - A riddle-like expression suggesting the character '州' (zhou) can be formed by combining three '刀' (dao) characters.
    • 交刀 - Crossed knives or interlocking knives.
    • 君亦當交州 - You will also govern Jiaozhou.
    • 放牛野中 - Letting cattle graze in the wild.
    • 伴輩數人 - A few companions.
    • 依諸丛草間 - Among the tall grass.
    • 設網 - Setting up nets.
    • 欲以捕人 - To catch people.
    • 設網後未竟 - After setting up the nets but not yet finished.
    • 伧小儿 - A rustic or simple-minded child.
    • 窃取前网 - Secretly taking the front net.
    • 仍以罨之 - And then covering him with it.
    • 即缚得鬼 - Immediately capturing the ghost.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 曲阿 - An ancient place name, possibly referring to a location in present-day Jiangsu Province.
    • 爰琮 - Historical figure Yuan Cong, who served as the governor of Xin'an.
    • 新安 - An ancient county name, possibly referring to present-day Huizhou, Anhui Province.
    • 孫洲 - Refers to a specific island or region associated with Sun Quan.
    • 交州 - An ancient administrative region in southern China, roughly corresponding to present-day Guangdong, Guangxi, and Vietnam.
    • 伧 - A term used to describe a rustic or simple-minded person.

    Proper Nouns

    • 曲阿 - An ancient place name.
    • 爰琮 - Historical figure Yuan Cong.
    • 新安 - Ancient county name.
    • 孫洲 - Specific island or region.
    • 交州 - Ancient administrative region.
    • 长沙桓王 - Historical figure Sun Quan.
    • 三刀 - Riddle-like expression.
    • 交刀 - Riddle-like expression.
    • 伧 - Term for a rustic or simple-minded person.

    In Qua, there was a man whose name was forgotten, returning from the capital. As dusk approached, he could not reach his home. Encountering rain, he took shelter in a large open-roofed building. When the rain stopped and the moon became bright, he saw a woman far away, coming to the eaves of the house. She emitted sounds of sorrow and lamentation, then untied the rope around her waist and hung herself from the corner of the roof. He also felt as if someone was pulling the rope on the eaves to strangle her. This man secretly cut the rope with a knife, then cut the roof, seeing a ghost go westward. At dawn, the woman's breath returned, and she could speak: "My home is ahead." He took her back to her parents and told them about the incident. Perhaps it was fate, and he married her as his wife.

    Yuan Cong was the governor of Xin'an, and in the southern boundary of the county, there was a carved stone. He held a feast at its base. Suddenly, someone found a pair of scissors under the stone, and everyone was surprised. Yuan Cong asked the clerk, who replied, "In the past, Wu's Prince of Changsha, Sun Quan, once held a farewell banquet on Sun Island. The elders said, 'This island is narrow and long. Have you ever been to Changsha?' It turned out to be true. The character 'zhou' (州) is formed by combining three 'dao' (刀) characters. Finding a pair of crossed knives, you will also govern Jiaozhou." Indeed, he later governed Jiaozhou.

    There was a rustic child who let cattle graze in the wild with several companions. They saw a ghost among the tall grass, setting up nets everywhere to catch people. After setting up the nets but not finishing, the rustic child secretly took the front net and covered the ghost with it, immediately capturing the ghost. [77]

    琅邪诸葛氏兄弟二人，寓居晋陵，家甚贫耗，常假乞自给。谷在圌中，计日月未应尽，而早以空罄。始者故谓是家中相窃盗，故复封检题识，而耗如初。后有宿客远来，际夕，至巷口，见数人担谷从门出，客借问：“诸葛在不？”答云：“悉在。”客进，语讫，因问：“卿何得大粜担？”主人云：“告乞少谷欲充口，云何复得粜之？”客云：“我向来逢见数人，担谷从门出。若不粜者，为是何事？”主人兄弟相视，窃自疑怪。试入看，封题俨然如故。试开圌量视，即无十许斛，知前后所失，非人为之也。
    　　河南阳起，字圣卿，少时病疟，逃于社中，得《素书》一卷，谴劾百鬼法，所劾辄效。为日南太守。母至厕上，见鬼，头长数尺，以告圣卿。圣卿曰：“此肃霜之神。劾之出来，変形如奴。送书京师，朝发暮反，作使当千人之力。有与忿恚者，圣卿遣神夜往，趋其床头，持两手，张目正赤，吐舌柱地，其人怖几死。 [78]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [78]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 寓居晋陵 - Means 'residing temporarily in Jinling,' indicating a temporary or non-native location.
    • 家甚贫耗 - Means 'their family was very poor and destitute.'
    • 常假乞自给 - Means 'they often begged for food to sustain themselves.'
    • 谷在圌中 - Means 'the grain was stored in a large basket or container.'
    • 计日月未应尽 - Means 'according to the calculation, it should not have run out yet.'
    • 而早以空罄 - Means 'but it had already run out early.'
    • 始者故谓是家中相窃盗 - Means 'at first, they thought someone in the family was stealing.'
    • 故复封检题识 - Means 'so they sealed and marked it again.'
    • 耗如初 - Means 'but it was still consumed as before.'
    • 后有宿客远来 - Means 'later, a distant guest arrived.'
    • 际夕，至巷口 - Means 'at dusk, he arrived at the alley entrance.'
    • 见数人担谷从门出 - Means 'he saw several people carrying grain out through the door.'
    • 客借问：“诸葛在不？” - Means 'the guest asked, 'Are the Ge family here?''
    • 答云：“悉在。” - Means 'they replied, 'Yes, they are all here.''
    • 客进，语讫 - Means 'the guest entered and finished speaking.'
    • 因问：“卿何得大粜担？” - Means 'then he asked, 'How did you get so much grain to sell?''
    • 主人云：“告乞少谷欲充口，云何复得粜之？” - Means 'the host said, 'We begged for a little grain to eat, how could we sell it?''
    • 客云：“我向来逢见数人，担谷从门出。若不粜者，为是何事？” - Means 'the guest said, 'I saw several people carrying grain out through the door earlier. If you weren't selling, what were they doing?''
    • 主人兄弟相视，窃自疑怪 - Means 'the brothers looked at each other, feeling suspicious and puzzled.'
    • 试入看，封题俨然如故 - Means 'they went inside to check, and the seal and markings were still intact.'
    • 试开圌量视，即无十许斛 - Means 'they opened the basket to check, and it had already been reduced by about ten shi.'
    • 知前后所失，非人为之也 - Means 'they realized that the loss was not due to human theft.'
    • 河南阳起，字圣卿 - Refers to a person named Yang Qi from Henan, with the courtesy name Shengqing.
    • 少时病疟，逃于社中 - Means 'when he was young, he fell ill with malaria and hid in a shrine.'
    • 得《素书》一卷 - Means 'he found a scroll of *Su Shu*.'
    • 谴劾百鬼法 - Means 'a method for summoning and exorcising a hundred spirits.'
    • 所劾辄效 - Means 'whenever he summoned them, they were effective.'
    • 为日南太守 - Means 'he became the governor of Rì Nán.'
    • 母至厕上，见鬼 - Means 'his mother went to the toilet and saw a ghost.'
    • 头长数尺 - Means 'with a head several feet long.'
    • 以告圣卿 - Means 'she told Saint Qing.'
    • 此肃霜之神 - Means 'this is the spirit of Sù Shuāng.'
    • 劾之出来，变形如奴 - Means 'when he summoned it, it transformed into the form of a servant.'
    • 送书京师，朝发暮反 - Means 'he sent a letter to the capital, which returned by evening.'
    • 作使当千人之力 - Means 'it could perform tasks equivalent to the strength of a thousand people.'
    • 有与忿恚者 - Means 'if someone was angry with him.'
    • 圣卿遣神夜往，趋其床头，持两手，张目正赤，吐舌柱地 - Means 'Saint Qing sent the spirit at night to stand by the person's bed, holding two hands, with wide-open red eyes, and a tongue sticking out to the ground.'
    • 其人怖几死 - Means 'the person was terrified almost to death.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 琅邪 - A place name, likely referring to a region in ancient China.
    • 晋陵 - A place name, likely referring to a city or district in ancient China.
    • 河南 - A place name, likely referring to a region in ancient China.
    • 日南 - A place name, likely referring to a region in ancient China.
    • 素书 - A classical text or scroll, possibly a reference to a specific work or collection of writings.
    • 社 - A shrine or temple, often associated with local deities or spirits.
    • 肃霜之神 - A specific spirit or deity, likely from local folklore or religious beliefs.
    • 京师 - The capital city, often used in classical texts to refer to the central government or imperial court.
    • 太守 - A high-ranking official, often the governor of a region or district.
    • 疟 - Malaria, a disease transmitted by mosquitoes.
    • 鬼 - Ghosts or spirits, often associated with supernatural phenomena in Chinese culture.
    • 忿恚 - Anger or resentment, often used in classical texts to describe strong emotions.
    • 床 - A bed or sleeping place, often used in classical texts to describe a place of rest or sleep.

    Proper Nouns

    • 琅邪诸葛氏 - The Lángyá Gě family.
    • 晋陵 - A place name, likely referring to a city or district in ancient China.
    • 河南 - A place name, likely referring to a region in ancient China.
    • 日南 - A place name, likely referring to a region in ancient China.
    • 素书 - A classical text or scroll.
    • 社 - A shrine or temple.
    • 肃霜之神 - A specific spirit or deity.

    Two brothers from the Lángyá Gě family were residing temporarily in Jinling, where their family was very poor and destitute, often begging for food to sustain themselves. The grain they had stored in a large basket was expected to last according to their calculations, but it had already run out early. At first, they suspected that someone in the family was stealing, so they sealed and marked the basket again, but it was still consumed as before. Later, a distant guest arrived at dusk, reaching the alley entrance, where he saw several people carrying grain out through the door. He asked, "Are the Ge family here?" They replied, "Yes, they are all here." The guest entered and finished speaking, then asked, "How did you get so much grain to sell?" The host said, "We begged for a little grain to eat, how could we sell it?" The guest said, "I saw several people carrying grain out through the door earlier. If you weren't selling, what were they doing?" The brothers looked at each other, feeling suspicious and puzzled. They went inside to check, and the seal and markings were still intact. They opened the basket to check, and it had already been reduced by about ten shi. They realized that the loss was not due to human theft.

    Yang Qi from Henan, with the courtesy name Shengqing, fell ill with malaria when he was young and hid in a shrine, where he found a scroll of Su Shu. It contained a method for summoning and exorcising a hundred spirits, which were always effective whenever he used it. He became the governor of Rì Nán. His mother went to the toilet and saw a ghost with a head several feet long, and she told Saint Qing about it. He said, "This is the spirit of Sù Shuāng. When I summon it, it transforms into the form of a servant. I can send a letter to the capital, which returns by evening, and it can perform tasks equivalent to the strength of a thousand people. If someone is angry with me, I will send the spirit at night to stand by the person's bed, holding two hands, with wide-open red eyes, and a tongue sticking out to the ground. The person will be terrified almost to death." [78]

    刘斌在吴郡时，娄县有一女，忽夜乘风雨，恍惚至郡城内。自觉去家止一炊顷，衣不沾濡。晓在门上，求通言：“我天使也，府君宜起迎我，当大富贵。不尔，必有凶祸。”刘问所来，亦不知。自后二十许日，刘果诛。
    　　护军琅邪王华，有一牛，甚快，常乘之，齿已长。华后梦牛语之曰：“衰老不复堪苦载，载二人尚可，过此必死。”华谓偶尔梦。与三人同载还府，此牛果死。
    　　吴兴戴眇家僮客姓王，有少妇，美色，而眇中弟恒往就之。客私怀忿怒，具以白眇：“中郎作此，甚为无礼，愿遵敕语。”眇以问弟，弟大骂曰：“何缘有此？必是妖鬼。敕令扑杀。”客初犹不敢约厉分明，后来闭户欲缚，便変成大狸，从窗中出。
    　　巴东有道士，忘其姓名。事道精进，入屋烧香。忽有风雨至，家人见一白鹭从屋中飞出。雨住，遂失道士所在。 [79]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [79]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 忽夜乘风雨 - Literary expression meaning 'suddenly riding the wind and rain at night,' used to describe a supernatural or dreamlike journey.
    • 恍惚 - Means 'vaguely' or 'unclearly,' often used to describe a dreamlike or supernatural state.
    • 止一炊顷 - Colloquial expression meaning 'only the time it takes to cook a meal,' used to indicate a very short period.
    • 衣不沾濡 - Means 'clothes not wet,' indicating that the journey was supernatural and unaffected by the weather.
    • 宜起迎我 - Means 'should rise to greet me,' a formal expression used by an angel or divine being.
    • 大富贵 - Means 'great wealth and honor,' a common phrase for blessings or rewards.
    • 衰老不复堪苦载 - Means 'too old to bear heavy loads,' a metaphor for the cow's declining strength.
    • 必死 - Means 'will surely die,' a direct and ominous statement.
    • 甚为无礼 - Means 'extremely disrespectful,' used to describe the behavior of the middle brother.
    • 妖鬼 - Means 'evil spirit' or 'ghost,' used to describe the supernatural entity.
    • 从窗中出 - Means 'from the window,' indicating the supernatural escape of the entity.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 吴郡 - Refers to Wu County, a region in modern-day Jiangsu Province, China.
    • 娄县 - Refers to Loucheng, a county in the Wu region.
    • 天使 - Means 'messenger of heaven,' a divine being who delivers messages or warnings.
    • 护军 - A military rank in ancient China, often held by high-ranking officials.
    • 琅邪王华 - Refers to Wang Hua, a historical figure from the琅邪 (Langya) region.
    • 巴东 - Refers to a region in modern-day Sichuan Province, China.
    • 道士 - Refers to a Taoist priest or practitioner.

    Proper Nouns

    • 刘斌 - Refers to Liu Bin, a historical figure.
    • 吴兴戴眇 - Refers to Dai Miao, a historical figure from Wu County.
    • 王华 - Refers to Wang Hua, a historical figure.
    • 巴东道士 - Refers to a Taoist priest from the Ba region.

    Liu Bin was in Wu County when a woman from Lou County suddenly rode the wind and rain at night, appearing vaguely in the city. She felt that she had left home for only the time it took to cook a meal, and her clothes were not wet. In the morning, she stood at the gate and asked to be let in, saying, "I am a messenger of heaven. You should rise to greet me, for you will attain great wealth and honor. If not, there will surely be calamity." Liu asked where she came from, but she did not know. After about twenty days, Liu was indeed executed.

    Wang Hua, a military officer from Langya, had a cow that was very strong and fast, and he often rode it, though its teeth had grown long. Later, Wang dreamed that the cow spoke to him, saying, "I am too old to bear heavy loads. I can still carry two people, but if more than that, I will surely die." Wang thought it was just an occasional dream. When he returned to the government office with three people, the cow indeed died.

    At the home of Dai Miao in Wu County, there was a young woman of great beauty, and Dai Miao's middle brother often visited her. A servant, whose name was Wang, harbored private anger and told Dai Miao, "Your brother has acted in a very disrespectful manner. I hope you will follow my instructions." Dai Miao asked his brother about it, and the brother shouted, "How could this be? It must be an evil spirit. Order me to kill it." The servant initially hesitated but later closed the door and tried to tie it up. The entity then transformed into a large fox and escaped through the window.

    In Ba Eastern, there was a Taoist priest whose name is forgotten. He was diligent in his practice and was burning incense when suddenly a storm came. His family saw a white heron fly out of the house. When the rain stopped, the Taoist priest was nowhere to be found. [79]

    会稽谢祖之妇，初育一男，又生一蛇，长二尺许，便径出门去。后数十年，妇以老终。祖忽闻西北有风雨之声，顷之，见蛇，长十数丈，腹可十余围，入户造灵座。因至柩所，绕数匝，以头打柩，目血泪俱出，良久而去。
    　　会稽郡吏鄮县薛重，得假还家。夜，户闭，闻妻床上有丈夫鼾声。唤妻，妻从床上出，未及开户，重持刀便逆问妻曰：“醉人是谁？”妻大惊愕，因苦自申明，实无人意。重家唯有一户，搜索，了无所见，见一大蛇，隐在床脚，酒臭，重便斩蛇寸断，掷于后沟。经数日，而妇死。又数日，而重卒。经三日复生，说始死时，有神人将重到一官府，见官寮，问：“何以杀人？”重曰：“实不曾行凶。”曰：“寸断掷在后沟，此是何物？”重曰：“此是蛇，非人。”府君愕然而悟曰：“我常用为神，而敢淫人妇，又妄讼人。敕左右召来！”吏卒乃领一人来，着平巾帻，具诘其淫妻之过，将付狱。重乃令人送还。 [80]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [80]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 会稽 - Ancient Chinese prefecture, now in Zhejiang Province.
    • 谢祖之妇 - Wife of Xie Zu, a historical figure from the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
    • 初育一男，又生一蛇 - Supernatural event of a woman giving birth to a boy and then a snake.
    • 长二尺许 - Approximately two feet long.
    • 径出门去 - Went straight out the door.
    • 数十年后 - After several decades.
    • 以老终 - Died of old age.
    • 忽闻西北有风雨之声 - Suddenly heard the sound of wind and rain from the northwest.
    • 顷之，见蛇，长十数丈，腹可十余围 - Soon after, saw a snake, about ten or more zhang long, with a body circumference of more than ten chi.
    • 入户造灵座 - Entered the house and approached the spirit altar.
    • 因至柩所，绕数匝，以头打柩，目血泪俱出 - Then went to the coffin, circled it several times, hit the coffin with its head, and both blood and tears flowed from its eyes.
    • 良久而去 - After a long time, it left.
    • 会稽郡吏鄮县薛重 - Xue Zhong, an official from the county of Mobian in Kuaiji Prefecture.
    • 得假还家 - Got leave to return home.
    • 夜，户闭 - At night, the door was closed.
    • 闻妻床上有丈夫鼾声 - Heard a man's snoring on his wife's bed.
    • 唤妻，妻从床上出 - Called his wife, who came out of bed.
    • 未及开户，重持刀便逆问妻曰 - Before the door was opened, Zhong drew a knife and asked his wife.
    • 醉人是谁 - Who is the drunk person?
    • 大惊愕 - Greatly surprised and shocked.
    • 实无人意 - There was no one there.
