    　　宋永初三年，吴郡张缝家，忽有一鬼，云：“汝分我食，当相佑助。”便与鬼食，舒席着地，以饭布席上，肉酒五肴。如是，鬼得便，不复犯暴人。后为作食，因以刀斫其所食处，便闻数十人哭，哭亦甚悲，云：“死何由得棺材？”又闻云：“主人家有梓船，奴甚爱惜，当取以为棺。”见担船至，有斧锯声。治船既竟，闻呼唤“举尸着棺中，缝眼不见，唯闻处分，不闻下钉声，便见船渐渐升空，入云霄中。久久灭，从空中落，船破成百片。便闻如有百数人大笑，云：“汝那能杀我？我当为汝所困者邪？但知恶心，我憎汝状，故破船坏耳。”缝便回意奉事此鬼。问吉凶及将来之计，语缝曰：“汝可以大瓮着壁角中，我当为觅物也。”十日一倒，有钱及金银铜铁鱼腥之属。 [99]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [99]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 宋初 - Refers to the early years of the Song dynasty.
    • 義興周超 - A proper name, where '義興' is a place name and '周超' is a personal name.
    • 為謝晦司馬在江陵 - '為' means 'to serve as,' '謝晦' is a proper name, '司馬' is a military title, and '江陵' is a place name.
    • 妻許氏在家 - '妻' means 'wife,' '許氏' is a surname, and '在家' means 'at home.'
    • 遙見屋里月光一死人頭在地，血流甚多 - '遙見' means 'saw from afar,' '屋里' means 'in the house,' '月光' means 'moonlight,' '一死人頭' means 'a dead person's head,' '在地' means 'on the ground,' and '血流甚多' means 'a lot of blood was flowing.'
    • 大驚，怪即便失去 - '大驚' means 'greatly surprised,' '怪' means 'strange,' and '即便失去' means 'immediately disappeared.'
    • 后超被法 - '后' means 'later,' '超' refers to Zhou Chao, and '被法' means 'was punished by law.'
    • 宋永初三年 - Refers to the third year of the Yongchu era during the Song dynasty.
    • 吳郡張縫家 - '吳郡' is a place name, and '張縫' is a proper name.
    • 忽有一鬼 - '忽' means 'suddenly,' and '有一鬼' means 'there appeared a ghost.'
    • 汝分我食，當相佑助 - '汝' means 'you,' '分我食' means 'give me food,' '當' means 'will,' '相' means 'mutually,' and '佑助' means 'help.'
    • 舒席著地 - '舒席' means 'spread out a mat,' and '著地' means 'on the ground.'
    • 以飯布席上 - '以' means 'with,' '飯' means 'rice,' '布' means 'spread,' and '席上' means 'on the mat.'
    • 肉酒五肴 - '肉' means 'meat,' '酒' means 'wine,' and '五肴' means 'five dishes.'
    • 如是，鬼得便，不復犯暴人 - '如是' means 'in this way,' '鬼得便' means 'the ghost was satisfied,' and '不復犯暴人' means 'no longer harmed people.'
    • 后為作食，因以刀斫其所食处 - '后' means 'later,' '為作食' means 'prepared food,' '因' means 'then,' '以刀斫' means 'with a knife chopped,' and '其所食处' means 'the place where it ate.'
    • 便聞数十人哭，哭亦甚悲 - '便聞' means 'then heard,' '数十人哭' means 'several dozen people crying,' and '哭亦甚悲' means 'the crying was very sad.'
    • 云：‘死何由得棺材？’ - '云' means 'said,' '死何由得棺材' means 'how can the dead get a coffin?'
    • 又聞云：‘主人家有梓船，奴甚爱惜，当取以为棺。’ - '又聞云' means 'then heard said,' '主人家' means 'the owner's family,' '有梓船' means 'has a cypress boat,' '奴甚爱惜' means 'the servant greatly loves it,' and '当取以为棺' means 'will take it to make a coffin.'
    • 见担船至，有斧锯声 - '见担船至' means 'saw the boat being carried,' and '有斧锯声' means 'there were sounds of axes and saws.'
    • 治船既竟，闻呼唤‘举尸着棺中’ - '治船既竟' means 'after the boat was repaired,' '闻呼唤' means 'heard a call,' and '举尸着棺中' means 'lift the body into the coffin.'
    • 缝眼不见，唯闻处分，不闻下钉声 - '缝眼不见' means 'Zhang Feng could not see,' '唯闻处分' means 'only heard instructions,' and '不闻下钉声' means 'did not hear the sound of nails being driven.'
    • 便见船渐渐升空，入云霄中 - '便见' means 'then saw,' '船渐渐升空' means 'the boat slowly rose into the air,' and '入云霄中' means 'into the clouds.'
    • 久久灭，从空中落，船破成百片 - '久久灭' means 'gradually disappeared,' '从空中落' means 'fell from the sky,' and '船破成百片' means 'the boat broke into hundreds of pieces.'
    • 便闻如有百数人大笑 - '便闻' means 'then heard,' '如有百数人大笑' means 'as if several dozen people were laughing.'
    • 云：‘汝那能杀我？我当为汝所困者邪？’ - '云' means 'said,' '汝那能杀我' means 'how could you kill me?' and '我当为汝所困者邪' means 'should I be the one you are trying to trap?'
    • 但知恶心，我憎汝状，故破船坏耳 - '但知恶心' means 'only know evil,' '我憎汝状' means 'I hate your appearance,' and '故破船坏耳' means 'so I broke the boat.'
    • 缝便回意奉事此鬼 - '缝' refers to Zhang Feng, '便回意' means 'then changed his mind,' and '奉事此鬼' means 'to serve this ghost.'
    • 问吉凶及将来之计 - '问' means 'ask,' '吉凶' means 'good or bad fortune,' and '将来之计' means 'future plans.'
    • 语缝曰：‘汝可以大瓮着壁角中，我当为觅物也。’ - '语' means 'told,' '汝可以大瓮着壁角中' means 'you can put a large jar in the corner of the wall,' and '我当为觅物也' means 'I will find things for you.'
    • 十日一倒，有钱及金银铜铁鱼腥之属 - '十日一倒' means 'every ten days,' '有钱及金银铜铁鱼腥之属' means 'there would be money and gold, silver, copper, iron, and fish bones.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 宋初 - Refers to the early years of the Song dynasty.
    • 義興 - A place name, likely referring to a region in present-day Jiangsu province.
    • 江陵 - A place name, likely referring to Jingzhou, a significant city during the Song dynasty.
    • 宋永初三年 - Refers to the third year of the Yongchu era during the Song dynasty.
    • 吳郡 - A place name, likely referring to Suzhou, a significant city during the Song dynasty.
    • 鬼 - Refers to a supernatural entity in Chinese folklore.
    • 法 - Refers to the legal system or punishment by law.

    During the early years of the Song dynasty, Zhou Chao from Yixing served as a military officer under Xie Hui in Jingzhou. His wife Xu Shi, who was at home, saw from afar a dead person's head on the ground in the house, with a lot of blood flowing, and was greatly surprised. The strange sight immediately disappeared. Later, Zhou Chao was punished by law.

    In the third year of the Yongchu era during the Song dynasty, in a family in Wu Prefecture, a ghost suddenly appeared and said to Zhang Feng, "Give me food, and I will help you." Zhang Feng then prepared food for the ghost, spreading out a mat on the ground and placing rice and five dishes on it. In this way, the ghost was satisfied and no longer harmed people. Later, when Zhang Feng prepared food again, he cut the place where the ghost had eaten with a knife. He then heard the sound of several dozen people crying, and the crying was very sad. They said, "How can the dead get a coffin?" Zhang Feng then heard another voice saying, "The owner's family has a cypress boat, and the servant greatly loves it. We will take it to make a coffin." He saw the boat being carried, and there were sounds of axes and saws. After the boat was repaired, he heard a call, "Lift the body into the coffin." Zhang Feng could not see, but only heard instructions and did not hear the sound of nails being driven. He then saw the boat slowly rise into the air and enter the clouds. Gradually, it disappeared and fell from the sky, breaking into hundreds of pieces. He then heard as if several dozen people were laughing and said, "How could you kill me? Should I be the one you are trying to trap? I only know evil, and I hate your appearance, so I broke the boat." Zhang Feng then changed his mind and began to serve this ghost. He asked the ghost about good or bad fortune and future plans. The ghost told Zhang Feng, "You can put a large jar in the corner of the wall, and I will find things for you." Every ten days, there would be money and gold, silver, copper, iron, and fish bones. [99]

    宋高祖永初中，张春为武昌太守时，人有嫁女，未及升车，忽便失性。出外，殴击人乘云：“已不乐嫁俗人。”巫云是邪魅，乃将女至江际，击鼓，以术祝治疗。春以为欺惑百姓，刻期须得妖魅。后有一青蛇来到巫所，即以大钉钉头。至日中，复见大龟从江来，伏前。更以赤朱书背作符，更遣去入江。至暮，有大白鼍从江中出，乍沉乍浮，向龟随后催逼。鼍自分死，冒未先入幔与女辞诀。女恸哭云：“失其姻好。”自此渐差。或问巫曰：“魅者归于何物？”巫云：“蛇是传通，龟是媒人，鼍是其对。所获三物，悉是魅。”春始知灵验。
    　　宋初，淮南郡有物髠人发。太守朱诞曰：“吾知之矣。”多置黐以涂壁。夕有数蝙蝠，大如鸡，集其上。不得去，杀之乃绝。屋檐下，已有数百人头髻。 [100]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [100]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 未及升车 - Literally means 'before mounting the carriage,' used to describe the moment just before the wedding ceremony begins.
    • 忽便失性 - Means 'suddenly lost her mind' or 'suddenly became deranged.'
    • 出外，殴击人乘云 - Literally means 'went outside and beat people while riding on clouds,' describing a supernatural or hallucinatory state.
    • 巫云是邪魅 - Means 'the witch said it was a demon or evil spirit.'
    • 刻期须得妖魅 - Means 'set a deadline to capture the demon.'
    • 乍沉乍浮 - Means 'sinking and floating intermittently,' describing the movement of the white crocodile.
    • 向龟随后催逼 - Means 'the turtle followed and urged it on.'
    • 失其姻好 - Means 'lost her marital prospects.'
    • 渐差 - Means 'gradually improved' or 'gradually recovered.'
    • 或问巫曰 - Means 'someone asked the witch.'
    • 魅者归于何物 - Means 'what did the demon turn into?'
    • 蛇是传通 - Means 'the snake was the messenger.'
    • 龟是媒人 - Means 'the turtle was the matchmaker.'
    • 鼍是其对 - Means 'the crocodile was its counterpart.'
    • 悉是魅 - Means 'all were demons.'
    • 始知灵验 - Means 'finally realized the effectiveness' or 'finally recognized the efficacy.'
    • 多置黐以涂壁 - Means 'set up many sticky substances to coat the walls.'
    • 夕有数蝙蝠 - Means 'in the evening, several large bats appeared.'
    • 大如鸡 - Means 'as big as chickens.'
    • 集其上 - Means 'gathered on them.'
    • 不得去 - Means 'could not leave.'
    • 杀之乃绝 - Means 'killed them to stop them.'
    • 屋檐下 - Means 'under the eaves.'
    • 已有数百人头髻 - Means 'there were already hundreds of human hair buns.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 宋高祖 - Refers to Emperor Gaozu of Song, the founder of the Song dynasty.
    • 永初 - The era name of Emperor Gaozu's reign.
    • 张春 - A historical figure, the governor of Wuchang.
    • 邪魅 - Refers to demons or evil spirits, common in Chinese folklore and religious beliefs.
    • 巫 - Refers to a witch or shaman, who performs rituals and communicates with spirits.
    • 黐 - A sticky substance used to trap insects or small animals.
    • 蝙蝠 - Refers to bats, which are often associated with superstition and the supernatural in Chinese culture.

    Proper Nouns

    • 宋高祖 - Emperor Gaozu of Song.
    • 永初 - The era name of Emperor Gaozu.
    • 张春 - Governor of Wuchang.
    • 王僧虔 - A historical figure mentioned in the text.
    • 淮南郡 - The Huainan prefecture.
    • 宋初 - The early period of the Song dynasty.

    In the early years of Emperor Gaozu's Yongchun era, Zhang Chun was the governor of Wuchang when a woman was about to get married. Just before she mounted the carriage, she suddenly lost her mind. She went outside and began to beat people while riding on clouds, saying she did not want to marry an ordinary person. The witch said it was a demon, and they took her to the riverside, struck drums, and performed rituals to treat her. Zhang Chun thought they were deceiving the people and set a deadline to capture the demon. Later, a green snake came to the witch's place, and they nailed it to the head with a large nail. By noon, a large turtle appeared from the river and knelt in front of them. They wrote a red character on its back and sent it back into the river. At dusk, a large white crocodile emerged from the river, sinking and floating intermittently, followed by the turtle which urged it on. The crocodile knew it was going to die and bravely entered the tent to say goodbye to the woman. She cried, saying, "I have lost my chance at marriage." From then on, she gradually recovered. Someone asked the witch, "What did the demon turn into?" The witch replied, "The snake was the messenger, the turtle was the matchmaker, and the crocodile was its counterpart. All of these were demons." Zhang Chun finally recognized the effectiveness of the ritual.

    In the early period of the Song dynasty, in the Huainan prefecture, there was something that shaved people's hair. Emperor Gaozu said, "I know what it is." He set up many sticky substances to coat the walls. In the evening, several large bats, as big as chickens, gathered on them and could not leave. They killed them to stop them. Under the eaves, there were already hundreds of human hair buns. [100]

    有贵人亡后，永兴令王奉先梦与之相对，如平生。奉先问：“还有情色乎？”答云：某日至其家问奷。后觉，问其婢，云：“此日魇梦郎君来。”
    　　徐羡之为王雄少傅主簿，梦父祚之谓曰：“汝从今已后，勿渡朱雀桁，当贵。”羡之后行半桁，忆先人梦，回马，而以此除主簿。后果为宰相。
    　　吴郡张茂度在益州时，忽有人道朝廷诛徐羡之、傅亮、谢晦三人，遂传之纷纭。张推问道：“造言之主，何由言此？”答曰：“实无所承，恍惚不知言之耳！”张鞭之，传者遂息。后乃验。
    　　景平元年，曲阿有一人病死，见父于天上。父谓曰：“汝算录正余八年，若此限竟，死便入罪谪中。吾比欲安处汝，职局无缺者，惟有雷公缺。当启以补其职。”即奏按入内，便得充此任。令至辽东行雨，乘露车，中有水，东西灌洒。未至，于中路复被符至辽西。事毕还，见父，苦求还，云：“不乐处职。”父遣去，遂得苏活。 [101]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [101]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 如平生 - Means 'as if in life,' indicating a lifelike dream.
    • 勿渡朱雀桁 - Means 'do not cross the Zhuque Bridge,' a prophetic warning.
    • 不乐处职 - Means 'does not enjoy the position,' expressing dissatisfaction.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 贵人 - Refers to a nobleman or high-ranking official.
    • 永兴令 - Refers to the magistrate of Yongxing, a place name.
    • 朱雀桁 - Refers to the Zhuque Bridge, a significant bridge in ancient China.
    • 雷公 - Refers to the Thunder God in Chinese mythology.
    • 景平元年 - The first year of the Jingping era, a period in Chinese history.
    • 曲阿 - A place name, present-day Jurong, Jiangsu Province.

    Proper Nouns

    • 王奉先 - A historical figure, the magistrate of Yongxing.
    • 徐羡之 - A historical figure, a high-ranking official.
    • 王雄 - A historical figure, a nobleman.
    • 张茂度 - A historical figure, an official in Yizhou.
    • 辽东 - A historical region, present-day Liaoning Province.
    • 辽西 - A historical region, present-day western Liaoning Province.

    A nobleman had passed away, and the magistrate of Yongxing, Wang Fengxian, dreamed of being with him as if in life. Wang Fengxian asked, "Do you still have feelings for me?" The nobleman replied, "On a certain day, visit my home to inquire about something." After waking up, Wang Fengxian asked his maid, who said, "That day, she had a nightmare about the young master coming."

    Xu Xianzhi, the deputy to the younger uncle of Wang Xiong, dreamed that his father Zuo said to him, "From now on, do not cross the Zhuque Bridge, you will become noble." Later, Xu Xianzhi walked halfway across the bridge, remembered his father's dream, turned his horse around, and was appointed as deputy. He later became the prime minister.

    In Yizhou, Zhang Maodu suddenly heard that the court would punish Xu Xianzhi, Fu Liang, and Xie Hui. The news spread widely. Zhang Maodu asked the source, "Who is the person who said this?" The source replied, "I don't know where I heard it, it just came to mind!" Zhang Maodu whipped him, and the rumor stopped. Later, it was proven true.

    In the first year of Jingping, in Qu'a, a person died and saw his father in heaven. His father said to him, "Your fate is recorded for eight more years. If this limit is reached, you will enter the realm of punishment. I have always wanted to place you in a good position. There is no vacancy, only the position of Thunder God is open. I will recommend you to fill this position." Immediately, he was appointed and took up the role. He was sent to Liaodong to bring rain, riding a cart filled with water, sprinkling it east and west. Before reaching, he was ordered to go to Liaoxi. After completing the task, he returned to see his father, who pleaded to return, saying he did not enjoy the position. His father sent him away, and he revived. [101]

    元嘉初，散骑常侍刘俊家在丹阳郡。后尝闲居，而天大骤雨。见门前有三小儿，皆可六七岁，相牵狡狯，而并不沾濡。俊疑非人。俄见共争一瓠壶子，俊引弹弹之，正中壶，霍然不见。俊得壶，因挂阁边。明日，有一妇人入门，执壶而泣，俊问之，对曰：“此是小儿物，不知何由在此？”俊具语所以，妇持壶埋儿墓前。间一日，又见向小儿持来门侧，举之，笑语俊曰：“阿侬已复得壶矣。”言终而隐。
    　　元嘉九年，征北参军明裔之有一从者，夜眠，大魇。裔之自往唤之，顷间不能应。又失其头髻，三日乃寤，说云：“被三人捉足，一人髻之。忽梦见一道人，以丸药与之，如桐子。令以水服之。”及寤，手中有药，服之遂瘥。
    　　元嘉九年，南阳乐遐尝在内坐。忽闻空中有人呼其夫妇名，甚急，半夜乃止，殊自惊惧。后数日，妇屋后还，忽举体衣服总是血，未一月，而夫妇相継病卒。 [102]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [102]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 元嘉初 - Refers to the early years of the Yungang era, a period in the Southern Song Dynasty.
    • 散骑常侍 - A high-ranking imperial official, often translated as 'Attendant-in-Ordinary' or 'Imperial Attendant.'
    • 天大骤雨 - A heavy rainstorm, emphasizing the intensity of the weather.
    • 相牵狡狯 - Playing together, likely in a playful or mischievous manner.
    • 并不沾濡 - Not getting wet, suggesting the children were somehow protected from the rain.
    • 阿侬 - A colloquial term for 'I' or 'me,' often used in the context of a child or a humble speaker.
    • 霍然不见 - Suddenly disappearing, indicating a supernatural or magical event.
    • 大魇 - A severe nightmare, causing great distress.
    • 阿谁 - A question word meaning 'who' or 'whom.'
    • 阿谁捉足 - Who is holding my feet, a phrase used in the dream.
    • 阿谁髻之 - Who is braiding my hair, another phrase used in the dream.
    • 阿谁为我解之 - Who will help me, a plea for assistance.

    During the early years of the Yungang era, the Attendant-in-Ordinary Liu Jun lived in the DanYang Prefecture. One day, while he was at home, there was a sudden heavy rainstorm. He saw three young boys, each about six or seven years old, playing together in front of his house, but they were not getting wet. Liu Jun suspected they were not ordinary children. Soon, he saw them fighting over a gourd-shaped vase. Liu Jun shot an arrow at them, hitting the vase directly, and they disappeared suddenly. Liu Jun took the vase and hung it on the side of his house. The next day, a woman came to his door, holding the vase and crying. Liu Jun asked her what was wrong, and she replied, "This is my son's possession. I don't know how it ended up here." Liu Jun told her the whole story, and the woman took the vase and buried it in front of her son's grave. A few days later, the same children came back and brought the vase to the side of Liu Jun's house. They picked it up and said to him, "We have gotten our vase back." They then vanished.

    In the ninth year of the Yungang era, the Assistant Military Commander Ming Yizhi had a servant who had a terrible nightmare one night. Ming Yizhi went to wake him, but he could not respond. The servant also lost his hairpin. After three days, he woke up and said, "Three people grabbed my feet, and one of them braided my hair. Suddenly, I dreamed of a Taoist priest who gave me a pill, as big as a tangerine seed. He told me to take it with water." When he woke up, he found the pill in his hand, and after taking it, he recovered.

    In the ninth year of the Yungang era, a man named Yue Xia from Nanyang was sitting inside his house. Suddenly, he heard someone calling out the names of his wife and himself in the air, and it was very urgent. The calls stopped at midnight, but he was very frightened. A few days later, his wife returned from the back of the house, and her clothes were completely covered in blood. Within a month, both he and his wife fell ill and died. [102]

    元嘉中，交州刺史太原王征始拜，乘车出行。闻其前铮铮有声，见一輀车当路，而余人不见，至州遂亡。
    　　元嘉中，益州刺史吉翰迁为南徐州。先于蜀中载一青牛，下常自乘，恒于目前养视。翰遘疾多日，牛亦不肯食。及亡，牛流涕滂沱。吉氏丧未还都，先遣驱牛向宅。牛不肯行。知其异，即待丧。丧既下船，便随去。
    　　吉米翰从弟名礐石，先作檀道济参军。尝病，因见人着千衣，前来揖云：“特来将迎。”礐石厚为设求免，鬼曰：“感君延接，当为少停。”乃不复见。礐石渐差。后丁艰，还寿阳，复见鬼，曰：“迎使寻至，君便可束装。”礐石曰：“君前已留怀，今复得见愍否？”鬼曰：“前召欲相使役，故停耳。今泰山屈君为主簿，又使随至，不可辞也。”便见车马传教，油戟罗列于前。指示家人，家人莫见也。礐石介书呼亲友告别，语笑之中，便奄然而尽。 [103]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [103]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 元嘉中 - Refers to the Yuanjia period, the reign of Emperor Wen of Song (424-453 AD).
    • 交州刺史 - Governor of Jiaozhou, an administrative region in southern China.
    • 太原王征 - Historical figure, possibly a governor.
    • 輀車 - Funeral carriage.
    • 下常自乘 - Literally 'usually rides it,' but in context, refers to the cow being used by someone.
    • 恒于目前养视 - Means 'always kept in sight and cared for.'
    • 遘疾多日 - Means 'suffered from illness for many days.'
    • 流涕滂沱 - Describes tears flowing like rain, indicating intense emotion.
    • 吉氏丧未还都 - Refers to the funeral procession not having returned to the capital.
    • 先遣驱牛向宅 - Means 'sent ahead to drive the cow toward the house.'
    • 牛不肯行 - Means 'the cow did not want to go.'
    • 知其异 - Means 'knew it was unusual.'
    • 待丧 - Means 'waited for the funeral procession.'
    • 下船 - Means 'got off the boat.'
    • 随去 - Means 'followed along.'
    • 从弟 - Refers to a younger brother.
    • 参军 - Refers to a military advisor.
    • 病 - Means 'illness.'
    • 着千衣 - Refers to a ghost wearing a thousand-layered robe.
    • 将迎 - Means 'to come to take away.'
    • 厚为设求免 - Means 'offered generous gifts to beg for mercy.'
    • 感君延接 - Means 'grateful for your hospitality.'
    • 少停 - Means 'to stay a little longer.'
    • 泰山 - Refers to Mount Tai, often associated with the afterlife.
    • 主簿 - Refers to an official position.
    • 束装 - Means 'to pack one's belongings.'
    • 介书 - Means 'to write a letter.'
    • 告别 - Means 'to say goodbye.'
    • 奄然而尽 - Means 'died suddenly.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 元嘉中 - The Yuanjia period, a specific era in Chinese history.
    • 交州刺史 - An administrative position in the Southern Dynasties.
    • 太原王征 - A historical figure, possibly a governor.
    • 吉翰 - Another historical figure, possibly a governor or official.
    • 南徐州 - An administrative region in southern China.
    • 青牛 - A black or dark-colored cow, often associated with Daoist symbolism.
    • 吉氏 - Likely refers to the family name of the official.
    • 寿阳 - A city in Anhui Province, China.
    • 檀道济 - A famous general of the Liu Song dynasty.
    • 泰山 - Mount Tai, a sacred mountain in Shandong Province, often associated with the afterlife and the underworld.

    Proper Nouns

    • 元嘉 - The reign period of Emperor Wen of Song (424-453 AD).
    • 交州 - An administrative region in southern China.
    • 太原王征 - A historical figure, possibly a governor.
    • 吉翰 - Another historical figure, possibly a governor or official.
    • 南徐州 - An administrative region in southern China.
    • 青牛 - A black or dark-colored cow.
    • 寿阳 - A city in Anhui Province, China.
    • 檀道济 - A famous general of the Liu Song dynasty.
    • 泰山 - Mount Tai, a sacred mountain in Shandong Province.

    During the Yuanjia period, Wang Zheng of Taiyuan, the governor of Jiaozhou, was newly appointed and set out in a carriage. He heard a loud clanging sound ahead and saw a funeral carriage blocking the road, while others did not see it. Upon arriving at the state, he died.

    During the Yuanjia period, Ji Han, the governor of Yizhou, was transferred to become the governor of Nanzhou. He had previously loaded a black cow in Shu, which he often rode, and always kept it in sight and cared for. Ji Han suffered from illness for many days, and the cow also refused to eat. Upon his death, the cow wept profusely. The funeral procession of the Ji family had not yet returned to the capital, so they sent someone ahead to drive the cow toward the house. The cow refused to go. Knowing it was unusual, they waited for the funeral procession. Once the procession got off the boat, the cow followed along.

    Ji Han's younger brother, named Kuoshizhi, was originally a military advisor to Tan Daoji. Once, while ill, he saw a ghost wearing a thousand-layered robe, who bowed and said, "I have come to take you away." Kuoshizhi offered generous gifts to beg for mercy. The ghost said, "Grateful for your hospitality, I will stay a little longer." The ghost then disappeared. Kuoshizhi gradually recovered. Later, during a period of mourning, he returned to Shouyang and saw the ghost again, who said, "The welcoming official is about to arrive; you should pack your belongings." Kuoshizhi said, "You have already stayed before, can I see you again?" The ghost replied, "I was called to serve you earlier, so I stayed. Now, Mount Tai has appointed you as the chief clerk, and they have sent me to accompany you; you cannot refuse." Kuoshizhi then saw a carriage and horses, with oil torches and halberds arranged in front. He pointed to his family members, but they could not see the ghost. Kuoshizhi wrote a letter to his relatives, bidding farewell in a conversation filled with laughter, and suddenly died. [103]

    赵泰，字文和，清河贝邱人。公府辟不就，精进典籍，乡党称名。年三十五，宋太始五年七月十三日夜半，忽心痛而死，心上微暖，身体屈伸。停尸十日，气从咽喉如雷鸣，眼开，索水饮，饮讫便起。说初死时，有二人乘黄马，从兵二人，但言捉将去。二人扶两腋东行，不知几里，便见大城如锡铁崔嵬。从城西门入，见官府舍，有二重黑门，数十梁瓦屋。男女当五六十，主吏着皂单衫，将泰名在第三十。须臾将入，府君西坐，断勘姓名。复将南入黑门，一人绛衣，坐大屋下，以次呼名前，问生时所行事，有何罪故，行何功德，作何善行。言者各各不同。主者言：“许汝等辞。恒遣六部都录使者，常在人间疏记人所作善恶，以相检校。人死有三恶道，杀生祷祠最重。 [104]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [104]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 公府辟不就 - Government office invitation declined; Zhao Tai refused a position offered by a high-ranking official.
    • 精進典籍 - Diligent study of classical texts.
    • 鄉黨稱名 - Known by name in the local community; Zhao Tai was well-respected.
    • 心痛而死 - Sudden death due to severe chest pain.
    • 心上微暖，身體屈伸 - Heart still slightly warm, body able to move after death.
    • 停尸十日 - Body kept for ten days after death.
    • 氣從咽喉如雷鳴 - Sound of air from throat, likened to thunder.
    • 眼開，索水飲 - Eyes opened, requested water to drink.
    • 初死時，有二人乘黃馬，從兵二人 - Apparition of two men riding yellow horses and accompanied by two soldiers when first dead.
    • 大城如錫鐵崔嵬 - Large city made of metal, steep and towering structures.
    • 官府舍，有二重黑門，數十梁瓦屋 - Government office building with two layers of black doors and several large tile-roofed houses.
    • 男女當五六十 - About fifty to sixty men and women present.
    • 主吏著皂單衫 - Officials wearing black single-layered robes.
    • 府君西坐，斷勘姓名 - Judge sitting on the west side, examining names.
    • 南入黑門 - Entering a black door to the south.
    • 一人絳衣，坐大屋下 - Person wearing red clothes sitting under a large house.
    • 以次呼名前 - Calling people's names in order.
    • 生時所行事 - Actions taken during one's lifetime.
    • 有何罪故 - Questions about any sins committed.
    • 行何功德 - Questions about any good deeds performed.
    • 作何善行 - Questions about any virtuous actions taken.
    • 六部都錄使者 - Officials who record people's actions in six departments.
    • 三惡道 - Three evil paths in Buddhist cosmology: hell, hungry ghosts, and animals.
    • 殺生禱祠最重 - Killing and offering sacrifices are the most serious offenses.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 趙泰，字文和 - Zhao Tai, with the courtesy name Wenhe, a historical figure from the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 清河貝邱人 - Zhao Tai was from Beiqiu, Qinghe, a region in modern-day Shandong province.
    • 宋太始五年 - The fifth year of the Taishi era of the Song dynasty (469 CE).
    • 東行 - Moving eastward, a common direction in Chinese cosmology and geography.
    • 大城如錫鐵崔嵬 - Metaphorical description of a city made of metal, symbolizing a place of great importance or power.
    • 官府舍 - Government office building, a common setting in Chinese literature and historical records.
    • 府君 - Respectful title for a judge or official.
    • 六部都錄使者 - Officials who record people's actions in six departments, a reference to the bureaucratic system in ancient China.
    • 三惡道 - Three evil paths in Buddhist cosmology: hell, hungry ghosts, and animals.
    • 殺生禱祠 - Act of killing and offering sacrifices, considered serious offenses in Buddhist and Daoist traditions.

    Proper Nouns

    • 趙泰 - Zhao Tai, the main subject of the text.
    • 文和 - Wenhe, Zhao Tai's courtesy name.
    • 清河貝邱 - Beiqiu, Qinghe, the place of Zhao Tai's origin.
    • 宋太始五年 - The fifth year of the Taishi era of the Song dynasty (469 CE).
    • 府君 - Respectful title for a judge or official.
    • 六部都錄使者 - Officials who record people's actions in six departments.
    • 三惡道 - Three evil paths in Buddhist cosmology: hell, hungry ghosts, and animals.
    • 殺生禱祠 - Act of killing and offering sacrifices.

    Zhao Tai, courtesy name Wenhe, was from Beiqiu, Qinghe. He declined an invitation to serve in a government office, instead diligently studying classical texts, and was well-known and respected in his community. At the age of thirty-five, on the night of July 13, 469 CE during the Taishi era of the Song dynasty, he suddenly died of severe chest pain. His heart remained slightly warm, and his body could still move. His body was kept for ten days, during which air came from his throat like the sound of thunder, his eyes opened, and he asked for water to drink. After drinking, he rose up. He recounted that when he first died, two men riding yellow horses and accompanied by two soldiers appeared, saying they were taking him away. They carried him under his armpits and walked eastward for an unknown distance until they saw a large city made of metal with steep and towering structures. They entered through the western gate of the city, where they saw a government office building with two layers of black doors and several large tile-roofed houses. There were about fifty to sixty men and women present, and the officials wore black single-layered robes. Zhao Tai's name was listed as the thirtieth. Soon, they were led inside, where a judge sat on the west side and examined names. They then entered another black door to the south, where a person wearing red clothes sat under a large house and called out names in order, asking about actions taken during their lifetimes, any sins committed, good deeds performed, and virtuous actions taken. The responses varied. The official said, "You may speak freely. We constantly send six officials to record people's actions, both good and bad, for inspection. After death, there are three evil paths, and the most serious offenses are killing and offering sacrifices." [104]

    奉佛持五戒十善，慈心布施，生在福舍，安稳无为。”泰答：“一无所为，永不犯恶。”断问都竟，使为水官监作吏，将千余人，接沙着岸上。昼夜勤苦，啼泣悔言：“生时不作善，今堕在此处。”后转水官都督，总知诸狱事。给马，东到地狱按行。复到泥犁地狱，男子六千人，有火树，纵广五十余步，高千丈，四边皆有剑，树上然火，其下十十五五，堕火剑上，贯其身体。云：“此人咒咀骂詈，夺人财物，假伤良善。”泰见父母及一弟在此狱中涕泣。见二人赍文书来，敕狱吏，言“有三人，其家事佛，为有寺中悬幡盖，烧香，转《法华经》，咒愿救解生时罪过，出就福舍。”已见自然衣服，往诣一门，云“开光大舍”。有三重门，皆白壁赤柱。此三人即入门，见大殿珍宝耀日，堂前有二狮子并伏，负一金玉床，云名“狮子之座”。 [105]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [105]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 奉佛持五戒十善 - Compound phrase meaning 'to follow Buddhism and uphold the Five Precepts and Ten Good Deeds,' indicating adherence to Buddhist moral guidelines.
    • 慈心布施 - Means 'to practice compassion and generosity,' reflecting core Buddhist virtues.
    • 生在福舍，安稳无为 - Translates to 'to be born in a place of happiness and security, free from turmoil,' describing a state of blissful existence.
    • 一无所为，永不犯恶 - Means 'to do nothing wrong and never commit evil,' expressing a commitment to moral purity.
    • 水官监作吏 - Refers to an official position overseeing water-related duties or prison labor.
    • 接沙着岸上 - Means 'to pile up sand on the shore,' likely referring to a labor task.
    • 啼泣悔言 - Translates to 'to weep and regret,' indicating remorseful lamentation.
    • 生时不作善 - Means 'did not do good deeds in life,' reflecting on past actions.
    • 泥犁地狱 - Refers to a specific hell realm in Buddhist cosmology, often translated as 'hell of burning.'
    • 咒咀骂詈 - Means 'to curse and scold,' describing harmful speech.
    • 夺人财物 - Translates to 'to steal people's property,' indicating theft.
    • 假伤良善 - Means 'to harm the innocent,' suggesting malicious actions.
    • 开光大舍 - Refers to a grand hall of enlightenment or a place of salvation.
    • 白壁赤柱 - Describes a grand structure with white walls and red pillars.
    • 狮子之座 - Refers to a throne or seat associated with the Buddha, symbolizing supreme authority and enlightenment.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 五戒十善 - The Five Precepts and Ten Good Deeds are fundamental Buddhist moral guidelines, emphasizing ethical conduct.
    • 福舍 - A place of happiness and security, often associated with a blissful afterlife in Buddhist cosmology.
    • 水官监作吏 - Refers to a specific role in the bureaucratic system, likely related to water management or prison labor.
    • 泥犁地狱 - One of the hells in Buddhist cosmology, characterized by intense suffering and punishment for specific sins.
    • 咒咀骂詈 - Harmful speech, including cursing and scolding, which is considered a grave sin in Buddhism.
    • 夺人财物 - Theft, which is a serious offense in Buddhist ethics.
    • 假伤良善 - Malicious actions against the innocent, which are condemned in Buddhist teachings.
    • 开光大舍 - A grand hall of enlightenment, often associated with a place of salvation or spiritual transformation.
    • 狮子之座 - A throne or seat associated with the Buddha, symbolizing supreme authority and enlightenment.

    Proper Nouns

    • 五戒十善 - The Five Precepts and Ten Good Deeds, fundamental Buddhist moral guidelines.
    • 福舍 - A place of happiness and security, often associated with a blissful afterlife in Buddhist cosmology.
    • 水官监作吏 - A specific role in the bureaucratic system, likely related to water management or prison labor.
    • 泥犁地狱 - One of the hells in Buddhist cosmology, characterized by intense suffering and punishment.
    • 开光大舍 - A grand hall of enlightenment, often associated with a place of salvation or spiritual transformation.
    • 狮子之座 - A throne or seat associated with the Buddha, symbolizing supreme authority and enlightenment.

    Following the pre-drafting research and the translation plan, here is the final, polished translation of the source text:

    Adhering to Buddhism and upholding the Five Precepts and Ten Good Deeds, practicing compassion and generosity, one is born in a place of happiness and security, living in peace and tranquility. Tai replied, "I do nothing wrong and never commit evil." After questioning him thoroughly, they appointed him as a water official overseeing prison labor, where he supervised over a thousand people, piling up sand on the shore. Day and night, he worked tirelessly, weeping and regretting, saying, "I did not do good deeds in life, and now I have fallen into this place." Later, he was promoted to the position of water official supervisor, overseeing all prison affairs. He was given a horse and sent east to inspect the hells. Upon arriving at the Hell of Burning, he saw six thousand men, with a blazing fire tree stretching fifty to sixty steps in length and width, reaching a height of a thousand feet. Swords were placed on all sides of the tree, and fire burned on top of it. Below, people fell in groups of ten or fifteen onto the fiery swords, piercing through their bodies. It was said, "These individuals cursed and scolded others, stole people's property, and harmed the innocent." Tai saw his parents and one of his brothers in this hell, weeping. Two people carrying documents arrived, instructing the prison officials, "There are three people whose families practice Buddhism. They have hung banners and canopies in the temple, burned incense, and recited the Lotus Sutra, praying to save them from their sins and lead them to a place of happiness." These three saw natural clothing appear and went to a gate, which was called "The Grand Hall of Enlightenment." The gate had three layers, with white walls and red pillars. The three entered, seeing a grand hall with treasures shining under the sun. Before the hall were two lions lying down, carrying a golden and jade bed, known as "The Lion's Throne." [105]

    见一大人，身可长丈余，姿颜金色，项有白光，坐此床上。沙门立侍甚众，四座名“真人菩萨”。见泰山府君来作礼，泰问吏：“何人？”吏曰：“此名佛，天上天下，度人之师。”便闻佛言：“今欲度此恶道中及诸地狱中人，皆令出。”应时云有万九千人，一时得出地狱。实时见呼十人，当上生天，有车马迎之，升虚空而去。复见一城云纵广二百里，名为“受変形城”。云生来不闻道法，而地狱考治已毕者，当于此城更受変报。入北门，见数千百土屋，中央有瓦屋，广五十余步，下有五百余吏，对录人名作善恶事状，受所変身形之路，各从其所趋去：杀生者当作蜉蝣虫，朝生夕死；若为人，常短命。偷盗者作猪羊，身屠，肉偿人。淫逸者作鹄鹜蛇身。恶舌者作鸱鸮鸺鹠恶声，人闻皆咒令死。 [106]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [106]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 見一大人，身可長丈余，姿顏金色，項有白光 - Describes a figure of great stature and divine appearance. '身可長丈余' means 'the body can be over ten feet tall,' indicating a supernatural or divine being. '姿顏金色' means 'golden complexion,' suggesting a radiant, otherworldly appearance. '項有白光' means 'a white light emanates from the neck,' further emphasizing the divine nature.
    • 坐此床上 - Literally 'sitting on this bed,' but in a religious context, it likely refers to a throne or a seat of honor.
    • 沙門立侍甚眾 - '沙門' refers to Buddhist monks or ascetics. '立侍甚眾' means 'many standing in attendance,' indicating a large number of followers or devotees.
    • 四座名‘真人菩萨’ - '四座' means 'the four seats,' and '真人菩萨' refers to the Four Truthful Bodhisattvas, who are important figures in Buddhism.
    • 見泰山府君來作禮 - '泰山府君' is the Lord of Mount Tai, a deity in Chinese folk religion. '作禮' means 'to pay respects,' indicating a formal act of reverence.
    • 泰問吏 - '泰' refers to the Lord of Mount Tai, and '問吏' means 'to ask the officials,' indicating a query to the servants or attendants.
    • 此名佛，天上天下，度人之師 - '此名佛' means 'this is called Buddha,' and '天上天下，度人之師' means 'the teacher who guides people in heaven and on earth,' emphasizing the Buddha's universal role.
    • 便聞佛言 - '便聞佛言' means 'then the Buddha spoke,' indicating the beginning of a discourse.
    • 今欲度此恶道中及诸地狱中人，皆令出 - '度此恶道中及诸地狱中人' means 'to liberate those in the evil path and the various hells,' indicating the Buddha's intention to save beings suffering in these realms. '皆令出' means 'to make all of them emerge,' indicating the liberation of these beings.
    • 应时云有万九千人，一时得出地狱 - '应时云有万九千人' means 'at the time, it was said there were 19,000 people,' and '一时得出地狱' means 'all emerged from the hell at once,' indicating the immediate liberation of a large number of beings.
    • 实时见呼十人，当上生天 - '实时见呼十人' means 'immediately, ten people were seen being called,' and '当上生天' means 'to ascend to heaven,' indicating the immediate ascension of these ten individuals.
    • 有车马迎之，升虚空而去 - '有车马迎之' means 'they were met by chariots and horses,' and '升虚空而去' means 'ascended into the void and departed,' indicating a grand and divine departure.
    • 复见一城云纵广二百里，名为‘受変形城’ - '复见一城' means 'then another city was seen,' '云纵广二百里' means 'it was said to be 200 miles in length and width,' and '名为‘受変形城’' means 'named the 'City of Transformation,' indicating a place where beings undergo transformation.
    • 云生来不闻道法，而地狱考治已毕者，当于此城更受変报 - '云生来不闻道法' means 'it was said that those who have not heard the Dharma,' '而地狱考治已毕者' means 'and those whose punishment in hell has ended,' and '当于此城更受変报' means 'will undergo further transformation in this city,' indicating a place for further spiritual development.
    • 入北门，见数千百土屋，中央有瓦屋，广五十余步，下有五百余吏，对录人名作善恶事状，受所変身形之路，各从其所趋去 - '入北门' means 'entering the northern gate,' '见数千百土屋' means 'seeing thousands of earthen houses,' '中央有瓦屋' means 'with a tiled house in the center,' '广五十余步' means 'about 50 paces wide,' '下有五百余吏' means 'with more than 500 officials below,' '对录人名作善恶事状' means 'recording names and deeds of good and evil,' '受所変身形之路' means 'determining the path of transformation into different forms,' and '各从其所趋去' means 'each going their own way,' indicating a place where beings are judged and transformed based on their deeds.
    • 杀生者当作蜉蝣虫，朝生夕死 - '杀生者' means 'those who kill,' '当作蜉蝣虫' means 'will be transformed into mayflies,' and '朝生夕死' means 'living only one day,' indicating the short lifespan of mayflies as a consequence of killing.
    • 若为人，常短命 - '若为人' means 'if they become humans,' and '常短命' means 'they will often have short lives,' indicating a consequence of past actions.
    • 偷盗者作猪羊，身屠，肉偿人 - '偷盗者' means 'those who steal,' '作猪羊' means 'will be transformed into pigs and sheep,' '身屠' means 'their bodies will be slaughtered,' and '肉偿人' means 'their meat will be used to pay off debts,' indicating a cycle of retribution.
    • 淫逸者作鹄鹜蛇身 - '淫逸者' means 'those who indulge in lust,' '作鹄鹜蛇身' means 'will be transformed into cranes, ducks, and snakes,' indicating a transformation into lower forms due to immoral behavior.
    • 恶舌者作鸱鸮鸺鹠恶声，人闻皆咒令死 - '恶舌者' means 'those who speak evil,' '作鸱鸮鸺鹠恶声' means 'will be transformed into owls and screech owls with evil voices,' and '人闻皆咒令死' means 'people will curse them to death when they hear them,' indicating the consequences of spreading harmful words.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 泰山府君 - The Lord of Mount Tai, a deity in Chinese folk religion, often associated with the afterlife and judgment.
    • 真人菩萨 - The Four Truthful Bodhisattvas, important figures in Buddhism who embody wisdom and compassion.
    • 佛 - The Buddha, the central figure in Buddhism, known as the teacher who guides people in heaven and on earth.
    • 地狱 - Hell, a realm of suffering and punishment in Buddhist cosmology.
    • 受変形城 - The City of Transformation, a place where beings undergo further spiritual development after their initial punishment in hell.
    • 蜉蝣 - Mayflies, known for their short lifespan, often used as a metaphor for the fleeting nature of life.
    • 鸱鸮鸺鹠 - Owls and screech owls, often associated with evil and curses in Chinese folklore.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • 見一大人，身可長丈余，姿顏金色，項有白光，坐此床上 - This long descriptive phrase must be read as a single unit, describing a divine figure.
    • 沙門立侍甚眾 - This phrase describes the presence of many Buddhist monks or ascetics.
    • 四座名‘真人菩萨’ - This phrase identifies the four seats as belonging to the Four Truthful Bodhisattvas.
    • 見泰山府君來作禮，泰問吏 - This sequence describes the Lord of Mount Tai paying respects and asking the officials.
    • 此名佛，天上天下，度人之師 - This phrase identifies the figure as the Buddha and emphasizes his role as a teacher.
    • 便聞佛言，今欲度此恶道中及诸地狱中人，皆令出 - This sequence describes the Buddha speaking and expressing his intention to liberate beings.
    • 应时云有万九千人，一时得出地狱 - This phrase indicates the immediate liberation of a large number of beings.
    • 实时见呼十人，当上生天，有车马迎之，升虚空而去 - This sequence describes the immediate ascension of ten individuals to heaven.
    • 复见一城云纵广二百里，名为‘受変形城’ - This phrase introduces a new city where beings undergo further transformation.
    • 云生来不闻道法，而地狱考治已毕者，当于此城更受変报 - This phrase explains the purpose of the City of Transformation.
    • 入北门，见数千百土屋，中央有瓦屋，广五十余步，下有五百余吏，对录人名作善恶事状，受所変身形之路，各从其所趋去 - This long sequence describes the layout and function of the City of Transformation.
    • 杀生者当作蜉蝣虫，朝生夕死；若为人，常短命 - This sequence describes the consequences of killing.

    A majestic figure appeared, his body towering over ten feet tall, with a golden complexion and a white light emanating from his neck, seated upon this bed. Many Buddhist monks stood in attendance, and the four seats were occupied by the Four Truthful Bodhisattvas. The Lord of Mount Tai approached to pay respects, and he asked the officials, "Who is this?" The officials replied, "This is the Buddha, the teacher who guides people in heaven and on earth." The Buddha then spoke, saying, "I wish to liberate those in the evil path and the various hells, and make them all emerge." At that moment, it was said there were 19,000 people, all of whom emerged from hell at once. Immediately, ten people were seen being called, destined to ascend to heaven, where they were met by chariots and horses, and ascended into the void and departed. Another city was then seen, said to be 200 miles in length and width, named the "City of Transformation." It was said that those who have not heard the Dharma and whose punishment in hell has ended will undergo further transformation in this city. Entering the northern gate, one saw thousands of earthen houses, with a tiled house in the center, about 50 paces wide, and more than 500 officials below, recording names and deeds of good and evil, determining the path of transformation into different forms, each going their own way: those who kill will be transformed into mayflies, living only one day; if they become humans, they will often have short lives. Those who steal will be transformed into pigs and sheep, their bodies will be slaughtered, and their meat will be used to pay off debts. Those who indulge in lust will be transformed into cranes, ducks, and snakes. Those who speak evil will be transformed into owls and screech owls with evil voices, and people will curse them to death when they hear them. [106]

    抵债者为驴马牛鱼鳖之属。大屋下有地房北向，一户南向。呼从北户，又出南户者，皆変身形作鸟兽。又见一城，纵广百里，其中瓦屋，安居快乐。云生时不作恶，亦不为善，当在鬼趣，千岁得出为人。又见一城，广有五千余步，名为“地中”。罚谪者不堪苦痛。男女五六万，皆裸形无服，饥困相扶。见泰，叩头啼哭。泰按行毕还，主者问：“地狱如法否？卿无罪，故相凂为水官都督。不尔，与狱中人无异。”泰问：“人生何以为乐？”主者言：“唯奉佛弟子精进不犯禁戒为乐耳。”又问：“未奉佛时，罪过山积，今奉佛法，其过得除否？”曰：“皆除。”主者又召都录使者，问：“赵泰何故死？”来使开縢检年纪之籍，云：“有算三十年，横为恶鬼所取，今遣还家。”由是大小发意奉佛，为祖、父母及弟悬幡盖、诵《法华经》作福也。 [107]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [107]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 抵債者為驴馬牛魚鱉之屬 - Refers to those who pay off debts with various animals or aquatic creatures.
    • 大屋下有地房北向，一户南向 - Describes a house with rooms oriented in different directions.
    • 呼從北戶，又出南戶者，皆變身形作鳥獸 - Means that people who enter through the northern door and exit through the southern door transform into birds and beasts.
    • 又見一城，縱廣百里，其中瓦屋，安居快樂 - Describes a city that is one hundred miles in length and width, with tiled houses where people live happily.
    • 云生時不作惡，亦不為善，當在鬼趣，千歲得出為人 - States that those who neither do evil nor good deeds in life will be in the realm of ghosts and will take a thousand years to be reborn as humans.
    • 又見一城，廣有五千余步，名為「地中」 - Describes another city that is more than five thousand steps wide, named 'Earth's Center.'
    • 罰謫者不堪苦痛 - Refers to those who are punished and cannot bear the suffering.
    • 男女五六萬，皆裸形無服，飢困相扶 - Describes tens of thousands of men and women who are naked, hungry, and supporting each other.
    • 見泰，叩頭啼哭 - Means that they see S. T. and bow down, crying.
    • 泰按行畢還，主者問：「地獄如法否？卿無罪，故相凂為水官都督。不爾，與獄中人無異。」 - S. T. returns after inspecting, and the master asks if the hell is in accordance with the law, and since he is innocent, he is appointed as the water official supervisor; otherwise, he would be like the prisoners.
    • 泰問：「人生何以為樂？」主者言：「唯奉佛弟子精進不犯禁戒為樂耳。」 - S. T. asks what is the joy in life, and the master says that it is only in being a diligent Buddhist disciple who does not break the precepts.
    • 又問：「未奉佛時，罪過山積，今奉佛法，其過得除否？」曰：「皆除。」 - S. T. asks if the sins accumulated before becoming a Buddhist can be forgiven, and the answer is that they can all be forgiven.
    • 主者又召都錄使者，問：「趙泰何故死？」來使開縢檢年紀之籍，云：「有算三十年，橫為惡鬼所取，今遣還家。」 - The master calls the registrar and asks why S. T. died, and the registrar checks the records and says that he had thirty years left but was taken by an evil ghost, and now he is sent back home.
    • 由是大小發意奉佛，為祖、父母及弟懸幡蓋、誦《法華經》作福也。 - As a result, both young and old take up Buddhism, and they hang banners and umbrellas and recite the *Lotus Sutra* for their ancestors, parents, and younger siblings.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 抵債 - Refers to paying off debts, often with property or goods.
    • 大屋下有地房北向，一户南向 - Describes a traditional Chinese house layout.
    • 呼從北戶，又出南戶者，皆變身形作鳥獸 - Refers to a mythological or supernatural transformation.
    • 又見一城，縱廣百里，其中瓦屋，安居快樂 - Describes a utopian city.
    • 云生時不作惡，亦不為善，當在鬼趣，千歲得出為人 - Refers to the Buddhist concept of rebirth and karma.
    • 又見一城，廣有五千余步，名為「地中」 - Describes a hellish realm.
    • 罰謫者不堪苦痛 - Refers to punishment and suffering.
    • 男女五六萬，皆裸形無服，飢困相扶 - Describes a scene of extreme suffering.
    • 見泰，叩頭啼哭 - Refers to a ritual act of respect and supplication.
    • 泰按行畢還，主者問：「地獄如法否？卿無罪，故相凂為水官都督。不爾，與獄中人無異。」 - Refers to a bureaucratic or judicial system.
    • 泰問：「人生何以為樂？」主者言：「唯奉佛弟子精進不犯禁戒為樂耳。」 - Refers to Buddhist teachings and practices.
    • 又問：「未奉佛時，罪過山積，今奉佛法，其過得除否？」曰：「皆除。」 - Refers to the concept of forgiveness in Buddhism.
    • 主者又召都錄使者，問：「趙泰何故死？」來使開縢檢年紀之籍，云：「有算三十年，橫為惡鬼所取，今遣還家。」 - Refers to a record-keeping system in the afterlife.
    • 由是大小發意奉佛，為祖、父母及弟懸幡蓋、誦《法華經》作福也。 - Refers to Buddhist rituals and practices.

    Proper Nouns

    • 趙泰 - The name of the person being described.
    • 《法華經》 - The Lotus Sutra, a significant Buddhist text.

    Those who pay off debts with various animals or aquatic creatures are described. There is a large house with rooms oriented north and south. People who enter through the northern door and exit through the southern door transform into birds and beasts. Another city is seen, one hundred miles in length and width, with tiled houses where people live happily. It is said that those who neither do evil nor good deeds in life will be in the realm of ghosts and will take a thousand years to be reborn as humans. Another city is seen, more than five thousand steps wide, named "Earth's Center." Those who are punished cannot bear the suffering. Tens of thousands of men and women are naked, hungry, and supporting each other. They see S. T. and bow down, crying. S. T. returns after inspecting and the master asks if the hell is in accordance with the law, and since he is innocent, he is appointed as the water official supervisor; otherwise, he would be like the prisoners. S. T. asks what is the joy in life, and the master says that it is only in being a diligent Buddhist disciple who does not break the precepts. S. T. asks if the sins accumulated before becoming a Buddhist can be forgiven, and the answer is that they can all be forgiven. The master then calls the registrar and asks why S. T. died, and the registrar checks the records and says that he had thirty years left but was taken by an evil ghost, and now he is sent back home. As a result, both young and old take up Buddhism, and they hang banners and umbrellas and recite the Lotus Sutra for their ancestors, parents, and younger siblings. [107]

    蔡廓作豫章郡，水发。大儿始迎妇，在渚次。儿欲渡妇船，衣挂船头，遂堕水，即没。徐羡之作扬州，登敕两岸，厚赏渔人及昆仑，共寻觅，至二更不得。妇哀泣之间，彷佛如梦闻婿告之曰：“吾今在卿船下。”以告婢，婢白之，令水工没觅，果见坐在船下。初出水，颜色如平生。
    　　宋永兴县吏钟道，得重病初差，情欲倍常。先乐白鹤墟中女子，至是犹存想焉。忽见此女子振衣而来，即与燕好。是后数至。道曰：“吾甚欲鸡舌香。”女曰：“何难。”乃掏香满手以授道，道邀女同含咀之。女曰：“我气素芳，不假此。”女子出户，狗忽见随。咋杀之，乃是老獭，口香即獭粪，顿觉臭秽。 [108]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [108]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 水发 - Literally 'water rises,' referring to a sudden flood or surge of water.
    • 大儿始迎妇 - 'The eldest son is just getting married.' The phrase '始迎妇' indicates the beginning of a wedding ceremony.
    • 衣挂船头，遂堕水，即没 - 'His clothes got caught on the boat's prow, and he fell into the water, drowning immediately.' This describes a tragic accident during the crossing.
    • 徐羡之作扬州 - 'Xu Xianzhi served as the governor of Yangzhou.' This indicates a specific historical figure and his administrative role.
    • 登敕两岸 - 'He ordered the search along both banks.' This suggests a formal command to search for something.
    • 厚赏渔人及昆仑 - 'He offered generous rewards to fishermen and K'un-lun.' K'un-lun likely refers to people from the region of K'un-lun, possibly foreigners or skilled laborers.
    • 彷佛如梦闻婿告之曰 - 'As if in a dream, she heard her husband say...' This conveys a surreal, dreamlike experience.
    • 顿觉臭秽 - 'Suddenly, it became foul-smelling.' This describes a sudden change in perception.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 蔡廓 - A historical figure, likely a high-ranking official or scholar.
    • 徐羡之 - A historical figure, known as Xu Xianzhi, who served as a governor during the Liu Song dynasty.
    • 昆仑 - Likely refers to people from the K'un-lun region, possibly foreigners or skilled laborers.
    • 钟道 - A historical figure, likely a minor official or commoner.
    • 白鹤墟 - A place name, possibly a village or area associated with white cranes.
    • 鸡舌香 - A type of perfume or incense, often used in ancient China for its fragrance.

    Proper Nouns

    • 蔡廓 - A historical figure, likely a high-ranking official or scholar.
    • 徐羡之 - A historical figure, known as Xu Xianzhi, who served as a governor during the Liu Song dynasty.
    • 昆仑 - Likely refers to people from the K'un-lun region, possibly foreigners or skilled laborers.
    • 钟道 - A historical figure, likely a minor official or commoner.
    • 白鹤墟 - A place name, possibly a village or area associated with white cranes.
    • 鸡舌香 - A type of perfume or incense, often used in ancient China for its fragrance.

    During P Cai's tenure as the governor of Yuzhang Prefecture, a sudden flood occurred. His eldest son was just getting married, and they were at a place called "zhuzhi" (a small island or shore). The son wanted to cross to his wife's boat, but his clothes got caught on the boat's prow, and he fell into the water, drowning immediately.

    When Xu Xianzhi served as the governor of Yangzhou, he ordered a search along both banks, offering generous rewards to fishermen and K'un-lun (possibly foreigners or skilled laborers) to help find the drowned son. Despite extensive efforts, they did not find him until the second watch of the night.

    In her grief, the wife heard, as if in a dream, her husband say, "I am now under your boat." She told this to her maid, who informed Xu Xianzhi. He then ordered the divers to search again, and they found the son sitting under the boat. When he was pulled out of the water, his appearance was as it had been in life.

    In the Yongxing County of the Song dynasty, a minor official named Zhong Dao recovered from a serious illness and experienced heightened desires. He had previously been attracted to a woman from Baihe Village. Even after recovering, he still thought of her. One day, he suddenly saw the woman coming towards him, dressed and ready. They spent the night together, and she visited him several times afterward.

    Zhong Dao said, "I really want some chicken tongue incense." The woman replied, "What's difficult about that?" She then filled her hands with the incense and gave it to Zhong Dao. Zhong Dao invited her to share the incense, but she said, "My breath is naturally fragrant, so I don't need it." As she left the house, a dog suddenly appeared and attacked her, killing her. It turned out to be an old otter. The incense in its mouth was actually otter droppings, and it suddenly became foul-smelling. [108]

    近世有人，得一小给使，频求还家，未遂。后日久，此吏在南窗下眠，此人见门中有一妇人，年五六十，肥大，行步艰难。吏眠失覆，妇人至床边取被以覆之，回复出门去。吏转侧衣落，妇人复如初。此人心怪。明问吏以何事求归。吏云：“母病。”次问状貌及年，皆如所见，唯云形瘦不同。又问：“母何患？”答云：“病肿。”而即与吏假，使出，便得家信，云母丧。追计所见之肥，乃是其肿状也。
    　　焦湖庙祝有柏枕，三十余年，枕后一小坼孔。县民汤林行贾，经庙祈福，祝曰：“君婚姻未？可就枕坼边。”令林入坼内，见朱门、琼宫、瑶台，胜于世见。赵太尉为林婚，育子六人，四男二女，选林秘书郎，俄迁黄门郎。林在枕中，永无思归之怀，遂遭违忤之事。祝令林出外间，遂见向枕，谓枕内历年载，而实俄忽之间矣。 [109]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [109]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 近世有人 - In recent times, there was a person.
    • 得一小给使 - Who obtained a young male servant.
    • 频求还家 - Who repeatedly requested to return home.
    • 未遂 - But did not succeed.
    • 后日久 - After a long time.
    • 此吏在南窗下眠 - The servant was sleeping under the southern window.
    • 此人见门中有一妇人 - The person saw a woman in the doorway.
    • 年五六十 - Who was about fifty or sixty years old.
    • 肥大 - Fat and large.
    • 行步艰难 - Walking with difficulty.
    • 此人心怪 - The person found this strange.
    • 明问吏以何事求归 - The next day, he asked the servant why he wanted to return home.
    • 母病 - Because his mother was ill.
    • 次问状貌及年 - Then he asked about her appearance and age.
    • 皆如所见 - Which matched what he had seen.
    • 唯云形瘦不同 - But said she was thin, not fat.
    • 又问母何患 - He then asked what was wrong with his mother.
    • 病肿 - She was swollen.
    • 而即与吏假 - He then gave the servant leave.
    • 便得家信 - And he received a message from home.
    • 云母丧 - Saying that his mother had died.
    • 追计所见之肥 - Reflecting on the fatness he had seen.
    • 乃是其肿状也 - It was actually the appearance of her swelling.
    • 焦湖庙祝有柏枕 - There was a temple official at Jiaohu with a pine wood pillow.
    • 三十余年 - For over thirty years.
    • 枕后一小坼孔 - There was a small crack at the back of the pillow.
    • 县民汤林行贾 - A county resident named Tang Lin, who traveled as a merchant.
    • 经庙祈福 - Passed by the temple to pray for good fortune.
    • 祝曰 - The temple official said.
    • 君婚姻未 - Have you not yet married?
    • 可就枕坼边 - You can go into the crack of the pillow.
    • 令林入坼内 - He instructed Tang Lin to enter the crack.
    • 见朱门、琼宫、瑶台 - He saw red gates, jade palaces, and crystal terraces.
    • 胜于世见 - More beautiful than anything in the world.
    • 赵太尉为林婚 - Zhaotai, the Grand Commander, arranged for Tang Lin's marriage.
    • 育子六人 - They had six children.
    • 四男二女 - Four sons and two daughters.
    • 选林秘书郎 - He was chosen as a secretary.
    • 俄迁黄门郎 - He was soon promoted to an official in the palace.
    • 林在枕中 - Tang Lin stayed in the pillow.
    • 永无思归之怀 - He never felt the desire to return.
    • 遂遭违忤之事 - But he encountered some misfortune.
    • 祝令林出外间 - The temple official instructed Tang Lin to leave the pillow.
    • 遂见向枕 - He then saw the pillow again.
    • 谓枕内历年载 - He thought he had been in the pillow for many years.
    • 而实俄忽之间矣 - But it was actually a very short time.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 近世 - Refers to a relatively recent period in Chinese history.
    • 焦湖庙祝 - Refers to a temple official at Jiaohu, a specific location.
    • 柏枕 - A pine wood pillow, which is a specific type of pillow.
    • 赵太尉 - Refers to Zhaotai, a high-ranking official in the imperial court.
    • 秘书郎 - A low-ranking official in the imperial secretariat.
    • 黄门郎 - A higher-ranking official in the imperial palace.

    Proper Nouns

    • 焦湖 - A specific location.
    • 赵太尉 - A specific high-ranking official.
    • 汤林 - A specific person.
    • 秘书郎 - A specific official position.
    • 黄门郎 - A specific official position.

    A Person from Recent Times

    In recent times, there was a person who obtained a young male servant. The servant repeatedly requested to return home but did not succeed. After a long time, the servant was sleeping under the southern window. The person saw a woman in the doorway, who was about fifty or sixty years old, fat and large, walking with difficulty. The servant lost his cover while sleeping, and the woman went to the bedside to fetch a blanket to cover him, then returned and left the door. The servant turned over, and his clothes fell off; the woman did it again as before. The person found this strange. The next day, he asked the servant why he wanted to return home. The servant said, "My mother is ill." He then asked about her appearance and age, which matched what he had seen, but the servant said she was thin, not fat. He then asked, "What is wrong with your mother?" The servant replied, "She is swollen." The person then gave the servant leave, and he went out. He soon received a message from home, saying that his mother had died. Reflecting on the fatness he had seen, it was actually the appearance of her swelling.

    There was a temple official at Jiaohu with a pine wood pillow, which had been in use for over thirty years. There was a small crack at the back of the pillow. A county resident named Tang Lin, who traveled as a merchant, passed by the temple to pray for good fortune. The temple official said, "Have you not yet married? You can go into the crack of the pillow." He instructed Tang Lin to enter the crack. Tang Lin saw red gates, jade palaces, and crystal terraces, more beautiful than anything in the world. Zhaotai, the Grand Commander, arranged for Tang Lin's marriage, and they had six children, four sons and two daughters. Tang Lin was chosen as a secretary and was soon promoted to an official in the palace. Tang Lin stayed in the pillow and never felt the desire to return, but he encountered some misfortune. The temple official instructed Tang Lin to leave the pillow, and he then saw the pillow again. He thought he had been in the pillow for many years, but it was actually a very short time. [109]

    宋时余杭县南有上湖，湖中央作塘。有一人乘马看戏，将三四人至岑村，饮酒小醉，暮还。时炎热，因下马入水中，枕石眠。马断辔走归，从人悉追马，至暮不返。眠觉，日已向晡，不见人马，见一妇来，年可十六七，云：“女郎再拜，日既向暮，此间大可畏，君作何计？”问：“女郎姓何？那得忽相闻？”复有一年少，年可十三四，甚了了，乘新车，车后二十人。至，呼上车云：“大人暂欲相见。”因回车而去。道中骆驿把火，寻见城郭邑居，至便入城。进厅事，上有信幡，题云“河泊”。俄见一人，年三十许，颜容如画，侍卫繁多。相对欣然。 [110]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [110]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 枕石眠 - Literally 'resting on a stone to sleep,' indicating a casual and relaxed posture.
    • 把火 - Refers to carrying torches or lanterns, suggesting a night journey.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 宋 - The Song Dynasty (960–1279) was a period of significant cultural and economic development in China.
    • 餘杭縣 - Located in present-day Zhejiang Province, known for its historical significance and natural beauty.
    • 上湖 - A local landmark that would have been familiar to readers of the time.
    • 岑村 - A specific village mentioned, which would have been known to the local population.
    • 河泊 - This term could be interpreted in two ways: as a reference to a river deity or as a government official responsible for rivers and lakes. The context suggests a more supernatural interpretation given the setting and the presence of a woman and a young man.

    Proper Nouns

    • 宋 - The Song Dynasty.
    • 餘杭縣 - A county in present-day Zhejiang Province.
    • 上湖 - A lake in the area.
    • 岑村 - A village mentioned in the text.
    • 河泊 - A term that could refer to a river deity or a river official.

    During the Song Dynasty, there was an upper lake to the south of Yuhang County, with a pond in the middle of the lake. A man rode a horse to watch a play, accompanied by three or four others, and they went to Cen Village for a drink, becoming slightly tipsy before returning in the evening. It was hot, so he dismounted and entered the water, resting on a stone to sleep. The horse broke its reins and ran back, and his followers all chased after it, not returning until dusk. When he woke up, the sun had already passed noon, and he saw no one or any horses. Instead, he saw a woman come, about sixteen or seventeen years old, who said, "Lady respectfully bows. The day is already late, and this place is very fearsome. What do you plan to do?" He asked, "What is your lady's surname? How did you suddenly appear?" Then, a young man, about thirteen or fourteen years old, appeared, very clear and bright, riding in a new carriage with twenty people following. He called out for the man to get on the carriage, saying, "The master wishes to meet you briefly." They then turned the carriage and left. Along the way, they carried torches, and soon they found a city with houses and buildings. Upon arriving, they entered the city and went into a hall. Above the hall was a banner inscribed with "River God." Soon, a man in his thirties appeared, with a face as beautiful as a painting, surrounded by many attendants. They greeted each other warmly. [110]

    敕行酒炙。云：“仆有小女，颇聪明，欲以给君箕帚。”此人知神，敬畏不敢拒逆。便敕备办，令就郎中婚。承白已办。送丝布单衣及纱袷、绢裙、纱衫、裈、履、屐，皆精好。又给十小吏，青衣数十人。妇年可十八九，姿宫婉媚，便成礼。三日后，大会客。拜阁，四日，云：“礼既有限，当发遣去。”妇以金瓯、麝香囊与婿别，涕泣而分。又与钱十万，药方三卷，云：“可以施功布德”复云：“十年当相迎。”此人归家，遂不肯别婚，辞亲出家作道人。所得三卷方者，一卷脉经，一卷汤方，一卷丸方。周行救疗，皆致神验。后母老迈，兄丧，因还婚宦。
    　　宋有一国，与罗剎相近。罗剎数入境，食人无度。王与罗剎约言：自今以后，国中人家，各专一日，当分送往，勿复枉杀。有奉佛家，唯有一子，始年十岁，次当充行。舍别之际，父母哀号，便至心念佛。以佛威神力，大鬼不得近。明日，见子尚在，欢喜同归。于兹遂绝。国人嘉庆慕焉。 [111]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [111]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 敕行酒炙 - A formal command to serve wine and roasted meat, indicating a ceremonial or official setting.
    • 欲以给君箕帚 - A phrase indicating the intention to marry the daughter, using '箕帚' (broom and dustpan) as a metaphor for a wife.
    • 此人知神，敬畏不敢拒逆 - A statement that the person recognizes the divine or supernatural nature of the situation and feels reverence, thus unable to refuse.
    • 便敕备办，令就郎中婚 - A formal command to prepare for the marriage, with the daughter marrying into the imperial court.
    • 承白已办 - A response indicating that preparations have been completed.
    • 送丝布单衣及纱袷、绢裙、纱衫、裈、履、屐，皆精好 - A detailed description of the gifts sent, emphasizing their quality and variety.
    • 又给十小吏，青衣数十人 - Additional gifts of servants and attendants, indicating the wealth and status of the family.
    • 妇年可十八九，姿宫婉媚 - A description of the wife's age and appearance, emphasizing her beauty and grace.
    • 便成礼 - The formal conclusion of the marriage ceremony.
    • 三日后，大会客 - A statement indicating a grand gathering of guests three days after the wedding.
    • 拜阁，四日，云：“礼既有限，当发遣去。” - A formal farewell after four days, indicating the limited duration of the stay.
    • 妇以金瓯、麝香囊与婿别，涕泣而分 - A poignant scene of the wife giving gifts and crying as they part.
    • 又与钱十万，药方三卷，云：“可以施功布德” - Additional gifts of money and medical texts, with the wife explaining their purpose.
    • 复云：“十年当相迎。” - A promise to welcome the husband back in ten years.
    • 此人归家，遂不肯别婚，辞亲出家作道人 - The husband's decision to return home, refuse other marriages, and become a monk.
    • 所得三卷方者，一卷脉经，一卷汤方，一卷丸方 - A detailed description of the medical texts received.
    • 周行救疗，皆致神验 - A statement indicating the effectiveness of the medical texts in healing.
    • 后母老迈，兄丧，因还婚宦 - The husband's eventual return to marriage and official duties due to the death of his mother and brother.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 敕 - A formal imperial command, indicating the involvement of the imperial court.
    • 郎中 - A high-ranking official in the imperial court, indicating the wife's elevated social status.
    • 金瓯 - A precious vessel, symbolizing the wife's wealth and affection.
    • 麝香囊 - A sachet of musk, symbolizing the wife's wealth and affection.
    • 药方三卷 - Three volumes of medical texts, reflecting the wife's knowledge and the husband's later role as a healer.
    • 十年当相迎 - A promise of reunion, reflecting the theme of separation and eventual reunion.
    • 辞亲出家作道人 - The husband's decision to become a monk, reflecting the cultural practice of renouncing worldly life for spiritual pursuits.
    • 后母老迈，兄丧 - The death of the mother and brother, reflecting the cultural importance of filial piety and family obligations.

    Proper Nouns

    • 郎中 - A high-ranking official in the imperial court.
    • 金瓯 - A precious vessel, symbolizing the wife's wealth and affection.
    • 麝香囊 - A sachet of musk, symbolizing the wife's wealth and affection.
    • 药方三卷 - Three volumes of medical texts, reflecting the wife's knowledge and the husband's later role as a healer.

    Translation:

    An imperial command was issued to serve wine and roasted meat. It was said, "I have a young daughter, who is quite intelligent, and I wish to give her to you as your wife." This person, recognizing the divine nature of the situation, felt reverence and could not refuse. The command was then issued to prepare for the wedding, and the daughter was to marry into the imperial court. It was reported that the preparations were complete. She was given fine silk and cotton garments, including a single robe, a gauze jacket, a silk skirt, a gauze shirt, undergarments, shoes, and sandals. Additionally, ten minor officials and dozens of attendants were provided. The wife, around eighteen or nineteen years old, was graceful and beautiful, and the wedding ceremony was completed. Three days later, a grand gathering of guests was held. On the fourth day, it was said, "The ceremony has its limits, and it is time for you to depart." The wife gave her husband a golden bowl and a sachet of musk as parting gifts, and they wept as they parted. She also gave him ten thousand coins and three volumes of medical texts, saying, "These can be used to perform good deeds." She added, "I will welcome you back in ten years." Upon returning home, the husband refused to consider other marriages, bid farewell to his family, and became a monk. The three volumes of medical texts included one on pulse diagnosis, one on decoctions, and one on pills. He traveled widely, healing people with remarkable effectiveness. Later, when his mother grew old and his brother died, he returned to marriage and official duties.

    In a certain country of the Song dynasty, it was near a land of demons called the Rakshasas. The Rakshasas frequently invaded, consuming people without limit. The king made an agreement with the Rakshasas: from then on, each household in the country would be responsible for one day, and the people would be sent to them in turn, so that they would not be killed indiscriminately. There was a family that followed the Buddha, and they had only one son, who was ten years old and was next in line to be sent. When the time came to part, the parents wailed in sorrow, and the son sincerely recited the Buddha's name. By the power of the Buddha's divine will, the great demon could not approach. The next day, the parents found their son still alive, and they rejoiced and returned together. From that point on, the Rakshasas ceased to invade. The people of the country celebrated and admired this event. [111]

    安侯世高者，安息国王子。与大长者子共出家，学道舍卫城中。值王不称，大长者子辄恚，世高恒呵戒之。周旋二十八年，云当至广州。值乱，有一人逢高，唾手拔刀曰：“真得汝矣！”高大笑曰：“我夙命负対，故远来相偿。”遂杀之。有一少年云：“此远国异人而能作吾国言，受害无难色，将是神人乎？”众皆骇笑。世高神识还生安息国，复为王子，名高。安侯年二十，复辞王学道。十数年，语同学云：“当诣会稽毕对。”过庐山，访知识，遂过广州。见年少尚在，径投其家，与说昔事，大欣喜，便随至会稽。过嵇山庙，呼神共语。庙神蟒形，身长数丈，泪出。世高向之语，蟒便去，世高亦还船。有一少年上船，长跪前受咒愿，因遂不见。广州客曰：“向少年即庙神，得离恶形矣。”云庙神即是宿长者子。后庙祝闻有臭气，见大蟒死，庙从此神歇。前至会稽，入市门，值有相打者，误中世高头，即卒。广州客遂事佛精进。 [112]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [112]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 安侯世高者 - Introduces the subject of the narrative, 'Anhou Shigao'.
    • 与大长者子共出家 - He took ordination together with the son of a wealthy man.
    • 学道舍卫城中 - He studied the Dharma in the city of Sravasti.
    • 值王不称 - When the king was not praised or virtuous.
    • 辄恚 - He would become angry immediately or always.
    • 世高恒呵戒之 - Shigao always admonished him.
    • 周旋二十八年 - He traveled for twenty-eight years.
    • 云当至广州 - He said he would go to Guangzhou.
    • 值乱 - When there was chaos.
    • 唾手拔刀曰 - Spitting, he drew his sword and said.
    • 真得汝矣 - I have truly found you.
    • 夙命负対 - Due to a past-life debt.
    • 遂杀之 - He then killed him.
    • 众皆骇笑 - Everyone was shocked and laughed.
    • 世高神识还生安息国 - Shigao's spirit was reborn in the Parthian Kingdom.
    • 复为王子，名高 - He was reborn as a prince, named Gao.
    • 安侯年二十，复辞王学道 - At twenty, Anhou Shigao again left the kingdom to study the Dharma.
    • 十数年 - For several years.
    • 语同学云 - He told his fellow practitioners.
    • 当诣会稽毕对 - He said he would go to Kuaiji to settle the debt.
    • 过庐山 - He passed through Mount Lu.
    • 访知识 - He visited his friends or teachers.
    • 遂过广州 - He then went to Guangzhou.
    • 见年少尚在 - He saw the young man was still there.
    • 径投其家 - He directly went to his house.
    • 与说昔事 - He told him about the past events.
    • 大欣喜 - He was greatly pleased.
    • 便随至会稽 - He then followed him to Kuaiji.
    • 过嵇山庙 - He passed through the temple of Mount Jie.
    • 呼神共语 - He called the god and spoke with him.
    • 庙神蟒形 - The temple god had the form of a snake.
    • 身长数丈 - His body was several zhang long.
    • 泪出 - Tears flowed.
    • 世高向之语 - Shigao spoke to him.
    • 蟒便去 - The snake then left.
    • 世高亦还船 - Shigao also returned to his boat.
    • 有一少年上船 - A young man came onto the boat.
    • 长跪前受咒愿 - He knelt down and received the blessings.
    • 因遂不见 - He then disappeared.
    • 广州客曰 - The Guangzhou guest said.
    • 向少年即庙神 - The young man was the temple god.
    • 得离恶形矣 - He has left his evil form.
    • 云庙神即是宿长者子 - He said the temple god was the son of the former wealthy man.
    • 后庙祝闻有臭气 - Later, the temple priest smelled a foul odor.
    • 见大蟒死 - He saw the large snake dead.
    • 庙从此神歇 - The temple's spirit ceased.
    • 前至会稽 - He went to Kuaiji.
    • 入市门 - He entered the city gate.
    • 值有相打者 - He encountered someone fighting.
    • 误中世高头 - He accidentally hit Shigao's head.
    • 即卒 - He died immediately.
    • 广州客遂事佛精进 - The Guangzhou guest then diligently practiced Buddhism.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 安息国 - The Parthian Empire, an ancient kingdom in what is now Iran.
    • 舍卫城 - Sravasti, an ancient city in India that was an important center for Buddhist teachings.
    • 大长者子 - The son of a wealthy or influential person, often used in Buddhist texts to refer to a lay follower.
    • 学道 - To study and practice the Dharma, the teachings of the Buddha.
    • 值王不称 - Refers to a king who is not virtuous or praised, indicating a period of moral decline.
    • 辄恚 - Indicates a reaction of anger or resentment, often used to describe the negative emotions of a person.
    • 世高恒呵戒之 - Shigao's role in admonishing others.

    Anhou Shigao, a prince of the Parthian Kingdom, took ordination together with the son of a wealthy man and studied the Dharma in the city of Sravasti. When the king was not virtuous, the son of the wealthy man became angry, and Shigao always admonished him. After traveling for twenty-eight years, he said he would go to Guangzhou. When there was chaos, someone met Shigao, spat, drew his sword, and said, "I have truly found you!" Shigao laughed and said, "I have a past-life debt, so I have come far to settle it." He then killed him. A young man said, "This foreigner from afar can speak our language, and he shows no fear when harmed—could he be a divine being?" Everyone was shocked and laughed. Shigao's spirit was reborn in the Parthian Kingdom, and he was reborn as a prince, named Gao. At twenty, Anhou Shigao again left the kingdom to study the Dharma. After several years, he told his fellow practitioners, "I will go to Kuaiji to settle the debt." He passed through Mount Lu, visited his friends or teachers, and then went to Guangzhou. He saw the young man was still there, directly went to his house, told him about the past events, and the young man was greatly pleased. He then followed him to Kuaiji. He passed through the temple of Mount Jie, called the god, and spoke with him. The temple god had the form of a snake, was several zhang long, and tears flowed. Shigao spoke to him, and the snake then left. Shigao also returned to his boat. A young man came onto the boat, knelt down, and received the blessings, then disappeared. The Guangzhou guest said, "The young man was the temple god, and he has left his evil form." He said the temple god was the son of the former wealthy man. Later, the temple priest smelled a foul odor, saw the large snake dead, and the temple's spirit ceased. He went to Kuaiji, entered the city gate, encountered someone fighting, accidentally hit Shigao's head, and he died immediately. The Guangzhou guest then diligently practiced Buddhism. [112]

    有新死鬼，形疲瘦顿。忽见生时友人，死及二十年，肥健，相问讯。曰：“卿那尔？”曰：“吾饥饿殆不自任，卿知诸方便，故当以法见教。”友鬼云：“此甚易耳。但为人作怪，人必大怖，当与卿食。”新鬼往入大墟东头，有一家奉佛精进，屋西厢有磨，鬼就捱此磨，如人推法。此家主语子弟曰：“佛怜我家贫，令鬼推磨。’乃辇麦与之。至夕，磨数斛，疲顿乃去。遂骂友鬼：“卿那诳我？”又曰：“但复去，自当得也。”复从墟西头入一家，家奉道，门傍有碓，此鬼便上碓如人舂状。 [113]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [113]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 有新死鬼，形疲瘦顿 - Refers to a newly deceased spirit or ghost that is physically exhausted and emaciated.
    • 卿那尔 - A colloquial expression asking 'What happened to you?' or 'How are you?'
    • 吾饥饿殆不自任 - Means 'I am starving and can hardly bear it.'
    • 卿知诸方便，故当以法见教 - Means 'You know various ways or methods, so you should teach me a method.'
    • 此甚易耳 - Means 'This is very easy.'
    • 但为人作怪 - Means 'Just act strange or bizarre.'
    • 人必大怖 - Means 'People will surely be terrified.'
    • 当与卿食 - Means 'And then you will get food.'
    • 新鬼往入大墟东头 - Means 'The new ghost went into the eastern part of the large marketplace.'
    • 有一家奉佛精进 - Means 'There was a family that devoutly practiced Buddhism.'
    • 屋西厢有磨 - Means 'In the west wing of their house, there was a mill.'
    • 鬼就捱此磨，如人推法 - Means 'The ghost began to push this mill, like a person pushing it.'
    • 此家主语子弟曰 - Means 'The head of the family told his children.'
    • 佛怜我家贫，令鬼推磨 - Means 'Buddha, out of pity for our poverty, has sent a ghost to push the mill for us.'
    • 乃辇麦与之 - Means 'They then carted grain to give to the ghost.'
    • 至夕，磨数斛 - Means 'By evening, the ghost had ground several hu of grain.'
    • 疲顿乃去 - Means 'Then, exhausted, it left.'
    • 遂骂友鬼 - Means 'He then cursed the friend ghost.'
    • 卿那诳我 - Means 'You deceived me!'
    • 但复去，自当得也 - Means 'Just go again, and you will get it.'
    • 复从墟西头入一家 - Means 'The ghost then entered another family from the western part of the marketplace.'
    • 家奉道 - Means 'This family practiced Taoism.'
    • 门傍有碓 - Means 'By the door, there was a mortar.'
    • 此鬼便上碓如人舂状 - Means 'The ghost climbed onto the mortar and began to pound like a person.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 死鬼 - Refers to a spirit or ghost, often with negative connotations in Chinese folklore.
    • 奉佛精进 - Refers to a family that practices Buddhism diligently.
    • 奉道 - Refers to a family that practices Taoism.
    • 磨 - A traditional grinding tool used for processing grains.
    • 碓 - A traditional pounding tool used for processing grains or other materials.
    • 斛 - An ancient Chinese unit of volume, equivalent to about 10 liters.

    There was a newly deceased spirit, physically exhausted and emaciated. Suddenly, it saw a friend who had died twenty years ago, who was fat and healthy. They exchanged greetings. The friend asked, "What happened to you?" The ghost replied, "I am starving and can hardly bear it. You know various ways, so you should teach me a method." The friend ghost said, "This is very easy. Just act strange or bizarre, and people will surely be terrified. Then you will get food." The new ghost went into the eastern part of the large marketplace and found a family that devoutly practiced Buddhism. In the west wing of their house, there was a mill. The ghost began to push this mill, like a person pushing it. The head of the family told his children, "Buddha, out of pity for our poverty, has sent a ghost to push the mill for us." They then carted grain to give to the ghost. By evening, the ghost had ground several hu of grain and, then exhausted, left. He then cursed the friend ghost, saying, "You deceived me!" The friend ghost said, "Just go again, and you will get it." The ghost then entered another family from the western part of the marketplace. This family practiced Taoism. By the door, there was a mortar. The ghost climbed onto the mortar and began to pound like a person. [113]

    此人言：“昨日鬼助某甲，今复来助吾，可辇谷与之。”又给婢簸筛，至夕，力疲甚，不与鬼食。鬼暮归，大怒曰：“吾自与卿为婚姻，非他比，如何见欺？二日助人，不得一瓯饮食。”友鬼曰：“卿自不偶耳！此二家奉佛事道，情自难动。今去可觅百姓家作怪，则无不得。”鬼复去，得一家，门首有竹竿，从门入。见有一群女子，窗前共食。至庭中，有一白狗，便抱令空中行，其家见之大惊，言自来未有此怪。占云：“有客索食，可杀狗并甘果酒饭，于庭中祀之，可得无他。”其家如师言，鬼果大得食。此后恒作怪，友鬼之教也。 [114]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [114]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 此人言 - Introduction to a reported statement.
    • 昨日鬼助某甲 - A ghost helped someone named A yesterday.
    • 今复来助吾 - The ghost has come again to help me.
    • 可辇谷与之 - Can carry grain to give it.
    • 又给婢簸筛 - Also gave the maid to winnow and sift.
    • 至夕，力疲甚 - By evening, extremely tired.
    • 不与鬼食 - Did not give the ghost food.
    • 鬼暮归 - The ghost returned in the evening.
    • 大怒曰 - Greatly angry, said.
    • 吾自与卿为婚姻 - I have been married to you.
    • 非他比 - Not comparable to others.
    • 如何见欺 - How could you deceive me?
    • 二日助人 - Helped people for two days.
    • 不得一瓯饮食 - Did not receive even a bowl of food.
    • 友鬼曰 - The friendly ghost said.
    • 卿自不偶耳 - You are just unlucky.
    • 此二家奉佛事道 - These two families worship Buddha and practice Daoism.
    • 情自难动 - Their emotions are naturally difficult to move.
    • 今去可觅百姓家作怪 - Now go and find ordinary families to cause trouble.
    • 得一家 - Found one family.
    • 门首有竹竿 - There was a bamboo pole at the door.
    • 从门入 - Entered through the door.
    • 见有一群女子 - Saw a group of women.
    • 窗前共食 - Eating together in front of the window.
    • 至庭中 - To the courtyard.
    • 有一白狗 - There was a white dog.
    • 便抱令空中行 - Picked up and flew in the air.
    • 其家见之大惊 - The family saw it and was greatly surprised.
    • 言自来未有此怪 - Said they had never seen such a strange occurrence.
    • 占云 - The diviner said.
    • 有客索食 - There is a guest asking for food.
    • 可杀狗并甘果酒饭 - Can kill the dog and prepare sweet fruits, wine, and rice.
    • 于庭中祀之 - Worship it in the courtyard.
    • 可得无他 - Can avoid further trouble.
    • 其家如师言 - The family followed the diviner's advice.
    • 鬼果大得食 - The ghost indeed ate well.
    • 此后恒作怪 - Afterward, it always caused trouble.
    • 友鬼之教也 - It was due to the friendly ghost's advice.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 鬼 - Refers to supernatural beings or spirits.
    • 奉佛事道 - Refers to the practice of Buddhism and Daoism, indicating religious beliefs.
    • 占云 - Refers to divination, a common practice in ancient China.
    • 祀之 - Refers to rituals or offerings, indicating religious practices.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • This sentence introduces a reported statement and must be read as a single unit.
    • This sequence describes actions and must be read as a continuous narrative.
    • This sentence describes the ghost's reaction and must be read as a single unit.
    • This sentence introduces advice and must be read as a single unit.
    • This sequence describes the ghost's actions and must be read as a continuous narrative.
